3_13_32: And on the seventh day the priest shall examine the infection; and indeed if the scale has not spread, and there is no yellow hair in it, and the scale does not appear deeper than the skin,
3_13_33: He shall shave himself, but the scaly area he shall not shave, and the priest shall quarantine the one who has the scale another seven days.
3_13_34: On the seventh day the priest shall examine the scaly outbreak, and if the scale has not spread in the skin and it appears to be no deeper than the skin, then the priest shall pronounce him clean, and he shall wash his clothes and be clean.
3_13_35: But if the scale should at all spread farther over the skin after his cleansing,
3_13_36: Then the priest shall examine him, and if the scaly outbreak has spread in the skin, the priest need not seek for yellow hair; he is unclean.
3_13_37: But if the scale appears to be unchanged and black hair has grown in it, the scale has healed, he is clean, and the priest shall pronounce him clean.
3_13_38: If a man or a woman has bright spots on the skin of the body, specifically white bright spots,
3_13_39: Then the priest shall examine them, and if the spots on the skin of the body are dull white, it is a harmless rash that has broken out on the skin; he is clean.
3_13_40: As for the man whose hair has fallen from his head, he is bald, but he is clean.
3_13_41: He whose hair has fallen from his hairline on his forehead, he is bald on the forehead, but he is clean.
3_13_42: And if there is on the bald head or bald forehead a reddish-white infection, it is a serious skin disease breaking out on his bald head or his bald forehead.
3_13_43: Then the priest shall examine him, and if the diseased swelling of the sore is reddish-white on his bald head or on his bald forehead, like the appearance of leprosy on the skin of the body,
3_13_44: He is a leprous man, he is unclean, and the priest must surely pronounce him unclean because the infection is on his head.
3_13_45: Now the leper who has the disease, his clothes shall be torn and his hair hanging loose, and he shall cover his mouth and cry out, 'Unclean! Unclean!'
3_13_46: He shall remain unclean all the days he has the infection; he is unclean, and he shall live alone with his dwelling outside the camp.
3_13_47: Also, if a fabric has a contaminating disease in it, whether it is a woolen garment or a linen garment,
3_13_48: whether it is in the warp or woof of linen or wool, whether in leather or in any article made of leather,
3_13_49: If the mark is greenish or reddish in the garment or in the leather, whether in the warp or in the woof, or in any article made of leather, it is a case of defiling disease and shall be shown to the priest.
3_13_50: The priest shall examine the contamination and quarantine that which has the contamination seven days.
3_13_51: And he shall examine the contamination on the seventh day, and if the plague has spread in the garment, either in the warp or in the woof, in the leather or in anything made of leather, the plague is an active leprous disease and it is unclean.
3_13_52: He shall therefore burn that garment in which is the contamination, whether warp or woof, in wool or in linen, or anything of leather, for it is a harmful leprosy; the garment shall be burned in the fire.
3_13_53: But if the priest examines it and indeed the contamination has not spread in the garment, either in the warp or in the woof, or in any article made of leather,
3_13_54: Then the priest shall command that they wash the thing in which the contamination occurs, and he shall quarantine it another seven days.
3_13_55: Then the priest shall examine the contaminated article after it has been washed, and if the contamination has not changed its appearance, though it has not spread, it is unclean, and you shall burn it in the fire; it is a corrosive mildew, whether the damage is on the outside or inside.
3_13_56: If the priest examines it and indeed the contaminated area has faded after washing it, then he shall tear it out of the garment, whether out of the warp or out of the woof, or out of the leather.
3_13_57: But if it appears again in the garment, either in the warp or in the woof, or in anything made of leather, it is a spreading outbreak; you shall burn with fire that in which is the plague.
3_13_58: And if you wash the garment, either warp or woof, or whatever is made of leather, and the mark has disappeared from it, then it shall be washed a second time and shall be clean.
3_13_59: This is the law for the mark of a defiling disease in a garment of wool or linen, either in the warp or woof, or in anything made of leather, to determine whether it is clean or unclean.
3_14_21: But if he is poor and his means are insufficient, then he shall take one male lamb as a guilt offering to be waved, to make atonement for him, one-tenth of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering, and a log of oil.
3_14_22: and two turtledoves or two young pigeons, whatever he can afford: one shall be a sin offering and the other a burnt offering.
3_14_23: He shall bring them to the priest on the eighth day for his cleansing, to the entrance of the tabernacle of meeting, before the LORD.
3_14_24: And the priest shall take the lamb of the guilt offering and the log of olive oil, and the priest shall wave them as a wave offering before the LORD.
3_14_25: Then he shall slaughter the lamb of the guilt offering, and the priest shall take some of the blood of the guilt offering and apply it to the lobe of the right ear of the one to be cleansed, on the thumb of his right hand, and on the big toe of his right foot.
3_14_26: And the priest shall pour some of the olive oil into the palm of his own left hand.
3_14_27: Then the priest shall sprinkle with his right finger some of the oil that is in his left palm seven times before the LORD.
3_14_28: The priest shall put some of the oil that is in his palm on the lobe of the right ear of him who is to be cleansed, on the thumb of his right hand, and on the big toe of his right foot, on the same place as the blood of the guilt offering.
3_14_29: The rest of the oil that is in the priest's palm he shall put on the head of him who is to be cleansed, to make atonement for him before the LORD.
3_14_30: Then he shall offer one of the turtledoves or young pigeons, whatever he can afford—
3_14_31: Such as he is able to afford, the one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering, together with the grain offering, and so the priest shall make atonement before the LORD on behalf of him who is to be cleansed.
3_14_32: This is the law for one who had a serious skin disease, who cannot afford the offerings for his cleansing.
3_14_33: And the LORD further spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying:
3_14_34: When you have come into the land of Canaan, which I give you as a possession, and I put a case of defiling disease in a house in the land of your possession,
3_14_35: The owner of the house shall come and tell the priest, saying, 'It appears to me that there is something like a contamination in my house.'
3_14_36: The priest shall command that they empty the house before the priest goes into it to examine the contamination, so that everything in the house will not be declared unclean, and afterward the priest shall go in to examine the house.
3_14_37: And he shall examine the contamination, and indeed if the contamination is on the walls of the house with greenish or reddish depressions which appear to be deeper than the surface.
3_14_38: Then the priest shall go out of the house to the doorway and quarantine the house for seven days.
3_14_39: And the priest shall return on the seventh day and look; and indeed if the contamination has spread on the walls of the house,
3_14_40: then the priest shall command that they take out the stones in which is the contamination, and they shall throw them into an unclean place outside the city.
3_14_41: And he shall have the inside of the house scraped completely all around, and the plaster that they scrape off they shall dump in an unclean place outside the city.
3_14_42: Then they shall take other stones and replace those stones, and he shall take other mortar and replaster the house.
3_14_43: Now if the contamination comes back and breaks out in the house, after he has taken away the stones, after he has scraped the house, and after it is plastered,
3_14_44: Then the priest shall come and examine it, and if the contamination has indeed spread in the house, it is a persistent defiling disease in the house; it is unclean.
3_14_45: And he shall tear down the house, its stones, its timber, and all the plaster of the house, and he shall carry them outside the city to an unclean place.
3_14_46: Moreover whoever enters the house during the time it is shut up shall be unclean until evening.
3_14_47: And whoever sleeps in the house shall wash his clothes, and whoever eats in the house shall wash his clothes.
3_14_48: But if the priest comes in and examines it, and indeed the contamination has not spread in the house after the house was replastered, then the priest shall pronounce the house clean because the contamination is healed.
3_14_49: And he shall take, to purify the house, two birds, cedar wood, scarlet yarn, and hyssop.
3_14_50: Then he shall slaughter one of the birds in an earthenware vessel over fresh running water.
3_14_51: And he shall take the cedar wood, the hyssop, the scarlet yarn, and the living bird, and dip them in the blood of the slain bird and in the fresh water, and sprinkle the house seven times.
3_14_52: And he shall cleanse the house with the blood of the bird and the fresh water and the living bird, with the cedar wood, the hyssop, and the scarlet yarn.
3_14_53: Then he shall release the living bird outside the city into the open field, and make atonement for the house, and it shall be clean.
3_14_54: This is the law for any case of leprous disease and scale,
3_14_55: for the leprous disease of a garment and of a house,
3_14_56: for a swelling and a scab and a bright spot,
3_14_57: to teach when something is unclean and when it is clean. This is the law of leprosy.
3_15_1: The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying,
3_15_2: Speak to the children of Israel, and say to them: When any man has a bodily discharge from his body, his discharge is unclean.
3_15_3: And this shall be his uncleanness in regard to his discharge—whether his body runs with his discharge, or his body is blocked up by his discharge, it is his uncleanness.
3_15_4: Every bed on which he who has the discharge lies becomes unclean, and everything on which he sits shall be unclean.
3_15_5: Whoever touches his bed shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water, and remain unclean until evening.
3_15_6: He who sits on anything on which the man with the discharge sat shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water, and he will remain unclean until evening.
3_15_7: And he who touches the body of the man with the discharge shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water, and be unclean until evening.
3_15_8: If the man who has the discharge spits on someone who is clean, then he shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water, and be unclean until evening.
3_15_9: Any saddle on which the person with the discharge rides shall be unclean.
3_15_10: Whoever touches anything that was under him shall be unclean until evening, and whoever carries such things shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water and remain unclean until evening.
3_15_11: And whoever the one who has the discharge touches without having rinsed his hands in water shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water, and remain unclean until evening.
3_15_12: The clay vessel that he who has the discharge touches shall be broken, and every wooden vessel shall be rinsed in water.
3_15_13: And when he who has a discharge is cleansed of his discharge, then he shall count off for himself seven days for his cleansing, wash his clothes, and bathe his body in fresh water; then he shall be clean.
3_15_14: On the eighth day he shall take for himself two turtledoves or two young pigeons and come before the LORD to the entrance of the tent of meeting and give them to the priest.
3_15_15: Then the priest shall offer them, one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering, and through this process the priest shall make atonement for him before the LORD because of his discharge.
3_15_16: If any man has an emission of semen, then he shall bathe all his body in water and be unclean until evening.
3_15_17: And any garment and any leather on which there is an emission of semen shall be washed with water and be unclean until evening.
3_15_18: Also when a woman lies with a man and there is an emission of semen, both of them shall bathe in water and be unclean until evening.
3_15_19: If a woman has a discharge, and the discharge from her body is blood, she shall continue in her menstrual impurity seven days, and whoever touches her shall be unclean until evening.
3_15_20: Everything that she lies on during her menstrual impurity shall be unclean; also everything that she sits on shall be unclean.
3_15_21: Anyone who touches her bed shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water, and will remain unclean until evening.
3_15_22: And whoever touches anything that she sat on shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water, and remain unclean until evening.
3_15_23: If anything is on her bed or on anything on which she sits, when he touches it he shall be unclean until evening.
3_15_24: And if any man lies with her at all, so that her menstrual impurity comes upon him, he shall be unclean seven days, and every bed on which he lies shall be unclean.
3_15_25: If a woman has a discharge of blood for many days, not at the time of her menstrual impurity, or if she has a discharge beyond her period, all the days of her unclean discharge shall continue as in the days of her menstrual impurity; she shall be unclean.
3_15_26: Every bed on which she lies all the days of her discharge shall be to her as the bed of her menstrual impurity, and everything on which she sits shall be unclean, as the uncleanness of her impurity.
3_15_27: Whoever touches those things shall be unclean; he shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water, and be unclean until evening.
3_15_28: But if she is cleansed of her discharge, then she shall count off for herself seven days, and after that she will be clean.
3_15_29: And on the eighth day she shall take for herself two turtledoves or two young pigeons and present them to the priest at the entrance of the tabernacle of meeting.
3_15_30: Then the priest shall offer the one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering, and the priest shall make atonement on her behalf before the LORD for the discharge of her uncleanness.
3_15_31: Thus you shall keep the children of Israel separate from their uncleanness, so that they do not die in their uncleanness when they defile My tabernacle that is among them.
3_15_32: This is the law for someone who has a discharge, and for the man who emits semen and is unclean thereby.
3_15_33: And for her who is unwell because of her menstrual impurity, and for anyone who has a discharge, whether male or female, and for the man who lies with a woman who is ceremonially unclean.
3_17_1: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
3_17_2: Speak to Aaron, to his sons, and to all the people of Israel, and say to them, This is what the LORD has commanded, saying:
3_17_3: If anyone from the house of Israel slaughters an ox or lamb or goat in the camp, or slaughters it outside the camp,
3_17_4: and does not bring it to the entrance of the tabernacle of meeting to offer an offering to the LORD before the tabernacle of the LORD, the guilt of bloodshed shall be imputed to that man. He has shed blood, and that man shall be cut off from among his people,
3_17_5: The purpose of this is so that the children of Israel may bring their sacrifices which they offer in the open field to the LORD at the entrance of the tabernacle of meeting, to the priest, and offer them as peace offerings to the LORD.
3_17_6: The priest shall sprinkle the blood on the altar of the LORD at the entrance of the tabernacle of meeting, and burn the fat as a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
3_17_7: They shall no more offer their sacrifices to goat demons, after whom they have played the harlot, and this shall be a permanent statute for them throughout their generations.
3_17_8: Also you shall say to them: Whatever man of the house of Israel, or of the foreigners who sojourn among you, who offers a burnt offering or sacrifice,
3_17_9: and does not bring it to the entrance of the tabernacle of meeting to offer it to the LORD, that man shall be cut off from among his people.
3_17_10: And whatever man of the house of Israel, or of the foreigners who dwell among you, who eats any blood, I will set My face against that person who eats blood and will cut him off from among his people.
3_17_11: For the life of the flesh is in the blood, and I have appointed it to you upon the altar to make atonement for your souls; for it is the blood that makes atonement by reason of the life.
3_17_12: Therefore I said to the children of Israel, no person among you shall eat blood, nor shall any stranger who sojourns among you eat blood.
3_17_13: Whatever man of the children of Israel, or of the strangers who sojourn among you, who hunts and catches any wild animal or bird that may be eaten, he shall pour out its blood and cover it with earth.
3_17_14: For the life of every creature is its blood, its blood sustains its life, therefore I said to the children of Israel, you shall not eat the blood of any flesh, for the life of all flesh is its blood, and whoever eats it shall be cut off.
3_17_15: And every person who eats what died naturally or what was torn by wild beasts, whether he is a native of your own country or a sojourner, he shall wash his clothes and bathe in water, and remain unclean until evening, then he shall be clean.
3_17_16: But if he does not wash them or bathe his body, then he shall bear his iniquity.
3_18_1: Then the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
3_18_2: Speak to the children of Israel and say to them: I am the LORD your God.
3_18_3: You shall not do according to the practices of the land of Egypt where you lived, and you shall not do according to the practices of the land of Canaan where I am bringing you, nor shall you walk in their customs.
3_18_4: You shall observe My judgments and keep My statutes, to walk in them: I am the LORD your God.
3_18_5: You shall therefore keep My statutes and My judgments, which if a person obeys them, he shall live by them: I am the LORD.
3_18_6: None of you shall approach any blood relative to uncover nakedness: I am the LORD.
3_18_7: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father or the nakedness of your mother, for she is your mother; you shall not uncover her nakedness.
3_18_8: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father's wife, for this would violate your father's nakedness.
3_18_9: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your sister, the daughter of your father or the daughter of your mother, whether born at home or elsewhere, their nakedness you shall not uncover.
3_18_10: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your son's daughter or your daughter's daughter, for their nakedness is your own nakedness.
3_18_11: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father's wife's daughter, born to your father—she is your sister—you shall not uncover her nakedness.
3_18_12: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father's sister; she is your father's blood relative.
3_18_13: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your mother's sister, for she is your mother's close relative.
3_18_14: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father's brother by approaching his wife for she is your aunt.
3_18_15: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your daughter-in-law—she is your son's wife—you must not uncover her nakedness.
3_18_16: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your brother's wife, for this would violate your brother.
3_18_17: You shall not uncover the nakedness of a woman and her daughter, nor shall you take her son's daughter or her daughter's daughter to uncover her nakedness, for they are close relatives and it is depravity.
3_18_18: You shall not marry a woman as a rival to her sister, to uncover her nakedness while her sister is still alive.
3_18_19: Also you shall not approach a woman to uncover her nakedness while she is in her menstrual impurity.
3_18_20: Moreover you shall not have sexual intercourse with your neighbor's wife, to defile yourself with her.
3_18_21: You shall not give any of your children to be offered to Molech, nor shall you profane the name of your God: I am the LORD.
3_18_22: You shall not lie with a male as with a woman; it is detestable.
3_18_23: Nor shall you mate with any animal to defile yourself with it, nor shall any woman give herself to an animal to mate with it; it is perversion.
3_18_24: Do not defile yourselves with any of these things, for by all these practices the nations I am driving out before you have become defiled.
3_18_25: For the land is defiled; therefore I am punishing it for its iniquity, and the land vomits out its inhabitants.
3_18_26: You shall therefore keep My statutes and My judgments, and shall not commit any of these detestable abominations, either any of your own nation or any stranger who sojourns among you.
3_18_27: For all these detestable abominations the people of the land who were before you have committed, and thus the land has become defiled.
3_18_28: Do not defile the land lest it vomit you out also, as it vomited out the nations that were before you.
3_18_29: For whoever commits any of these detestable abominations, those persons who commit them shall be cut off from among their people.
3_18_30: Therefore you shall keep My ordinance, so that you do not practice any of these abominable customs which were committed before you, and that you do not defile yourselves by them: I am the LORD your God.
3_21_1: And the LORD said to Moses, "Speak to the priests, the sons of Aaron, and say to them: 'None shall make himself ceremonially unclean for the dead among his people,
3_21_2: except for his immediate relatives who are nearest to him: his mother, his father, his son, his daughter, and his brother;
3_21_3: Also his virgin sister who is near to him in his immediate family, who has had no husband, for her he may defile himself.
3_21_4: He shall not defile himself as a husband among his people and so profane himself.
3_21_5: They shall not make any bald patches on their heads, nor shall they shave off the edges of their beards, nor make any cuts in their flesh.
3_21_6: They shall be holy to their God and not profane the name of their God, for they present the offerings of the LORD made by fire, the food of their God; therefore they shall be holy.
3_21_7: They shall not marry a woman who is a prostitute or defiled by prostitution, nor shall they marry a woman divorced from her husband, for the priest is set apart as holy to his God.
3_21_8: Therefore you shall consecrate him, for he offers the food of your God. He shall be holy to you, for I the LORD, who sanctify you, am holy.
3_21_9: The daughter of any priest, if she defiles herself by playing the harlot, she profanes her father and she shall be burned with fire.
3_21_10: He who is the high priest among his brothers, on whose head the anointing oil was poured and who has been consecrated to wear the garments, shall not let his hair hang loose nor tear his clothes.
3_21_11: Nor shall he approach any dead person or make himself unclean even for his father or his mother.
3_21_12: He must not leave the sanctuary or profane the sanctuary of his God, for the consecration of the anointing oil of his God is upon him: I am the LORD.
3_21_13: And he shall take a woman as his wife in her virginity.
3_21_14: A widow or a divorced woman or a defiled woman or a prostitute—these he shall not marry; but rather he shall take a virgin of his own people as wife.
3_21_15: Nor shall he profane his offspring among his people, for I the LORD sanctify him.
3_21_16: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
3_21_17: Speak to Aaron, saying: No man of your offspring throughout their generations who has any defect may approach to offer the food of his God.
3_21_18: For any man who has a defect shall not approach: a man blind or lame, who has a disfigured face or any deformed limb.
3_21_19: a man who has a broken or injured foot or hand,
3_21_20: or is a hunchback or a dwarf, or a man who has a defect in his eye, or an itching disease or scabs, or has crushed testicles.
3_21_21: No man of the descendants of Aaron the priest who has a defect shall come near to present the offerings made by fire to the LORD, since he has a defect he shall not come near to offer the bread of his God.
3_21_22: He may eat the food of his God, both the most holy and the holy.
3_21_23: Only he shall not go through the veil or approach the altar, because he has a defect, lest he profane My sanctuaries, for I the LORD sanctify them.
3_21_24: So Moses spoke these instructions to Aaron and his sons and to all the children of Israel.
3_23_1: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
3_23_2: Speak to the children of Israel and say to them: The appointed feasts of the LORD which you shall proclaim to be holy convocations, these are My feasts.
3_23_3: Six days shall work be done, but the seventh day is a Sabbath of complete rest, a holy convocation. You shall do no work on it; it is the Sabbath of the LORD in all your dwellings.
3_23_4: These are the appointed feasts of the LORD, sacred assemblies which you shall proclaim at their appointed times.
3_23_5: On the fourteenth day of the first month at twilight begins the LORD's Passover.
3_23_6: And on the fifteenth day of the same month is the Festival of Unleavened Bread to the LORD; for seven days you must eat unleavened bread.
3_23_7: On the first day you shall have a holy convocation and you shall do no ordinary work on it.
3_23_8: But you shall present an offering made by fire to the LORD for seven days, and the seventh day shall be a sacred assembly; you shall do no ordinary work on it.
3_23_9: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
3_23_10: Speak to the children of Israel and say to them: When you enter the land which I give to you and reap its harvest, then you shall bring a sheaf of the firstfruits of your harvest to the priest.
3_23_11: He shall wave the sheaf before the LORD so that you may be accepted; on the day after the Sabbath the priest shall wave it.
3_23_12: And you shall offer on that same day when you wave the sheaf a male lamb one year old without blemish as a burnt offering to the LORD.
3_23_13: Its grain offering shall be two-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil, a food offering to the LORD for a pleasing aroma, and its drink offering shall be of wine, one-fourth of a hin.
3_23_14: You shall eat neither bread nor roasted grain nor fresh grain until this very day that you have brought an offering to your God; it shall be a perpetual statute throughout your generations in all your dwellings.
3_23_15: And you shall count for yourselves from the day after the Sabbath, from the day that you brought the sheaf of the wave offering, seven complete weeks.
3_23_16: Count fifty days to the day after the seventh Sabbath; then you shall present a new grain offering to the LORD.
3_23_17: You shall bring from your dwellings two loaves of bread for a wave offering, made of two-tenths of an ephah of fine flour and baked with leaven as the firstfruits to the LORD.
3_23_18: Along with the bread you shall offer seven lambs of the first year, without blemish, one young bull, and two rams as a burnt offering to the LORD, with their grain offering and their drink offerings, a fire offering of a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
3_23_19: Then you shall offer one male goat as a sin offering, and two male lambs a year old as a sacrifice of peace offerings.
3_23_20: The priest shall wave them with the bread of the firstfruits as a wave offering before the LORD, together with the two lambs, and they shall be holy to the LORD for the priest.
3_23_21: And you shall make a proclamation on the same day that it is a holy convocation to you, and you shall do no ordinary work on it; it shall be a perpetual statute in all your dwellings throughout your generations.
3_23_22: When you reap the harvest of your land, you shall not reap to the very corners of your field, nor shall you gather any gleaning from your harvest, but you shall leave them for the poor and for the stranger: I am the LORD your God.
3_23_23: Then the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
3_23_24: Speak to the children of Israel, saying: In the seventh month, on the first day of the month, you shall observe a sabbath-rest, a sacred commemoration proclaimed with blowing of trumpets, a holy convocation.
3_23_25: You shall do no ordinary work on it, and you shall present an offering by fire to the LORD.
3_23_26: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:
3_23_27: Also the tenth day of this seventh month shall be the Day of Atonement, a holy convocation for you; you shall humble yourselves and offer an offering made by fire to the LORD.
3_23_28: And you shall do no work on that very day, for it is the Day of Atonement, to make atonement for you before the LORD your God.
3_23_29: For any person who does not humble himself in soul on that same day shall be cut off from his people.
3_23_30: And any person who does any work on that very day, that person I will destroy from among his people.
3_23_31: You shall do no work at all; it shall be a permanent statute throughout your generations in all your dwellings.
3_23_32: It shall be to you a Sabbath of complete rest, and you shall humble yourselves; on the ninth day of the month beginning at evening, from evening to evening you shall keep your Sabbath.
3_23_33: Then the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
3_23_34: Speak to the children of Israel, saying: The fifteenth day of this seventh month shall be the Feast of Tabernacles to the LORD for seven days.
3_23_35: On the first day there shall be a holy convocation, and you shall do no ordinary work on it.
3_23_36: For seven days you shall offer a food offering made by fire to the LORD, and on the eighth day you shall hold a holy convocation and offer a food offering made by fire to the LORD; it is a solemn assembly, and you shall do no ordinary work on it.
3_23_37: These are the appointed feasts of the LORD which you shall proclaim as holy convocations, to present offerings made by fire to the LORD, burnt offerings and grain offerings, sacrifices and drink offerings, each on its proper day—
3_23_38: These are besides the Sabbaths of the LORD, besides your gifts, besides all your vow offerings, and besides all your freewill offerings which you give to the LORD.
3_23_39: Also on the fifteenth day of the seventh month, when you have gathered in the produce of the land, you shall celebrate the feast of the LORD for seven days; on the first day there shall be a solemn sabbath-rest, and on the eighth day a sabbath-rest.
3_23_40: And you shall take for yourselves on the first day the fruit of beautiful trees, branches of palm trees, the boughs of leafy trees, and willows of the brook, and you shall rejoice with joy before the LORD your God for seven days.
3_23_41: You shall celebrate it as a feast to the LORD for seven days each year, a permanent statute throughout your generations, observed in the seventh month.
3_23_42: You shall dwell in booths for seven days, and all who are native-born Israelites shall dwell in booths.
3_23_43: so that your generations may know that I made the children of Israel dwell in booths when I brought them out of the land of Egypt: I am the LORD your God.
3_23_44: So Moses declared to the children of Israel the appointed feasts of the LORD.
3_25_1: The LORD spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai, saying,
3_25_2: Speak to the people of Israel and say to them: When you enter the land that I give you, the land itself shall keep a sabbath rest to the LORD.
3_25_3: Six years you shall sow your field, and six years you shall prune your vineyard and gather in its produce.
3_25_4: But in the seventh year there shall be a sabbath of complete rest for the land, a sabbath to the LORD, and you shall neither sow your field nor prune your vineyard.
3_25_5: What grows of its own accord of your harvest you shall not reap, nor gather the grapes from your unpruned vines, for it is a year of complete rest for the land.
3_25_6: And the sabbath produce of the land shall provide food for you: for yourself, your male and female servants, your hired worker, and the temporary resident who dwells with you.
3_25_7: For your livestock and the wild animals that are in your land, all its produce shall be for food.
3_25_8: You shall count off seven sabbaths of years for yourself, seven times seven years, so that the time period of the seven sabbaths of years amounts to forty-nine years.
3_25_9: Then you shall sound the ram's horn loudly on the tenth day of the seventh month; on the Day of Atonement you shall make the trumpet sound throughout all your land.
3_25_10: You shall consecrate the fiftieth year and proclaim liberty throughout all the land to all its inhabitants; it shall be a Jubilee for you when each of you shall return to his own property and each of you shall return to his clan.
3_25_11: That fiftieth year shall be a Jubilee to you; in it you shall neither sow nor reap what grows of itself, nor gather the grapes from your unpruned vines.
3_25_12: For it is the Jubilee; it shall be holy to you; you may eat its produce directly from the field.
3_25_13: In this Year of Jubilee, each of you shall return to his own possession.
3_25_14: And if you sell anything to your neighbor or buy from your neighbor's hand, you shall not wrong one another.
3_25_15: According to the number of years after the Jubilee you shall buy from your neighbor, and according to the number of remaining harvest years he shall sell to you.
3_25_16: According to the multitude of years you shall increase its price, and according to the fewer number of years you shall diminish its price, for what he is selling to you is the number of harvests.
3_25_17: Therefore you shall not wrong one another, but you shall fear your God; for I am the LORD your God.
3_25_18: So you shall observe My statutes and keep My judgments, and carefully perform them, and you will dwell in the land securely.
3_25_19: Then the land will yield its fruit, and you will eat your fill and live there in safety.
3_25_20: And if you say, "What shall we eat in the seventh year, since we are not allowed to sow nor gather in our produce?"
3_25_21: Then I will command My blessing on you in the sixth year, and it will bring forth a crop sufficient for three years.
3_25_22: And you shall sow in the eighth year and still eat old produce until the ninth year; until its crop comes in, you shall eat of the old harvest.
3_25_23: The land shall not be sold permanently, for the land is Mine; you are only foreigners and sojourners with Me.
3_25_24: And in all the land of your property you shall grant the right of redemption of the land.
3_25_25: If one of your brethren becomes poor and has sold some of his property, then his nearest kinsman may come and redeem what his brother has sold.
3_25_26: Or if the man has no one to redeem it, but he himself prospers and finds sufficient means to redeem it,
3_25_27: Then let him calculate the years since its sale, and repay the balance to the man to whom he sold it, that he may return to his property.
3_25_28: But if he does not have sufficient means to have it restored to himself, then what was sold shall remain in the hand of him who bought it until the Year of Jubilee, and in the Jubilee it shall be released, and he shall return to his possession.
3_25_29: If a man sells a dwelling house in a walled city, then he may redeem it within a whole year after it is sold; for a full year he shall have the right of redemption.
3_25_30: But if it is not redeemed within the space of a full year, then the house in the walled city shall belong permanently to the buyer throughout his generations, and it shall not be released in the Jubilee.
3_25_31: However the houses of villages which have no wall around them shall be counted as the open fields of the country, they may be redeemed, and they shall be released in the Jubilee.
3_25_32: Nevertheless the cities of the Levites, and the houses in the cities of their possession, the Levites have a permanent right to redeem at any time.
3_25_33: And if one of the Levites exercises his right of redemption, then the house that was sold in the city of their possession shall be released in the Jubilee, for the houses in the cities of the Levites are their possession among the children of Israel.
3_25_34: But the pastureland fields around their cities may not be sold, for it is their permanent possession.
3_25_35: If one of your brethren becomes poor and cannot maintain himself among you, then you shall support him like a stranger or a sojourner, that he may live with you.
3_25_36: Take no usury or profit from him, but fear your God, that your brother may live with you.
3_25_37: You shall not lend him your silver at interest, nor give him your food for profit.
3_25_38: I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt to give you the land of Canaan and to be your God.
3_25_39: If one of your brethren who dwells by you becomes poor and sells himself to you, you shall not make him serve as a slave.
3_25_40: As a hired worker and a temporary resident he shall be with you, and shall serve you until the Year of Jubilee.
3_25_41: And then he shall depart from you—he and his children with him—and shall return to his own family and go back to the possession of his fathers.
3_25_42: For they are My servants, whom I brought out of the land of Egypt; they must never be sold as slaves.
3_25_43: You shall not rule over him with harshness, but you shall fear your God.
3_25_44: And as for your male and female slaves whom you may have—from the pagan nations that are around you, from them you may purchase male and female slaves.
3_25_45: Moreover you may purchase from the children of the foreigners who sojourn among you and their families who are with you, whom they have produced in your land, and they may become your possession.
3_25_46: You may take them as an inheritance for your children after you, to inherit them as a possession; they shall be your permanent slaves, but regarding your brethren the children of Israel, you shall not rule over one another harshly.
3_25_47: Now if a foreigner or temporary resident living among you becomes rich, and one of your brethren who dwells by him becomes poor and sells himself to the stranger or sojourner close to you, or to a member of the stranger's family.
3_25_48: After he is sold he shall have the right to be redeemed again, and one of his brothers may redeem him.
3_25_49: His uncle or his uncle's son may redeem him, or any close relative from his clan may redeem him, or if he prospers he may redeem himself.
3_25_50: He shall calculate with the one who purchased him from the year when he sold himself to him until the Year of Jubilee, and the price of his release shall be determined by the number of years, reckoned as the wages of a hired worker for that period of time.
3_25_51: If there are still many years remaining until the jubilee, he shall pay back proportionately for his redemption based on the price for which he was bought.
3_25_52: And if there remain but a few years until the Year of Jubilee, then he shall calculate with him, and according to his years of service he shall repay the price of his redemption in proportion to those years.
3_25_53: He shall be with him as a yearly hired servant, and he shall not rule ruthlessly over him in your sight.
3_25_54: And if he is not redeemed by these means, then he shall go free in the Year of Jubilee—he and his children with him.
3_25_55: For the children of Israel belong to Me as servants; they are My servants whom I brought out of the land of Egypt: I am the LORD your God.
3_26_1: You shall not make idols for yourselves; neither a carved image nor a sacred pillar shall you erect for yourselves, nor shall you set up a sculptured stone in your land to bow down to it, for I am the LORD your God.
3_26_2: You shall keep My Sabbaths and show reverence for My sanctuary: I am the LORD.
3_26_3: If you walk in My statutes and keep My commandments and faithfully perform them,
3_26_4: Then I will give you rain at the right time, the land shall yield its produce, and the trees of the field will bear their fruit.
3_26_5: Your threshing shall last until grape harvest, and the grape harvest shall last until sowing time; you shall eat your food to the full and dwell securely in your land.
3_26_6: I will give peace in the land, and you shall lie down with nothing to frighten you; I will remove dangerous beasts from the land, and no sword will pass through your land.
3_26_7: You will chase your enemies, and they will fall by the sword before you.
3_26_8: Five of you shall chase a hundred, and a hundred of you shall put ten thousand to flight; your enemies shall fall before you by the sword.
3_26_9: For I will turn to you favorably and make you fruitful, multiply you and confirm My covenant with you.
3_26_10: You shall eat the old store long kept, and clear out the old to make room for the new.
3_26_11: I will set My dwelling among you, and My soul shall not reject you.
3_26_12: I will walk among you and be your God, and you shall be My people.
3_26_13: I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt so that you would no longer be their slaves; I have broken the bars of your yoke and enabled you to walk upright.
3_26_14: But if you do not listen to Me and do not carry out all these commandments,
3_26_15: And if you despise My statutes, or if your soul abhors My judgments, so that you will not do all My commandments, but break My covenant,
3_26_16: I also will do this to you: I will appoint sudden terror over you, wasting disease and fever which shall consume the eyes and cause sorrow of heart, and you shall sow your seed in vain, for your enemies shall eat it.
3_26_17: I will set My face against you, and you will be struck down before your enemies. Those who hate you shall rule over you, and you shall flee when no one is pursuing you.
3_26_18: And after all this, if you still do not obey Me, then I will discipline you seven times more for your sins.
3_26_19: I will break the pride of your power; I will make your heavens like iron and your earth like bronze.
3_26_20: And your strength shall be spent uselessly for nothing, for your land shall not yield its produce, nor shall the trees of the land yield their fruit.
3_26_21: Then, if you walk contrary to Me and are not willing to obey Me, I will multiply and bring on you seven times more plagues according to your sins.
3_26_22: I will also send wild beasts among you, which shall bereave you of your children, destroy your livestock, and reduce you few in number, and your roads shall be deserted.
3_26_23: And if by these things you are not reformed by Me, but continue to walk contrary to Me,
3_26_24: Then I also will walk contrary to you with hostility, and I myself will punish you seven times for your sins.
3_26_25: And I will bring a sword against you that will execute the vengeance of the covenant; though you are gathered together within your cities I will send pestilence among you, and you shall be delivered into the hand of the enemy.
3_26_26: When I break your supply of bread, ten women will bake your bread in one oven and ration it out by weight, and you will eat but not be satisfied.
3_26_27: And if in spite of all this you do not obey Me, but walk contrary to Me with hostility,
3_26_28: Then I also will act contrary to you in fury, and I, even I, will discipline you seven times for your sins.
3_26_29: You shall eat the flesh of your own sons, and you shall eat the flesh of your own daughters.
3_26_30: I will destroy your high places, cut down your incense altars, and heap your carcasses on the lifeless forms of your idols, and My soul shall abhor you.
3_26_31: I will lay your cities waste and bring your sanctuaries to desolation, and I will not smell the pleasing fragrance of your sweet aromas.
3_26_32: I myself will bring the land to desolation, and your enemies who settle in it shall be appalled at it.
3_26_33: I will scatter you among the nations and draw out a sword to chase after you; your land shall be desolate and your cities waste.
3_26_34: Then the land will enjoy its sabbaths all the days it lies desolate and you are in your enemies' land; then the land shall rest and enjoy its sabbaths.
3_26_35: As long as it lies desolate it shall observe rest for the time it did not rest on your sabbaths when you dwelt in it.
3_26_36: And as for those of you who are left, I will send faintness into their hearts in the lands of their enemies; the sound of a driven leaf shall chase them and cause them to flee as though fleeing from a sword, and they shall fall when no one pursues.
3_26_37: They shall stumble over one another as if fleeing from a sword, though no one pursues, and you shall have no power to stand before your enemies.
3_26_38: You will perish among the nations, and the land of your enemies will consume you.
3_26_39: And those of you who are left shall waste away in your enemies' lands because of their iniquity, and also because of the iniquities of their fathers they shall waste away with them.
3_26_40: But if they confess their iniquity and the iniquity of their fathers, with their unfaithfulness in which they were unfaithful to Me, and that they also have walked with hostility contrary to Me,
3_26_41: and that I also have walked contrary to them and have brought them into the land of their enemies; if their uncircumcised hearts are humbled, and they make amends for their guilt—
3_26_42: Then I will remember My covenant with Jacob, and also My covenant with Isaac and My covenant with Abraham I will remember, and I will remember the land.
3_26_43: The land also shall be left abandoned by them, and will make up for its sabbaths while it lies desolate without them; they will accept their punishment because they rejected My judgments and because their soul abhorred My statutes.
3_26_44: Yet in spite of all this, when they are in the land of their enemies, I will not reject them, nor shall I abhor them, to utterly destroy them and break My covenant with them; for I am the LORD their God.
3_26_45: But for their sake I will remember the covenant with their ancestors, whom I brought out of the land of Egypt in the sight of all the nations, that I might be their God: I am the LORD.
3_26_46: These are the statutes and judgments and laws which the LORD established between Himself and the children of Israel on Mount Sinai through Moses.
3_27_1: Now the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
3_27_2: Speak to the children of Israel and say to them: When anyone makes a special vow to consecrate certain persons to the LORD according to your valuation,
3_27_3: If your valuation is of a male from twenty years old up to sixty years old, then your assessment shall be fifty shekels of silver, according to the shekel of the sanctuary.
3_27_4: If it is a female, then your valuation shall be thirty shekels of silver.
3_27_5: And if from five years old up to twenty years old, then your valuation for a male shall be twenty shekels, and for a female ten shekels.
3_27_6: And if the person is from a month old up to five years old, then your valuation for a male shall be five shekels of silver, and for a female your valuation shall be three shekels of silver.
3_27_7: And if the person is sixty years old and above, if it is a male, then your valuation shall be fifteen shekels, and for a female ten shekels.
3_27_8: But if he is too poor to pay your valuation, then he shall present himself before the priest, and the priest shall set a value for him; according to what the one who vowed can afford, the priest shall value him.
3_27_9: If the vow involves an animal that may be brought as an offering to the LORD, all that anyone gives to the LORD shall be holy.
3_27_10: He shall not substitute it or exchange it, good for bad or bad for good; and if he does in fact exchange animal for animal, then both it and the substitute shall be holy.
3_27_11: If it is an unclean animal of the kind which they do not offer as a sacrifice to the LORD, then he shall present the animal before the priest.
3_27_12: The priest shall set a value for it, whether it is good or bad; as you, the priest, assess it, so it shall be final.
3_27_13: But if he wants at all to redeem it, then he must add one-fifth to the assessed valuation.
3_27_14: And when a man dedicates his house as holy to the LORD, then the priest shall assess its value, whether it is good or bad; as the priest values it, so it shall stand.
3_27_15: If he who consecrated it wishes to redeem his house, then he must add one-fifth of the money of your valuation to it, and it shall be his.
3_27_16: If a man dedicates to the LORD part of a field of his possession, then your valuation shall be proportionate to the seed needed for it: a homer of barley seed shall be valued at fifty shekels of silver.
3_27_17: If he dedicates his field during the Year of Jubilee, according to your valuation it shall stand.
3_27_18: But if he dedicates his field after the Jubilee, then the priest shall calculate the price for him according to the years that remain until the Year of Jubilee, and it shall be deducted from your valuation.
3_27_19: And if he who dedicates the field ever wishes to redeem it, then he must add one-fifth of the money of your valuation to it, and it shall belong to him.
3_27_20: But if he does not wish to redeem the field, or if he has sold the field to another man, it may no longer be redeemed.
3_27_21: But the field, when it is released in the Jubilee, shall be holy to the LORD as a field devoted, and it shall become the property of the priest.
3_27_22: And if a man dedicates to the LORD a field which he has purchased, which is not part of the field of his family possession,
3_27_23: Then the priest shall calculate for him the amount of your valuation up to the Year of Jubilee, and he shall give your valuation on that day as a holy offering to the LORD.
3_27_24: In the Year of Jubilee the field shall return to him from whom it was bought, to the original owner who possessed the land.
3_27_25: All your valuations shall be measured according to the standard sanctuary shekel, twenty gerahs to the shekel.
3_27_26: However, the firstborn of the animals, which as a firstborn already belongs to the LORD, no man shall dedicate; whether ox or sheep, it is the LORD's.
3_27_27: And if it is an unclean animal, then he shall buy it back according to your valuation and add one-fifth to it; or if it is not redeemed, then it shall be sold according to your valuation.
3_27_28: Nevertheless no devoted offering that a man may permanently devote to the LORD of all that he has, both man and beast, or the field of his possession, shall be sold or redeemed; every devoted offering is most holy to the LORD.
3_27_29: No person under the ban who has been set apart for destruction among men shall be ransomed but shall surely be put to death.
3_27_30: Every tithe of the land, whether grain from the fields or fruit from the trees, belongs to the LORD and is holy to the LORD.
3_27_31: If a man wishes to redeem any part of his tithe, he shall add one-fifth to its value.
3_27_32: And concerning the tithe of the herd or the flock, of whatever passes under the shepherd's rod, the tenth one shall be holy to the LORD.
3_27_33: He shall not inspect whether it is good or bad, nor shall he exchange it; and if he exchanges it at all, then both it and the one exchanged for it shall be holy; it shall not be redeemed.
3_27_34: These are the commandments which the LORD gave through Moses for the children of Israel on Mount Sinai.
4_2_1: The LORD gave these instructions to Moses and Aaron, saying:
4_2_2: Everyone of the children of Israel shall camp by his own standard, beside the banners of his father's house; they shall camp facing the tent of meeting at a distance on every side.
4_2_3: On the east side, toward the sunrise, those of the standard of the forces with Judah shall camp according to their armies, and Nahshon the son of Amminadab shall be the leader of the children of Judah.
4_2_4: And his army was listed at seventy-four thousand six hundred.
4_2_5: Those who camp next to him shall be the tribe of Issachar, and Nethanel the son of Zuar shall be the leader of the people of Issachar.
4_2_6: And his army was listed at fifty-four thousand four hundred.
4_2_7: Then comes the tribe of Zebulun, and Eliab the son of Helon shall be the chief of the people of Zebulun.
4_2_8: And his army was enrolled at fifty-seven thousand four hundred.
4_2_9: All who were numbered in the camp of Judah according to their armies were one hundred and eighty-six thousand four hundred—they shall set out first on the march.
4_2_10: On the south side shall be the standard of the camp of Reuben according to their armies, and the leader of the people of Reuben shall be Elizur the son of Shedeur.
4_2_11: And his army was numbered at forty-six thousand five hundred.
4_2_12: Those who camp next to him shall be the tribe of Simeon, and the chief of the children of Simeon shall be Shelumiel the son of Zurishaddai.
4_2_13: And his army was numbered at fifty-nine thousand three hundred.
4_2_14: Then comes the tribe of Gad, and the leader of the people of Gad shall be Eliasaph the son of Reuel.
4_2_15: And his army was numbered at forty-five thousand six hundred and fifty.
4_2_16: All who were numbered according to their armies of the forces with Reuben, one hundred and fifty-one thousand four hundred and fifty—they shall set out second.
4_2_17: Then the tabernacle of meeting shall set out with the camp of the Levites in the middle of the camps; just as they camp, so they shall move out, each in position by their standards.
4_2_18: On the west side shall be the standard of the camp of Ephraim according to their companies, and the leader of the children of Ephraim shall be Elishama the son of Ammihud.
4_2_19: And his army was numbered at forty thousand five hundred.
4_2_20: Next to him comes the tribe of Manasseh, and the chief of the children of Manasseh shall be Gamaliel the son of Pedahzur.
4_2_21: And his company was numbered at thirty-two thousand two hundred.
4_2_22: Next comes the tribe of Benjamin, and the leader of the children of Benjamin shall be Abidan the son of Gideoni.
4_2_23: And his army was numbered at thirty-five thousand four hundred.
4_2_24: All who were numbered according to their military divisions of the camp of Ephraim totaled one hundred and eight thousand one hundred, and they shall set out third on the march.
4_2_25: The standard of the camp of Dan shall be on the north side according to their companies, and the leader of the children of Dan shall be Ahiezer the son of Ammishaddai.
4_2_26: And his division was numbered at sixty-two thousand seven hundred.
4_2_27: Those who camp next to him shall be the tribe of Asher, and the chief of the children of Asher shall be Pagiel the son of Ocran.
4_2_28: And his army was numbered at forty-one thousand five hundred.
4_2_29: Then comes the tribe of Naphtali, and the leader of the people of Naphtali shall be Ahira the son of Enan.
4_2_30: And his army was numbered at fifty-three thousand four hundred.
4_2_31: All who were numbered in the camp of Dan were one hundred and fifty-seven thousand six hundred—they shall set out last, with their standards.
4_2_32: These are the ones who were numbered of the children of Israel by their fathers' houses, and all who were numbered in the camps according to their military divisions were six hundred and three thousand five hundred and fifty.
4_2_33: But the Levites were not numbered among the people of Israel, just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
4_2_34: Thus the children of Israel did everything the LORD commanded Moses; so they camped by their standards and so they set out, each one by his clan, according to their fathers' houses.
4_4_1: Then the LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying:
4_4_2: Take a census of the descendants of Kohath from among the children of Levi, by their clans, by their fathers' house.
4_4_3: From thirty years old and above, even to fifty years old, all who are qualified to enter the service to do the work in the tent of meeting.
4_4_4: This is the work of the sons of Kohath in the tabernacle of meeting, concerning the most holy things:
4_4_5: When the camp prepares to set out, Aaron and his sons shall go in and take down the screening veil and cover the ark of the Testimony with it.
4_4_6: Then they shall put on it a covering of fine leather, and spread over that a cloth entirely of blue, and they shall insert its poles.
4_4_7: On the table of showbread they shall spread a blue cloth and put on it the dishes, the pans, the bowls, and the pitchers for the drink offering, and the continual showbread shall be on it.
4_4_8: They shall spread over them a scarlet cloth and cover the same with a covering of fine leather, and they shall insert its poles.
4_4_9: And they shall take a blue cloth and cover the lampstand for the light, along with its lamps, its wick-trimmers, its trays, and all its oil vessels with which they service it.
4_4_10: Then they shall put it with all its utensils in a covering of fine leather, and put it on the carrying frame.
4_4_11: Over the golden altar they shall spread a blue cloth and cover it with a covering of fine leather, and they shall insert its carrying poles.
4_4_12: Then they shall take all the utensils of service with which they minister in the sanctuary, put them in a blue cloth, cover them with a covering of fine leather, and put them on a carrying frame.
4_4_13: Also they shall remove the ashes from the altar and spread a purple cloth over it.
4_4_14: They shall put on it all its implements with which they serve there—the firepans, the meat forks, the shovels, the basins, and all the utensils of the altar—and they shall spread on it a covering of fine leather, and insert its poles.
4_4_15: After Aaron and his sons have finished covering the sanctuary and all the furnishings of the sanctuary when the camp is set to move on, then the sons of Kohath shall come to carry them, but they must not touch any holy thing lest they die, and these are the things in the tent of meeting which the sons of Kohath are to carry.
4_4_16: The appointed responsibility of Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest is the oil for the light, the fragrant incense, the regular grain offering, the anointing oil, and the oversight of the entire tabernacle and all that is in it, with the sanctuary and its furnishings.
4_4_17: Then the LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying:
4_4_18: Do not allow the tribe of the families of the Kohathites to be cut off from among the Levites.
4_4_19: But do this for them, that they may live and not die when they approach the most holy objects: Aaron and his sons shall go in and assign each of them to his task and his load.
4_4_20: But they shall not go in to look at the holy things even for a moment while they are being covered, lest they die.
4_4_21: Then the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:
4_4_22: Also take a census of the sons of Gershon, by their ancestral houses, by their families.
4_4_23: From thirty years old and above, even to fifty years old, you shall number them, all who enter to perform the duty, to do the work in the tabernacle of meeting.
4_4_24: This is the service of the families of the Gershonites, in serving and bearing burdens.
4_4_25: They shall carry the curtains of the tabernacle and the tent of meeting with its covering, the covering of fine leather that is on top of it, and the screen for the entrance of the tent of meeting.
4_4_26: The hangings of the court, the screen for the entrance of the gate of the court which surrounds the tabernacle and altar, and their cords and all the equipment for their service and everything that needs to be done with these things: so shall they serve.
4_4_27: At the command of Aaron and his sons shall be all the service of the sons of the Gershonites, in all their loads and in all their work, and you shall assign to them as their duty all that they are responsible to carry.
4_4_28: This is the service of the families of the sons of Gershon in the tabernacle of meeting, and their duties shall be under the direction of Ithamar the son of Aaron the priest.
4_4_29: As for the sons of Merari, you shall register them by their clans and by their fathers' households.
4_4_30: From thirty years old and above, even to fifty years old, you shall number them, everyone who is qualified to enter the service to do the work of the tabernacle of meeting.
4_4_31: And this is what they are responsible to carry as all their service for the tabernacle of meeting: the boards of the tabernacle, its bars, its pillars, its sockets,
4_4_32: The pillars around the court with their sockets, pegs, and cords, with all their equipment and all the work related to their service; and you shall assign to each man by name the items he is responsible to carry.
4_4_33: This is the service of the families of the sons of Merari, the whole of their service for the tabernacle of meeting, under the direction of Ithamar the son of Aaron the priest.
4_4_34: So Moses, Aaron, and the leaders of the congregation registered the sons of the Kohathites by their clans and by their fathers' house.
4_4_35: From thirty years old and above, even to fifty years old, everyone who could enter the service for work in the tabernacle of meeting.
4_4_36: And those who were numbered by their clans were two thousand seven hundred and fifty.
4_4_37: These were the ones who were registered of the families of the Kohathites, everyone who was eligible to serve in the tabernacle of meeting, whom Moses and Aaron numbered according to the commandment of the LORD through Moses.
4_4_38: And those who were registered of the sons of Gershon, by their clans and by their fathers' households,
4_4_39: From thirty years old and above, even to fifty years old, everyone who was qualified to enter the service for work in the tabernacle of meeting—
4_4_40: Those who were numbered by their clans, by their fathers' house, were two thousand six hundred and thirty.
4_4_41: These are the ones who were numbered of the families of the sons of Gershon, everyone who could serve in the tabernacle of meeting, whom Moses and Aaron numbered according to the commandment of the LORD.
4_4_42: Those of the families of the sons of Merari who were counted, by their clans, by their fathers' house,
4_4_43: From thirty years old and above, even to fifty years old, everyone who could enter the service for work in the tabernacle of meeting—
4_4_44: Those who were numbered by their clans were three thousand two hundred.
4_4_45: These are the ones who were numbered of the families of the sons of Merari, whom Moses and Aaron numbered according to the commandment of the LORD through Moses.
4_4_46: All who were numbered of the Levites, whom Moses, Aaron, and the chiefs of Israel numbered, by their clans and by their fathers' houses,
4_4_47: From thirty years old and above, even to fifty years old, everyone who could enter to do the work of service and the work of bearing burdens in the tabernacle of meeting.
4_4_48: Those who were registered were eight thousand five hundred and eighty.
4_4_49: According to the commandment of the LORD through Moses they were numbered, each one according to his service and according to his task of carrying, thus they were registered by him as the LORD commanded Moses.
4_7_18: On the second day Nethanel the son of Zuar, leader of the tribe of Issachar, presented his offering.
4_7_19: For his offering he presented one silver platter, the weight of which was one hundred and thirty shekels, and one silver basin of seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering.
4_7_20: One gold pan of ten shekels, filled with incense.
4_7_21: One young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old, as a burnt offering.
4_7_22: One male goat as a sin offering.
4_7_23: And as the sacrifice of peace offerings two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Nethanel the son of Zuar.
4_7_24: On the third day Eliab the son of Helon, leader of the tribe of Zebulun, presented an offering.
4_7_25: His offering was one silver platter, the weight of which was one hundred and thirty shekels, and one silver basin of seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering.
4_7_26: One gold pan of ten shekels, full of incense.
4_7_27: One young bull from the herd, one ram, and one male lamb in its first year, as a burnt offering.
4_7_28: One male goat as a sin offering;
4_7_29: And for the sacrifice of peace offerings two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five one-year-old male lambs. This was the offering of Eliab the son of Helon.
4_7_30: On the fourth day Elizur the son of Shedeur, leader of the tribe of Reuben, presented his offering.
4_7_31: His offering was one silver platter weighing one hundred and thirty shekels and one silver basin of seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering.
4_7_32: One gold pan of ten shekels, full of incense.
4_7_33: One young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old, as a burnt offering.
4_7_34: One male goat for a sin offering.
4_7_35: And for the sacrifice of peace offerings two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old—this was the offering of Elizur the son of Shedeur.
4_7_36: On the fifth day Shelumiel the son of Zurishaddai, leader of the tribe of Simeon, presented an offering.
4_7_37: His offering was one silver platter whose weight was one hundred and thirty shekels and one silver bowl of seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering.
4_7_38: one gold pan of ten shekels, full of incense;
4_7_39: One young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old, as a burnt offering.
4_7_40: one male goat as a sin offering;
4_7_41: And for the sacrifice of peace offerings two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old; this was the offering of Shelumiel the son of Zurishaddai.
4_7_42: On the sixth day Eliasaph the son of Deuel, leader of the tribe of Gad, presented an offering.
4_7_43: His offering was one silver platter, the weight of which was one hundred and thirty shekels, and one silver basin of seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering.
4_7_44: One gold pan of ten shekels, full of incense.
4_7_45: One young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old, as a burnt offering.
4_7_46: One male goat as a sin offering.
4_7_47: And as the sacrifice of fellowship offerings two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Eliasaph the son of Deuel.
4_7_48: On the seventh day Elishama the son of Ammihud, leader of the tribe of Ephraim, presented his offering.
4_7_49: His offering was one silver platter, the weight of which was one hundred and thirty shekels, and one silver basin of seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering.
4_7_50: one gold pan of ten shekels, full of incense;
4_7_51: One young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old, as a burnt offering.
4_7_52: One male goat as a sin offering.
4_7_53: And as the sacrifice of peace offerings two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Elishama the son of Ammihud.
4_7_54: On the eighth day Gamaliel the son of Pedahzur, leader of the tribe of Manasseh, presented his offering.
4_7_55: His offering consisted of one silver platter weighing one hundred and thirty shekels and one silver bowl of seventy shekels according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering.
4_7_56: one gold pan of ten shekels, full of incense;
4_7_57: One young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old, as a burnt offering.
4_7_58: One male goat as a sin offering.
4_7_59: And as the sacrifice of peace offerings two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Gamaliel the son of Pedahzur.
4_7_60: On the ninth day Abidan the son of Gideoni, leader of the tribe of Benjamin, presented an offering.
4_7_61: His offering was one silver platter, the weight of which was one hundred and thirty shekels, and one silver basin of seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering.
4_7_62: One gold pan of ten shekels, full of incense.
4_7_63: One young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old, as a burnt offering.
4_7_64: one male goat for a sin offering;
4_7_65: And for the sacrifice of peace offerings two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old—this was the offering of Abidan the son of Gideoni.
4_7_66: On the tenth day Ahiezer the son of Ammishaddai, leader of the tribe of Dan, presented an offering.
4_7_67: His offering was one silver platter, the weight of which was one hundred and thirty shekels, and one silver basin of seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering.
4_7_68: one gold pan of ten shekels, full of incense;
4_7_69: One young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old, as a burnt offering.
4_7_70: one male goat as a sin offering;
4_7_71: And for the sacrifice of fellowship offerings two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Ahiezer the son of Ammishaddai.
4_7_72: On the eleventh day Pagiel the son of Ocran, prince and leader of the tribe of Asher, presented an offering.
4_7_73: His offering was one silver platter, the weight of which was one hundred and thirty shekels, and one silver basin of seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering.
4_7_74: one gold pan of ten shekels, full of incense;
4_7_75: One young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old, as a burnt offering.
4_7_76: One male goat for a sin offering.
4_7_77: And as the sacrifice of peace offerings two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old; this was the offering of Pagiel the son of Ocran.
4_7_78: On the twelfth day Ahira the son of Enan, leader of the tribe of Naphtali, presented his offering.
4_7_79: His offering was one silver platter, the weight of which was one hundred and thirty shekels, and one silver basin of seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering.
4_7_80: One gold pan of ten shekels, full of incense.
4_7_81: One young bull, one ram, and one male lamb a year old, as a burnt offering.
4_7_82: one male goat as a sin offering;
4_7_83: And for the sacrifice of fellowship offerings two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs in their first year; this was the offering of Ahira the son of Enan.
4_7_84: This was the dedication offering for the altar on the day when it was anointed, from the leaders of Israel: twelve silver platters, twelve silver bowls, and twelve gold pans.
4_7_85: Each silver platter weighed one hundred and thirty shekels and each bowl seventy shekels, and all the silver of the utensils weighed two thousand four hundred shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary.
4_7_86: The twelve gold pans full of incense each weighed ten shekels according to the shekel of the sanctuary, all the gold of the pans weighing one hundred and twenty shekels.
4_7_87: All the cattle for the burnt offering totaled twelve young bulls, twelve rams, twelve male lambs a year old with their grain offering, and twelve male goats for the sin offering.
4_7_88: And all the oxen for the sacrifice of peace offerings were twenty-four bulls, the rams sixty, the male goats sixty, and the male lambs in their first year sixty. This was the dedication gift for the altar after it was anointed.
4_7_89: Now when Moses went into the tabernacle of meeting to speak with the LORD, he heard the voice speaking to him from above the mercy seat that was on the ark of the Testimony, from between the two cherubim; thus He spoke to him.
4_8_1: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:
4_8_2: Speak to Aaron and say to him, When you set up the lamps, the seven lamps shall give light in front of the lampstand.
4_8_3: And Aaron did so; he set up the lamps to give light toward the front of the lampstand, as the LORD commanded Moses.
4_8_4: Now this workmanship of the lampstand was hammered gold; from its base to its flowers it was hammered work, and according to the pattern which the LORD had shown Moses, so he made the lampstand.
4_8_5: Then the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:
4_8_6: Take the Levites from among the children of Israel and make them ceremonially clean.
4_8_7: Thus you shall do to them to cleanse them: Sprinkle water of purification on them, and let them shave their entire body, and let them wash their clothes, and so make themselves clean.
4_8_8: Then let them take a young bull with its grain offering of fine flour mixed with oil, and you shall take a second young bull as a sin offering.
4_8_9: And you shall bring the Levites before the tabernacle of meeting, and you shall assemble together the whole congregation of the children of Israel.
4_8_10: So you shall present the Levites before the LORD, and the people of Israel shall lay their hands on the Levites.
4_8_11: And Aaron shall offer the Levites before the LORD as a wave offering from the children of Israel, that they may be dedicated to perform the work of the LORD.
4_8_12: Then the Levites shall lay their hands on the heads of the young bulls, and you shall sacrifice one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering to the LORD, to make atonement for the Levites.
4_8_13: And you shall have the Levites stand before Aaron and his sons, and then present them as a wave offering to the LORD.
4_8_14: Thus you shall set apart the Levites from among the children of Israel, and the Levites shall be Mine.
4_8_15: After that the Levites shall go in to serve the tabernacle of meeting, once you have cleansed them and presented them as a wave offering.
4_8_16: For they are wholly given to Me from among the children of Israel; I have taken them for Myself in place of all who open the womb, the firstborn of all the children of Israel.
4_8_17: For all the firstborn among the children of Israel are Mine, both man and beast; on the day that I struck down all the firstborn in the land of Egypt I consecrated them for Myself.
4_8_18: I have taken the Levites in place of all the firstborn of the children of Israel.
4_8_19: And I have given the Levites as a gift to Aaron and his sons from among the children of Israel, to perform the work for the children of Israel in the tabernacle of meeting and to make atonement on behalf of the children of Israel, so that there will be no plague among the children of Israel when the children of Israel come near the sanctuary.
4_8_20: Thus Moses and Aaron and all the congregation of the children of Israel did to the Levites, carefully following all that the LORD commanded Moses concerning the Levites, so the children of Israel did to them.
4_8_21: The Levites purified themselves from sin and washed their clothes; then Aaron presented them as a wave offering before the LORD, and Aaron made atonement for them to cleanse them.
4_8_22: After that the Levites went in to perform their service in the tabernacle of meeting before Aaron and his sons; as the LORD had commanded Moses concerning the Levites, so they did to them.
4_8_23: Then the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
4_8_24: This is what applies to the Levites: From twenty-five years old and above one may enter to perform duty in the service of the tabernacle of meeting.
4_8_25: And at the age of fifty years they must retire from performing this work and shall serve no more.
4_8_26: They may assist their brothers in the tabernacle of meeting to keep guard and attend to needs, but they themselves shall do no service. Thus you shall deal with the Levites regarding their duties.
4_9_1: Now the LORD spoke to Moses in the Wilderness of Sinai, in the first month of the second year after their departure from the land of Egypt, saying:
4_9_2: Let the children of Israel observe the Passover at its appointed time.
4_9_3: On the fourteenth day of this month, at twilight, you shall observe it at its appointed time according to all its statutes and ceremonies you shall keep it.
4_9_4: So Moses told the children of Israel that they should observe the Passover.
4_9_5: And they observed the Passover on the fourteenth day of the first month, at twilight, in the Wilderness of Sinai; according to all that the LORD had commanded Moses, so the children of Israel did.
4_9_6: Now there were certain men who were unclean by touching a human corpse, so that they could not observe the Passover on that day, and they came before Moses and Aaron that same day.
4_9_7: And those men said to him, "We became defiled by a human corpse. Why should we be kept from presenting the offering of the LORD at its appointed time among the children of Israel?"
4_9_8: And Moses said to them, "Wait here, that I may hear what the LORD will command concerning you."
4_9_9: Then the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
4_9_10: Speak to the children of Israel, saying: If anyone of you or your posterity is unclean because of a dead body, or is on a distant journey, he may still keep the LORD's Passover.
4_9_11: On the fourteenth day of the second month at twilight they shall observe it, eating it with unleavened bread and bitter herbs.
4_9_12: They shall leave none of it until morning, nor break any of its bones. According to all the statutes of the Passover they shall observe it.
4_9_13: But the man who is clean and is not on a journey, and yet neglects to keep the Passover, that same person shall be cut off from among his people, because he did not bring the offering of the LORD at its appointed time; that man shall bear his sin.
4_9_14: And if a foreigner dwells among you and would observe the LORD's Passover, he must do so according to the statute of the Passover and according to its ordinance; you shall have one statute, both for the foreigner and the native of the land.
4_9_15: Now on the day that the tabernacle was set up, the cloud covered the tabernacle, the tent of the Testimony; from evening until morning it was above the tabernacle like the appearance of fire.
4_9_16: So it was continuously: the cloud covered it by day, and the appearance of fire by night.
