50_3_20: For our citizenship is in heaven, from which we also eagerly wait for the Savior, the Lord Jesus Christ,
50_3_21: who will transform our humble, lowly bodies to be conformed to His glorious body, by the power that enables Him to subject all things to Himself.
50_4_1: Therefore, my beloved and longed-for brothers and sisters, my joy and crown, stand firm in the Lord in this way, dear friends.
50_4_2: I implore Euodia and I implore Syntyche to agree in the Lord.
50_4_3: And I ask you, true companion, help these women who contended at my side in the gospel, along with Clement and the rest of my fellow workers, whose names are in the Book of Life.
50_4_4: Rejoice in the Lord always. Again I will say, rejoice!
50_4_5: Let your graciousness be known to everyone. The Lord is near.
50_4_6: Be anxious for nothing, but in everything by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, let your requests be made known to God;
50_4_7: and the peace of God, which surpasses all understanding, will guard your hearts and minds in Christ Jesus.
50_4_8: Finally, brothers and sisters, whatever things are true, whatever things are honorable, whatever things are just, whatever things are pure, whatever things are lovely, whatever things are commendable — if there is any moral excellence and if there is anything praiseworthy — dwell on these things.
50_4_9: The things which you learned and received and heard and saw in me — practice these things, and the God of peace will be with you.
50_4_10: But I rejoiced in the Lord greatly that now at last your concern for me has flourished again; though you certainly did care, you lacked the opportunity.
50_4_11: Not that I speak from want, for I have learned in whatever circumstances I find myself to be content.
50_4_12: I know how to be brought low, and I know how to abound. In any and every circumstance I have learned the secret of being content — whether well fed or hungry, whether in abundance or in need.
50_4_13: I can do all things through Christ who strengthens me.
50_4_14: Nevertheless you did well by partnering with me in my hardship.
50_4_15: Now you Philippians know also that in the early days of the gospel, when I departed from Macedonia, no church shared with me in the matter of giving and receiving except you alone.
50_4_16: For even in Thessalonica you sent help for my needs more than once.
50_4_17: Not that I seek the gift itself, but I seek the fruit that increases to your account.
50_4_18: But I have received everything in full, and I have an abundance. I am fully supplied, having received from Epaphroditus the gifts you sent — a fragrant offering, an acceptable sacrifice, well pleasing to God.
50_4_19: And my God shall supply all your needs according to His riches in glory by Christ Jesus.
50_4_20: Now to our God and Father be glory forever and ever. Amen.
50_4_21: Greet every saint in Christ Jesus. The brothers and sisters who are with me greet you.
50_4_22: All the saints greet you, especially those who are of Caesar's household.
50_4_23: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.
51_1_1: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, and Timothy our brother,
51_1_2: To the saints and faithful brothers and sisters in Christ who are in Colossae: Grace to you and peace from God our Father.
51_1_3: We always thank God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, when we pray for you,
51_1_4: since we heard of your faith in Christ Jesus and the love you have for all the saints,
51_1_5: because of the hope reserved for you in heaven, of which you heard before in the word of truth — the gospel —
51_1_6: which has come to you, and is bearing fruit and growing throughout all the world, just as it has been doing among you since the day you heard and truly came to understand the grace of God.
51_1_7: Just as you learned from Epaphras, our dearly loved fellow servant, who is a faithful minister of Christ on your behalf,
51_1_8: and who has told us about your love in the Spirit.
51_1_9: For this reason, since the day we heard it, we have not ceased to pray for you, asking that you may be filled with the knowledge of His will in all spiritual wisdom and understanding,
51_1_10: so that you may walk worthy of the Lord, fully pleasing to Him, bearing fruit in every good work and growing in the knowledge of God;
51_1_11: strengthened with all power according to His glorious might, so that you may have all endurance and patience, joyfully
51_1_12: giving thanks to the Father, who has qualified you to share in the saints' inheritance in the light.
51_1_13: He has rescued us from the domain of darkness and transferred us into the kingdom of the Son He loves,
51_1_14: in whom we have redemption — the forgiveness of sins.
51_1_15: He is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn over all creation.
51_1_16: For by Him all things were created — in heaven and on earth, visible and invisible, whether thrones or dominions or rulers or authorities. All things have been created through Him and for Him.
51_1_17: And He is before all things, and in Him all things hold together.
51_1_18: He is also the head of the body, the church; He is the beginning, the firstborn from the dead, so that He might come to have first place in everything.
51_1_19: For it pleased the Father that all His fullness should dwell in Him,
51_1_20: and through Him to reconcile all things to Himself — whether things on earth or things in heaven — having made peace through the blood of His cross.
51_1_21: And you, who once were alienated and hostile in mind, doing evil deeds,
51_1_22: He has now reconciled in the body of His flesh through death, to present you holy, faultless, and blameless before Him —
51_1_23: if indeed you continue in the faith, grounded and steadfast, not moved away from the hope of the gospel which you heard, which has been proclaimed in all creation under heaven, of which I, Paul, became a servant.
51_1_24: I now rejoice in my sufferings for you, and in my flesh I am filling up what is lacking in Christ's afflictions for the sake of His body, which is the church,
51_1_25: of which I became a servant according to the stewardship from God given to me for you, to make the word of God fully known —
51_1_26: the mystery hidden for ages and generations past, but now revealed to His saints.
51_1_27: To them God chose to make known how great are the riches of the glory of this mystery among the Gentiles: which is Christ in you, the hope of glory.
51_1_28: Him we proclaim, warning everyone and teaching everyone with all wisdom, so that we may present everyone mature in Christ.
51_1_29: To this end I also labor, striving with all His strength that works powerfully within me.
51_2_1: For I want you to know what a great struggle I have for you and those in Laodicea, and for all who have not seen my face in person,
51_2_2: that their hearts may be encouraged, being knit together in love, and attaining to all the riches of full assurance of understanding — the knowledge of God's mystery, which is Christ,
51_2_3: in whom are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
51_2_4: I say this so that no one will deceive you with arguments that sound reasonable.
51_2_5: For though I am absent in the flesh, I am with you in spirit, rejoicing to see your good order and the firmness of your faith in Christ.
51_2_6: As you therefore have received Christ Jesus the Lord, so continue to walk in Him,
51_2_7: rooted and built up in Him, established in the faith just as you were taught, and overflowing with gratitude.
51_2_8: See to it that no one takes you captive through philosophy and empty deceit, according to human tradition, according to the elementary principles of the world, and not according to Christ.
51_2_9: For in Him dwells all the fullness of the Godhead bodily,
51_2_10: and you have been filled in Him, who is the head over every ruler and authority.
51_2_11: In Him you were also circumcised — not with a circumcision made by hands, but by putting off the body of the flesh, in the spiritual circumcision of Christ —
51_2_12: having been buried with Him in baptism, in which you were also raised with Him through faith in the powerful working of God, who raised Him from the dead.
51_2_13: And you, who were dead in your trespasses and in the uncircumcision of your flesh, He made alive together with Him, having forgiven us all our trespasses —
51_2_14: canceling the certificate of debt that stood against us with its legal demands. He has taken it away, nailing it to the cross.
51_2_15: Having disarmed the rulers and authorities, He made a public spectacle of them, triumphing over them in it.
51_2_16: So let no one pass judgment on you in questions of food or drink, or with regard to a festival or a new moon or a Sabbath day —
51_2_17: these are a shadow of things to come; but the substance belongs to Christ.
51_2_18: Let no one disqualify you, insisting on ascetic practices and the worship of angels, going into detail about visions he has seen, inflated without cause by his unspiritual mind,
51_2_19: and not holding fast to the Head, from whom the whole body — nourished and held together by its joints and ligaments — grows with growth that is from God.
51_2_20: If you died with Christ to the elementary principles of the world, why do you live as if you still belonged to the world? Why do you submit to regulations:
51_2_21: "Do not touch, do not taste, do not handle" —
51_2_22: all of which refer to things destined to perish with use, according to the commandments and doctrines of men?
51_2_23: These things have an appearance of wisdom in self-imposed religion, false humility, and severe treatment of the body — but they are of no value in curbing the indulgence of the flesh.
51_3_1: If then you have been raised with Christ, seek those things which are above, where Christ is, seated at the right hand of God.
51_3_2: Set your minds on things above, not on earthly things.
51_3_3: For you died, and your life is hidden with Christ in God.
51_3_4: When Christ, who is your life, appears, then you also will appear with Him in glory.
51_3_5: Therefore put to death what is earthly in you: sexual immorality, moral impurity, lust, evil desire, and greed, which is idolatry.
51_3_6: Because of these things, the wrath of God is coming on those who are disobedient.
51_3_7: In these you yourselves once walked when you were living in them.
51_3_8: But now you must put away all these: anger, wrath, malice, slander, and filthy language from your mouth.
51_3_9: Do not lie to one another, since you have put off the old self with its evil practices,
51_3_10: and have put on the new self, which is being renewed in knowledge according to the image of its Creator.
51_3_11: In this new life there is no Greek or Jew, circumcised or uncircumcised, barbarian, Scythian, slave or free — but Christ is all and in all.
51_3_12: Therefore, as God's chosen ones, holy and dearly loved, put on compassionate hearts, kindness, humility, gentleness, and patience,
51_3_13: bearing with one another and forgiving one another — if anyone has a grievance against another, just as the Lord has forgiven you, so you also must forgive.
51_3_14: And above all these things put on love, which is the perfect bond of unity.
51_3_15: And let the peace of Christ rule in your hearts, to which indeed you were called in one body; and be thankful.
51_3_16: Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly, in all wisdom teaching and admonishing one another with psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, singing with gratitude in your hearts to God.
51_3_17: And whatever you do in word or deed, do everything in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God the Father through Him.
51_3_18: Wives, submit to your own husbands, as is fitting in the Lord.
51_3_19: Husbands, love your wives and do not be harsh or bitter toward them.
51_3_20: Children, obey your parents in everything, for this is well pleasing to the Lord.
51_3_21: Fathers, do not exasperate your children, so that they will not become discouraged.
51_3_22: Bondservants, obey your earthly masters in everything — not with eye-service, as people-pleasers, but with sincerity of heart, fearing the Lord.
51_3_23: Whatever you do, do it from the heart, as for the Lord and not for people,
51_3_24: knowing that from the Lord you will receive the inheritance as your reward. You serve the Lord Christ.
51_3_25: For the wrongdoer will be repaid for the wrong he has done, and there is no partiality.
51_4_1: Masters, treat your bondservants with justice and fairness, knowing that you also have a Master in heaven.
51_4_2: Devote yourselves to prayer, staying alert in it with thanksgiving;
51_4_3: and at the same time, pray also for us, that God would open to us a door for the word, to speak the mystery of Christ — for which I am also in chains —
51_4_4: that I may make it known as clearly as I ought to speak.
51_4_5: Walk in wisdom toward those who are outside, making the most of every opportunity.
51_4_6: Let your speech always be gracious, seasoned with salt, so that you may know how you ought to answer each person.
51_4_7: Tychicus, our dearly loved brother, faithful minister, and fellow servant in the Lord, will tell you all the news about me.
51_4_8: I have sent him to you for this very purpose — so that you may know how we are and that he may encourage your hearts,
51_4_9: with Onesimus, our faithful and beloved brother, who is one of you. They will inform you about everything happening here.
51_4_10: Aristarchus my fellow prisoner greets you, as does Mark the cousin of Barnabas — about whom you received instructions: if he comes to you, welcome him —
51_4_11: and Jesus who is called Justus. These are my only fellow workers for the kingdom of God who are from the circumcision; they have proved to be a comfort and encouragement to me.
51_4_12: Epaphras, who is one of you, a servant of Christ Jesus, greets you, always wrestling earnestly for you in his prayers, that you may stand mature and fully assured in all the will of God.
51_4_13: For I bear him witness that he has deep concern and works hard for you, and for those in Laodicea, and those in Hierapolis.
51_4_14: Luke the beloved physician and Demas greet you.
51_4_15: Greet the brothers and sisters in Laodicea, and Nympha and the church that meets in her home.
51_4_16: Now when this letter is read among you, see that it is also read in the church of the Laodiceans; and you likewise read the letter from Laodicea.
51_4_17: And say to Archippus, "Pay attention to the ministry you have received in the Lord, so that you may fulfill it."
51_4_18: This greeting is by my own hand — Paul. Remember my chains. Grace be with you. Amen.
46_1_1: Paul, called as an apostle of Christ Jesus through the will of God, and Sosthenes our brother,
46_1_2: To the church of God which is at Corinth, to those who have been sanctified in Christ Jesus, called as saints, with all who in every place call on the name of Jesus Christ our Lord, both theirs and ours:
46_1_3: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
46_1_4: I thank my God always concerning you for the grace of God which was given to you in Christ Jesus,
46_1_5: that in every way you were enriched in Him, in all speech and all knowledge —
46_1_6: even as the testimony about Christ was confirmed among you —
46_1_7: so that you are not lacking in any spiritual gift, eagerly waiting for the revelation of our Lord Jesus Christ,
46_1_8: who will also strengthen you to the end, so that you may be blameless in the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.
46_1_9: God is faithful, through whom you were called into fellowship with His Son, Jesus Christ our Lord.
46_1_10: Now I appeal to you, brethren, by the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that you all speak the same thing, and that there be no divisions among you, but that you be united in the same mind and in the same judgment.
46_1_11: For it has been reported to me concerning you, my brethren, by those of Chloe's household, that there are quarrels among you.
46_1_12: Now I mean this, that each of you says, 'I follow Paul,' or 'I follow Apollos,' or 'I follow Cephas,' or 'I follow Christ.'
46_1_13: Is Christ divided? Was Paul crucified for you? Or were you baptized in the name of Paul?
46_1_14: I thank God that I baptized none of you except Crispus and Gaius,
46_1_15: so that no one may say that you were baptized in my name.
46_1_16: Yes, I also baptized the household of Stephanas. Beyond that, I do not know whether I baptized anyone else.
46_1_17: For Christ did not send me to baptize, but to preach the gospel — not with eloquent wisdom of words, lest the cross of Christ be emptied of its power.
46_1_18: For the message of the cross is foolishness to those who are perishing, but to us who are being saved it is the power of God.
46_1_19: For it is written: 'I will destroy the wisdom of the wise, and the intelligence of the intelligent I will set aside.'
46_1_20: Where is the one who is wise? Where is the scribe? Where is the debater of this age? Has not God made foolish the wisdom of this world?
46_1_21: For since, in the wisdom of God, the world through its wisdom did not know God, it pleased God through the foolishness of what is preached to save those who believe.
46_1_22: For Jews ask for signs and Greeks seek wisdom,
46_1_23: but we preach Christ crucified, to the Jews a stumbling block and to the Gentiles foolishness,
46_1_24: but to those who are called, both Jews and Greeks, Christ the power of God and the wisdom of God.
46_1_25: Because the foolishness of God is wiser than men, and the weakness of God is stronger than men.
46_1_26: For consider your calling, brethren — not many were wise according to the flesh, not many powerful, not many of noble birth, are called.
46_1_27: But God has chosen the foolish things of the world to shame the wise, and God has chosen the weak things of the world to shame the strong;
46_1_28: and the lowly and despised things of the world, even the things that are not, God has chosen to bring to nothing the things that are,
46_1_29: so that no one may boast in the presence of God.
46_1_30: But by His doing you are in Christ Jesus, who became for us wisdom from God — our righteousness and sanctification and redemption —
46_1_31: that, as it is written, 'Let the one who boasts, boast in the Lord.'
46_2_1: And I, brethren, when I came to you, did not come with lofty speech or wisdom, proclaiming to you the testimony of God.
46_2_2: For I resolved to know nothing among you except Jesus Christ and Him crucified.
46_2_3: I was with you in weakness and in fear and in much trembling.
46_2_4: And my speech and my preaching were not with persuasive words of human wisdom, but in demonstration of the Spirit and of power,
46_2_5: that your faith should not rest in the wisdom of men, but in the power of God.
46_2_6: Yet we do speak wisdom among those who are mature — though it is not the wisdom of this age, nor of the rulers of this age who are passing away;
46_2_7: but we speak the wisdom of God in a mystery, the hidden wisdom which God ordained before the ages for our glory,
46_2_8: which none of the rulers of this age understood; for if they had understood it, they would not have crucified the Lord of glory.
46_2_9: But as it is written: 'Eye has not seen, nor ear heard, nor have entered into the heart of man the things which God has prepared for those who love Him.'
46_2_10: But God has revealed them to us through His Spirit; for the Spirit searches all things, even the deep things of God.
46_2_11: For what man knows the thoughts of a man except the spirit of that man that is within him? Even so no one knows the thoughts of God except the Spirit of God.
46_2_12: Now we have received, not the spirit of the world, but the Spirit who is from God, so that we might understand the things freely given to us by God.
46_2_13: These things we also speak, not in words taught by human wisdom, but in words taught by the Spirit, interpreting spiritual truths to those who are spiritual.
46_2_14: But the natural man does not receive the things of the Spirit of God, for they are foolishness to him; nor can he understand them, because they are spiritually discerned.
46_2_15: But he who is spiritual discerns all things, yet he himself is judged by no one.
46_2_16: 'For who has known the mind of the Lord that he may instruct Him?' But we have the mind of Christ.
46_3_1: And I, brethren, could not speak to you as to spiritual people but as to carnal — as to infants in Christ.
46_3_2: I fed you with milk and not with solid food; for until now you were not able to receive it, and even now you are still not able,
46_3_3: for you are still carnal. For where there are envy, strife, and divisions among you, are you not carnal and walking in a merely human way?
46_3_4: For when one says, 'I follow Paul,' and another, 'I follow Apollos,' are you not acting like mere men?
46_3_5: Who then is Paul, and who is Apollos, but servants through whom you believed, as the Lord assigned to each?
46_3_6: I planted, Apollos watered, but God gave the growth.
46_3_7: So then neither he who plants is anything, nor he who waters, but God who gives the growth.
46_3_8: Now he who plants and he who waters are one, and each will receive his own reward according to his own labor.
46_3_9: For we are God's fellow workers; you are God's field, God's building.
46_3_10: According to the grace of God given to me, like a skilled master builder I have laid the foundation, and another builds on it. But let each one be careful how he builds on it.
46_3_11: For no other foundation can anyone lay than that which is laid, which is Jesus Christ.
46_3_12: Now if anyone builds on this foundation with gold, silver, precious stones, wood, hay, or straw,
46_3_13: each one's work will become evident, for the Day will declare it, because it will be revealed by fire; and the fire will test each one's work, of what sort it is.
46_3_14: If anyone's work which he has built on it endures, he will receive a reward.
46_3_15: If anyone's work is burned up, he will suffer loss, though he himself will be saved, but only as through fire.
46_3_16: Do you not know that you are the temple of God and that the Spirit of God dwells in you?
46_3_17: If anyone destroys the temple of God, God will destroy him. For the temple of God is holy, and you are that temple.
46_3_18: Let no one deceive himself. If anyone among you thinks he is wise in this age, let him become a fool so that he may become truly wise.
46_3_19: For the wisdom of this world is foolishness with God. For it is written, 'He catches the wise in their own craftiness;'
46_3_20: and again, 'The Lord knows the thoughts of the wise, that they are futile.'
46_3_21: Therefore let no one boast in men. For all things are yours:
46_3_22: whether Paul or Apollos or Cephas or the world or life or death or things present or things to come — all are yours,
46_3_23: and you are Christ's, and Christ is God's.
46_4_1: This is how one should regard us — as servants of Christ and stewards of the mysteries of God.
46_4_2: Moreover, it is required of stewards that one be found faithful.
46_4_3: But with me it is a very small thing that I should be judged by you or by any human court; in fact, I do not even judge myself.
46_4_4: For I am not aware of anything against myself, yet I am not thereby acquitted. It is the Lord who judges me.
46_4_5: Therefore judge nothing before the appointed time; wait until the Lord comes, who will both bring to light the things hidden in darkness and disclose the motives of the heart. Then each one will receive his commendation from God.
46_4_6: Now these things, brethren, I have applied figuratively to myself and Apollos for your sakes, so that in us you may learn not to go beyond what is written, that none of you may become arrogant in favor of one against another.
46_4_7: For who sees anything different in you? What do you have that you did not receive? And if you received it, why do you boast as if you did not receive it?
46_4_8: Already you have all you want! Already you have become rich! Without us you have become kings — and I wish that you did reign, so that we also might reign with you!
46_4_9: For I think that God has put us apostles on display last of all, as men condemned to death; for we have become a spectacle to the world, to angels, and to men.
46_4_10: We are fools for Christ's sake, but you are wise in Christ! We are weak, but you are strong! You are held in honor, but we are in disrepute!
46_4_11: Even to this present hour we hunger and thirst and are poorly clothed and are roughly treated and are homeless,
46_4_12: and we labor, working with our own hands. When reviled, we bless; when persecuted, we endure;
46_4_13: when slandered, we offer a kind word. We have become as the scum of the world, the refuse of all things, even until now.
46_4_14: I do not write these things to shame you, but as my beloved children I am warning you.
46_4_15: For if you were to have countless instructors in Christ, yet you would not have many fathers; for in Christ Jesus I became your father through the gospel.
46_4_16: Therefore I urge you, imitate me.
46_4_17: For this reason I have sent Timothy to you, who is my beloved and faithful child in the Lord, who will remind you of my ways in Christ, just as I teach them everywhere in every church.
46_4_18: Now some are arrogant, as though I were not coming to you.
46_4_19: But I will come to you quickly, if the Lord wills, and I will find out not the talk of those who are arrogant but their power.
46_4_20: For the kingdom of God does not consist in talk but in power.
46_4_21: What do you wish? Shall I come to you with a rod of discipline, or with love and a spirit of gentleness?
46_5_1: It is actually reported that there is sexual immorality among you, and of a kind that is not even found among the Gentiles — that a man has his father's wife.
46_5_2: And you are arrogant! Should you not rather have mourned, so that the one who has done this would be removed from among you?
46_5_3: For I, though absent in body but present in spirit, have already judged, as though I were present, the one who has done such a thing.
46_5_4: In the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, when you are gathered together with my spirit and the power of our Lord Jesus Christ,
46_5_5: you are to deliver such a one to Satan for the destruction of the flesh, so that his spirit may be saved in the day of the Lord Jesus.
46_5_6: Your boasting is not good. Do you not know that a little leaven leavens the whole lump of dough?
46_5_7: Cleanse out the old leaven, that you may be a fresh unleavened batch, as you truly are. For Christ, our Passover lamb, has been sacrificed.
46_5_8: Therefore let us celebrate the feast, not with the old leaven, the leaven of malice and evil, but with the unleavened bread of sincerity and truth.
46_5_9: I wrote to you in my previous letter not to associate with sexually immoral people —
46_5_10: not at all meaning the sexually immoral of this world, or the greedy and swindlers, or idolaters, since then you would need to go out of the world.
46_5_11: But now I am writing to you not to associate with anyone who bears the name of brother if he is sexually immoral or greedy, or is an idolater, reviler, drunkard, or swindler — not even to eat with such a person.
46_5_12: For what have I to do with judging those outside? Is it not those inside the church whom you are to judge?
46_5_13: God judges those outside. Purge the evil person from among you.
46_6_1: Dare any of you, having a matter against another, go to law before the unrighteous, and not before the saints?
46_6_2: Do you not know that the saints will judge the world? And if the world is to be judged by you, are you incompetent to handle the smallest cases?
46_6_3: Do you not know that we will judge angels? How much more, then, matters pertaining to this life!
46_6_4: If then you have cases involving matters of this life, do you appoint as judges those who are least esteemed by the church?
46_6_5: I say this to your shame. Is there not even one wise man among you who is able to settle a dispute between his brothers?
46_6_6: But brother goes to law against brother, and that before unbelievers!
46_6_7: Actually, then, it is already a defeat for you that you have lawsuits against one another. Why not rather be wronged? Why not rather be defrauded?
46_6_8: On the contrary, you yourselves do wrong and defraud — and that against your own brothers!
46_6_9: Do you not know that the unrighteous will not inherit the kingdom of God? Do not be deceived: neither the sexually immoral, nor idolaters, nor adulterers, nor men who practice homosexuality,
46_6_10: nor thieves, nor the greedy, nor drunkards, nor revilers, nor swindlers will inherit the kingdom of God.
46_6_11: And such were some of you. But you were washed, you were sanctified, you were justified in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and by the Spirit of our God.
46_6_12: 'All things are lawful for me,' but not all things are beneficial. 'All things are lawful for me,' but I will not be mastered by anything.
46_6_13: 'Food is for the stomach and the stomach for food,' but God will do away with both. The body is not meant for sexual immorality but for the Lord, and the Lord for the body.
46_6_14: And God both raised up the Lord and will also raise us up by His power.
46_6_15: Do you not know that your bodies are members of Christ? Shall I then take the members of Christ and make them members of a prostitute? Certainly not!
46_6_16: Or do you not know that he who is joined to a prostitute becomes one body with her? For, as it is written, 'The two will become one flesh.'
46_6_17: But he who is joined to the Lord becomes one spirit with Him.
46_6_18: Flee sexual immorality! Every other sin a person commits is outside the body, but the sexually immoral person sins against his own body.
46_6_19: Or do you not know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit within you, whom you have from God? You are not your own,
46_6_20: for you were bought with a price. So glorify God in your body.
46_7_1: Now concerning the things about which you wrote: it is good for a man not to have sexual relations with a woman.
46_7_2: But because of the temptation to sexual immorality, each man should have his own wife, and each woman her own husband.
46_7_3: The husband should give to his wife her conjugal rights, and likewise the wife to her husband.
46_7_4: For the wife does not have authority over her own body, but the husband does. Likewise, the husband does not have authority over his own body, but the wife does.
46_7_5: Do not deprive one another, except perhaps by agreement for a limited time, that you may devote yourselves to prayer; and then come together again, so that Satan may not tempt you because of your lack of self-control.
46_7_6: But I say this as a concession, not as a command.
46_7_7: I wish that all were as I myself am. But each has his own gift from God, one of one kind and one of another.
46_7_8: To the unmarried and the widows I say: it is good for them if they remain as I am.
46_7_9: But if they cannot exercise self-control, they should marry. For it is better to marry than to burn with passion.
46_7_10: To the married I give this charge — not I, but the Lord: a wife should not separate from her husband
46_7_11: (but if she does separate, let her remain unmarried or else be reconciled to her husband), and a husband should not divorce his wife.
46_7_12: To the rest I say (I, not the Lord): if any brother has a wife who is an unbeliever, and she consents to live with him, he should not divorce her.
46_7_13: And if any woman has an unbelieving husband, and he consents to live with her, she should not divorce him.
46_7_14: For the unbelieving husband is made holy through his wife, and the unbelieving wife is made holy through her husband. Otherwise your children would be unclean, but as it is, they are holy.
46_7_15: But if the unbelieving partner separates, let it be so. In such cases the brother or sister is not bound — God has called you to peace.
46_7_16: For how do you know, wife, whether you will save your husband? Or how do you know, husband, whether you will save your wife?
46_7_17: But as God has distributed to each one, as the Lord has called each one, so let him walk. And so I direct in all the churches.
46_7_18: Was anyone already circumcised when he was called? Let him not seek to remove the marks. Was anyone called while uncircumcised? Let him not seek circumcision.
46_7_19: Circumcision is nothing, and uncircumcision is nothing, but what matters is keeping the commandments of God.
46_7_20: Let each one remain in the calling in which he was called.
46_7_21: Were you called while a slave? Do not be concerned about it. But if you can gain your freedom, avail yourself of the opportunity.
46_7_22: For he who was called in the Lord while a slave is the Lord's freedman. Likewise he who was called while free is Christ's slave.
46_7_23: You were bought with a price; do not become slaves of men.
46_7_24: Brothers, let each one remain with God in the condition in which he was called.
46_7_25: Now concerning virgins: I have no command from the Lord, but I give my judgment as one who by the Lord's mercy is trustworthy.
46_7_26: I think that in view of the present distress it is good for a person to remain as he is.
46_7_27: Are you bound to a wife? Do not seek to be released. Are you free from a wife? Do not seek a wife.
46_7_28: But if you do marry, you have not sinned. And if a virgin marries, she has not sinned. Yet those who marry will face worldly troubles, and I am trying to spare you that.
46_7_29: This is what I mean, brothers: the appointed time has grown very short. From now on, let those who have wives live as though they had none,
46_7_30: and those who mourn as though they were not mourning, and those who rejoice as though they were not rejoicing, and those who buy as though they had no goods,
46_7_31: and those who deal with the world as though they had no dealings with it. For the present form of this world is passing away.
46_7_32: I want you to be free from anxieties. The unmarried man is concerned about the things of the Lord — how he may please the Lord.
46_7_33: But the married man is concerned about the things of the world — how he may please his wife —
46_7_34: and his interests are divided. And the unmarried woman or the virgin is concerned about the things of the Lord, how to be holy in both body and spirit. But the married woman is concerned about the things of the world — how she may please her husband.
46_7_35: I say this for your own benefit, not to put a restraint on you, but to promote what is appropriate and secure undivided devotion to the Lord.
46_7_36: But if anyone thinks he is behaving improperly toward his virgin, if his passions are strong, and it has to be so, let him do what he wishes; he does not sin — let them marry.
46_7_37: But he who stands firm in his heart, having no necessity but having control over his own will, and has decided in his heart to keep his virgin, he does well.
46_7_38: So then he who gives his virgin in marriage does well, but he who does not give her in marriage does even better.
46_7_39: A wife is bound to her husband as long as he lives. But if her husband dies, she is free to marry whom she wishes, only in the Lord.
46_7_40: But she is happier if she remains as she is, in my judgment. And I think that I also have the Spirit of God.
46_8_1: Now concerning food offered to idols: we know that 'we all have knowledge.' This knowledge puffs up, but love builds up.
46_8_2: If anyone imagines he knows something, he does not yet know as he ought to know.
46_8_3: But if anyone loves God, he is known by God.
46_8_4: Therefore, as to the eating of food offered to idols, we know that 'an idol has no real existence in the world' and that 'there is no God but one.'
46_8_5: For even if there are so-called gods, whether in heaven or on earth — as indeed there are many 'gods' and many 'lords' —
46_8_6: yet for us there is one God, the Father, from whom all things came and for whom we exist, and one Lord, Jesus Christ, through whom all things were made and through whom we exist.
46_8_7: But this knowledge is not in everyone; for some, being accustomed to idols until now, eat food as if it were offered to an idol, and their conscience, being weak, is defiled.
46_8_8: Food will not commend us to God. We are no worse off if we do not eat, and no better off if we do.
46_8_9: But take care that this right of yours does not somehow become a stumbling block to the weak.
46_8_10: For if anyone sees you, who have knowledge, eating in an idol's temple, will not his conscience, if he is weak, be emboldened to eat food offered to idols?
46_8_11: And so by your knowledge this weak brother, for whom Christ died, is destroyed.
46_8_12: But when you sin against your brothers and wound their weak conscience in this way, you sin against Christ.
46_8_13: Therefore, if food causes my brother to stumble, I will never eat meat again, so that I will not cause my brother to fall.
46_9_1: Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Have I not seen Jesus our Lord? Are you not my work in the Lord?
46_9_2: If to others I am not an apostle, at least I am to you; for you are the seal of my apostleship in the Lord.
46_9_3: This is my defense to those who would examine me.
46_9_4: Do we not have the right to eat and drink?
46_9_5: Do we not have the right to take along a believing wife, as do the other apostles and the brothers of the Lord and Cephas?
46_9_6: Or is it only Barnabas and I who have no right to refrain from working for a living?
46_9_7: Who serves as a soldier at his own expense? Who plants a vineyard and does not eat its fruit? Who tends a flock and does not drink of its milk?
46_9_8: Do I say these things merely on human authority? Does not the Law say the same?
46_9_9: For it is written in the Law of Moses: 'You shall not muzzle an ox when it treads out the grain.' Is God's concern only for oxen?
46_9_10: Surely He says this for our sake, doesn't He? It was written for our sake, because the plowman should plow in hope and the thresher thresh in hope of sharing in the harvest.
46_9_11: If we have sown spiritual seed among you, is it too much if we reap a material harvest from you?
46_9_12: If others share this rightful claim on you, do we not even more? Nevertheless, we have not made use of this right, but we endure anything rather than put an obstacle in the way of the gospel of Christ.
46_9_13: Do you not know that those who work in the temple get their food from the temple, and those who serve at the altar share in what is offered at the altar?
46_9_14: In the same way, the Lord commanded that those who proclaim the gospel should get their living by the gospel.
46_9_15: But I have made no use of any of these rights, nor am I writing these things so that it will be done so in my case. For I would rather die than have anyone deprive me of my reason to boast.
46_9_16: For if I preach the gospel, that gives me no ground for boasting. For necessity is laid upon me. Woe to me if I do not preach the gospel!
46_9_17: For if I do this of my own will, I have a reward; but if not of my own will, I am still entrusted with a stewardship.
46_9_18: What then is my reward? That in my preaching I may present the gospel free of charge, so as not to make full use of my rights in the gospel.
46_9_19: For though I am free from all, I have made myself a servant to all, that I might win more of them.
46_9_20: To the Jews I became as a Jew, in order to win Jews. To those under the law I became as one under the law — though not being myself under the law — that I might win those under the law.
46_9_21: To those outside the law I became as one outside the law — not being outside the law of God but under the law of Christ — that I might win those outside the law.
46_9_22: To the weak I became weak, that I might win the weak. I have become all things to all people, that by all means I might save some.
46_9_23: And I do it all for the sake of the gospel, that I may share in its blessings.
46_9_24: Do you not know that in a race all the runners run, but only one receives the prize? So run that you may win it.
46_9_25: Every athlete exercises self-control in all things. They do it to receive a perishable crown, but we an imperishable one.
46_9_26: So I do not run aimlessly; I do not box like one beating the air.
46_9_27: But I discipline my body and keep it under control, lest after preaching to others I myself should be disqualified.
46_10_1: For I do not want you to be unaware, brothers, that our fathers were all under the cloud, and all passed through the sea,
46_10_2: and all were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea,
46_10_3: and all ate the same spiritual food,
46_10_4: and all drank the same spiritual drink. For they drank from the spiritual Rock that followed them, and the Rock was Christ.
46_10_5: Nevertheless, with most of them God was not pleased, for their bodies were scattered in the wilderness.
46_10_6: Now these things took place as examples for us, so that we might not desire evil as they did.
46_10_7: Do not be idolaters as some of them were; as it is written, 'The people sat down to eat and drink and rose up to play.'
46_10_8: We must not indulge in sexual immorality as some of them did, and twenty-three thousand fell in a single day.
46_10_9: We must not put Christ to the test, as some of them did and were destroyed by serpents,
46_10_10: nor grumble, as some of them did and were destroyed by the Destroyer.
46_10_11: Now these things happened to them as examples, and they were written down for our instruction, on whom the ends of the ages have come.
46_10_12: Therefore let anyone who thinks he stands take heed lest he fall.
46_10_13: No temptation has overtaken you that is not common to man. God is faithful, and He will not let you be tempted beyond what you are able to bear, but with the temptation He will also provide the way of escape, that you may be able to endure it.
46_10_14: Therefore, my beloved, flee from idolatry.
46_10_15: I speak as to wise people; judge for yourselves what I say.
46_10_16: The cup of blessing that we bless — is it not a participation in the blood of Christ? The bread that we break — is it not a participation in the body of Christ?
46_10_17: Because there is one bread, we who are many are one body, for we all partake of the one bread.
46_10_18: Consider the people of Israel: are not those who eat the sacrifices participants in the altar?
46_10_19: What do I imply then? That food offered to idols is anything, or that an idol is anything?
46_10_20: No, I imply that what the Gentiles sacrifice they offer to demons and not to God. I do not want you to be participants with demons.
46_10_21: You cannot drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons. You cannot partake of the table of the Lord and the table of demons.
46_10_22: Shall we provoke the Lord to jealousy? Are we stronger than He?
46_10_23: 'All things are lawful,' but not all things are beneficial. 'All things are lawful,' but not all things build up.
46_10_24: Let no one seek his own good, but the good of his neighbor.
46_10_25: Eat whatever is sold in the meat market without raising any question of conscience,
46_10_26: for 'the earth is the Lord's, and everything in it.'
46_10_27: If one of the unbelievers invites you to dinner and you want to go, eat whatever is set before you without raising any question of conscience.
46_10_28: But if someone says to you, 'This has been offered to idols,' do not eat it, for the sake of the one who informed you, and for the sake of conscience —
46_10_29: I mean his conscience, not yours. For why should my freedom be judged by another's conscience?
46_10_30: If I partake with thankfulness, why am I spoken evil of for something for which I give thanks?
46_10_31: So, whether you eat or drink, or whatever you do, do all to the glory of God.
46_10_32: Give no offense to Jews or to Greeks or to the church of God,
46_10_33: just as I try to please everyone in everything I do, not seeking my own advantage but that of the many, so that they may be saved.
46_11_1: Be imitators of me, just as I also am of Christ.
46_11_2: Now I praise you because you remember me in everything and hold firmly to the traditions just as I delivered them to you.
46_11_3: But I want you to know that the head of every man is Christ, the head of a woman is her husband, and the head of Christ is God.
46_11_4: Every man who prays or prophesies with his head covered dishonors his head,
46_11_5: but every woman who prays or prophesies with her head uncovered dishonors her head — it is one and the same as if her head were shaved.
46_11_6: For if a woman will not cover her head, she should cut off her hair. But since it is disgraceful for a woman to cut off her hair or shave her head, she should cover her head.
46_11_7: For a man ought not to cover his head, since he is the image and glory of God, but woman is the glory of man.
46_11_8: For man was not made from woman, but woman from man.
46_11_9: Neither was man created for woman, but woman for man.
46_11_10: That is why a wife ought to have a symbol of authority on her head, because of the angels.
46_11_11: Nevertheless, in the Lord woman is not independent of man nor man independent of woman.
46_11_12: For as woman came from man, so also man is born of woman. And all things are from God.
46_11_13: Judge for yourselves: is it proper for a wife to pray to God with her head uncovered?
46_11_14: Does not nature itself teach you that if a man wears long hair, it is a disgrace for him,
46_11_15: but if a woman has long hair, it is her glory? For her hair is given to her as a covering.
46_11_16: But if anyone is inclined to be contentious, we have no other practice, nor do the churches of God.
46_11_17: But in giving the following instructions I do not praise you, because when you come together it is not for the better but for the worse.
46_11_18: For, to begin with, when you come together as a church, I hear that there are divisions among you, and I partly believe it;
46_11_19: for there must also be factions among you in order that those who are genuine may be recognized among you.
46_11_20: When you come together therefore, it is not the Lord's Supper you eat.
46_11_21: For in eating, each one goes ahead with his own meal. One goes hungry, another becomes drunk.
46_11_22: What! Do you not have houses to eat and drink in? Or do you show contempt for the church of God and humiliate those who have nothing? What shall I say to you? Shall I praise you in this? No, I will not.
46_11_23: For I received from the Lord what I also delivered to you: that the Lord Jesus on the night He was betrayed took bread,
46_11_24: and when He had given thanks, He broke it and said, 'This is My body, which is for you. Do this in remembrance of Me.'
46_11_25: In the same way He also took the cup after supper, saying, 'This cup is the new covenant in My blood. Do this, as often as you drink it, in remembrance of Me.'
46_11_26: For as often as you eat this bread and drink this cup, you proclaim the Lord's death until He comes.
46_11_27: Therefore whoever eats this bread or drinks this cup of the Lord in an unworthy manner will be guilty of sinning against the body and blood of the Lord.
46_11_28: Let a person examine himself, then, and so eat of the bread and drink of the cup.
46_11_29: For anyone who eats and drinks without discerning the body eats and drinks judgment on himself.
46_11_30: That is why many of you are weak and ill, and a number have died.
46_11_31: But if we examined ourselves rightly, we would not be judged.
46_11_32: But when we are judged by the Lord, we are being disciplined so that we may not be condemned along with the world.
46_11_33: So then, my brothers, when you come together to eat, wait for one another —
46_11_34: if anyone is hungry, let him eat at home, so that when you come together it will not be for judgment. About the other things I will give directions when I come.
46_12_1: Now concerning spiritual gifts, brothers, I do not want you to be uninformed.
46_12_2: You know that when you were pagans you were led astray to mute idols, however you were led.
46_12_3: Therefore I want you to understand that no one speaking in the Spirit of God ever says 'Jesus is accursed,' and no one can say 'Jesus is Lord' except by the Holy Spirit.
46_12_4: Now there are varieties of gifts, but the same Spirit;
46_12_5: and there are varieties of service, but the same Lord;
46_12_6: and there are varieties of activities, but it is the same God who empowers them all in everyone.
46_12_7: To each one is given the manifestation of the Spirit for the common good.
46_12_8: For to one is given through the Spirit the word of wisdom, and to another the word of knowledge according to the same Spirit,
46_12_9: to another faith by the same Spirit, to another gifts of healing by the one Spirit,
46_12_10: to another the working of miracles, to another prophecy, to another the ability to distinguish between spirits, to another various kinds of tongues, to another the interpretation of tongues.
46_12_11: All these are empowered by one and the same Spirit, who distributes to each one individually as He wills.
46_12_12: For just as the body is one and has many members, and all the members of the body, though many, are one body, so it is with Christ.
46_12_13: For in one Spirit we were all baptized into one body — Jews or Greeks, slaves or free — and all were made to drink of one Spirit.
46_12_14: For the body does not consist of one member but of many.
46_12_15: If the foot should say, 'Because I am not a hand, I do not belong to the body,' that would not make it any less a part of the body.
46_12_16: And if the ear should say, 'Because I am not an eye, I do not belong to the body,' that would not make it any less a part of the body.
46_12_17: If the whole body were an eye, where would the sense of hearing be? If the whole body were an ear, where would the sense of smell be?
46_12_18: But as it is, God has arranged the members of the body, each one of them, just as He desired.
46_12_19: If all were a single member, where would the body be?
46_12_20: As it is, there are many parts, yet one body.
46_12_21: The eye cannot say to the hand, 'I have no need of you,' nor again the head to the feet, 'I have no need of you.'
46_12_22: On the contrary, the parts of the body that seem to be weaker are indispensable,
46_12_23: and on those parts of the body that we think less honorable we bestow the greater honor, and our unpresentable parts are treated with greater modesty,
46_12_24: which our more presentable parts do not require. But God has so composed the body, giving greater honor to the part that lacked it,
46_12_25: that there may be no division in the body, but that the members may have the same care for one another.
46_12_26: If one member suffers, all suffer together; if one member is honored, all rejoice together.
46_12_27: Now you are the body of Christ and individually members of it.
46_12_28: And God has appointed in the church: first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracles, then gifts of healing, helps, administration, and various kinds of tongues.
46_12_29: Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Do all work miracles?
46_12_30: Do all have gifts of healing? Do all speak with tongues? Do all interpret?
46_12_31: But earnestly desire the greater gifts. And I will show you a still more excellent way.
46_13_1: If I speak in the tongues of men and of angels, but have not love, I am a noisy gong or a clanging cymbal.
46_13_2: And if I have prophetic powers, and understand all mysteries and all knowledge, and if I have all faith so as to move mountains, but have not love, I am nothing.
46_13_3: If I give away all I have, and if I deliver up my body to be burned, but have not love, I gain nothing.
46_13_4: Love is patient and kind; love does not envy or boast; it is not arrogant
46_13_5: or rude. It does not insist on its own way; it is not irritable or resentful;
46_13_6: it does not rejoice in wrongdoing, but rejoices with the truth.
46_13_7: Love bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.
46_13_8: Love never fails. But as for prophecies, they will pass away; as for tongues, they will cease; as for knowledge, it will pass away.
46_13_9: For we know in part and we prophesy in part,
46_13_10: but when the perfect comes, the partial will pass away.
46_13_11: When I was a child, I spoke like a child, I thought like a child, I reasoned like a child. When I became a man, I gave up childish ways.
46_13_12: For now we see in a mirror dimly, but then face to face. Now I know in part; then I shall know fully, even as I have been fully known.
46_13_13: So now faith, hope, and love abide — these three; but the greatest of these is love.
46_14_1: Pursue love, and earnestly desire spiritual gifts, especially that you may prophesy.
46_14_2: For one who speaks in a tongue speaks not to men but to God; for no one understands him, but he utters mysteries in the Spirit.
46_14_3: But the one who prophesies speaks to people for their upbuilding and encouragement and consolation.
46_14_4: The one who speaks in a tongue builds up himself, but the one who prophesies builds up the church.
46_14_5: Now I want you all to speak in tongues, but even more to prophesy. The one who prophesies is greater than the one who speaks in tongues, unless someone interprets, so that the church may be built up.
46_14_6: Now, brothers, if I come to you speaking in tongues, how will I benefit you unless I bring you some revelation or knowledge or prophecy or teaching?
46_14_7: If even lifeless instruments — such as the flute or the harp — do not give distinct notes, how will anyone know what is played?
46_14_8: And if the bugle gives an indistinct sound, who will get ready for battle?
46_14_9: So with yourselves: if you utter speech that is not intelligible, how will anyone know what is said? For you will just be speaking into the air.
46_14_10: There are doubtless many different languages in the world, and none is without meaning,
46_14_11: but if I do not know the meaning of the language, I will be a foreigner to the speaker and the speaker a foreigner to me.
46_14_12: So with yourselves: since you are eager for manifestations of the Spirit, strive to excel in building up the church.
46_14_13: Therefore, one who speaks in a tongue should pray that he may interpret.
46_14_14: For if I pray in a tongue, my spirit prays but my mind is unfruitful.
46_14_15: What am I to do? I will pray with my spirit, but I will pray with my mind also; I will sing praise with my spirit, but I will sing with my mind also.
46_14_16: Otherwise, if you give thanks with your spirit, how can anyone in the position of an outsider say 'Amen' to your thanksgiving when he does not know what you are saying?
46_14_17: For you may be giving thanks well enough, but the other person is not being built up.
46_14_18: I thank God that I speak in tongues more than all of you.
46_14_19: Nevertheless, in church I would rather speak five words with my understanding in order to instruct others, than ten thousand words in a tongue.
46_14_20: Brothers, do not be children in your thinking. Be infants in evil, but in your thinking be mature.
46_14_21: In the Law it is written, 'By people of strange tongues and by the lips of foreigners will I speak to this people, and even then they will not listen to Me, says the Lord.'
46_14_22: Thus tongues are a sign not for believers but for unbelievers, while prophecy is a sign not for unbelievers but for believers.
46_14_23: If, therefore, the whole church comes together and all speak in tongues, and outsiders or unbelievers enter, will they not say that you are out of your minds?
46_14_24: But if all prophesy, and an unbeliever or outsider enters, he is convicted by all, he is called to account by all,
46_14_25: the secrets of his heart are disclosed, and so, falling on his face, he will worship God and declare that God is truly among you.
46_14_26: What then, brothers? When you come together, each one has a hymn, a lesson, a revelation, a tongue, or an interpretation. Let all things be done for building up.
46_14_27: If any speak in a tongue, let there be only two or at most three, and each in turn, and let someone interpret.
46_14_28: But if there is no one to interpret, let each of them keep silent in church and speak to himself and to God.
46_14_29: Let two or three prophets speak, and let the others weigh what is said.
46_14_30: If a revelation is made to another who is seated, let the first be silent.
46_14_31: For you can all prophesy one by one, so that all may learn and all be encouraged,
46_14_32: and the spirits of prophets are subject to prophets.
46_14_33: For God is not a God of confusion but of peace. As in all the churches of the saints,
46_14_34: the women should keep silent in the churches. For they are not permitted to speak, but should be in submission, as the Law also says.
46_14_35: If there is anything they desire to learn, let them ask their husbands at home. For it is shameful for a woman to speak in church.
46_14_36: Or was it from you that the word of God came? Or are you the only ones it has reached?
46_14_37: If anyone thinks that he is a prophet, or spiritual, he should acknowledge that the things I am writing to you are a command of the Lord.
46_14_38: If anyone does not recognize this, he is not recognized.
46_14_39: So, my brothers, earnestly desire to prophesy, and do not forbid speaking in tongues.
46_14_40: But all things should be done decently and in order.
46_15_1: Now I would remind you, brothers, of the gospel I preached to you, which you received, in which you stand,
46_15_2: and by which you are being saved, if you hold fast to the word I preached to you — unless you believed in vain.
46_15_3: For I delivered to you as of first importance what I also received: that Christ died for our sins in accordance with the Scriptures,
46_15_4: that He was buried, that He was raised on the third day in accordance with the Scriptures,
46_15_5: and that He appeared to Cephas, then to the twelve.
46_15_6: Then He appeared to more than five hundred brothers at one time, most of whom are still alive, though some have fallen asleep.
46_15_7: Then He appeared to James, then to all the apostles.
46_15_8: Last of all, as to one untimely born, He appeared also to me.
46_15_9: For I am the least of the apostles, unworthy to be called an apostle, because I persecuted the church of God.
46_15_10: But by the grace of God I am what I am, and His grace toward me was not without effect. On the contrary, I worked harder than any of them — though it was not I, but the grace of God that is with me.
46_15_11: Whether then it was I or they, so we preach and so you believed.
46_15_12: Now if Christ is proclaimed as raised from the dead, how can some of you say that there is no resurrection of the dead?
46_15_13: But if there is no resurrection of the dead, then not even Christ has been raised.
46_15_14: And if Christ has not been raised, then our preaching is empty and your faith is empty.
46_15_15: We are even found to be misrepresenting God, because we testified about God that He raised Christ — whom He did not raise if it is true that the dead are not raised.
46_15_16: For if the dead are not raised, not even Christ has been raised.
46_15_17: And if Christ has not been raised, your faith is futile and you are still in your sins.
46_15_18: Then those also who have fallen asleep in Christ have perished.
46_15_19: If in this life only we have hope in Christ, we are of all people most to be pitied.
46_15_20: But in fact Christ has been raised from the dead, the firstfruits of those who have fallen asleep.
46_15_21: For as by a man came death, by a man has come also the resurrection of the dead.
46_15_22: For as in Adam all die, so also in Christ shall all be made alive.
46_15_23: But each in his own order: Christ the firstfruits, then at His coming those who belong to Christ.
46_15_24: Then comes the end, when He delivers the kingdom to God the Father after destroying every rule and every authority and power.
46_15_25: For He must reign until He has put all His enemies under His feet.
46_15_26: The last enemy to be destroyed is death.
46_15_27: For 'God has put all things in subjection under His feet.' But when it says 'all things are put in subjection,' it is plain that He who put all things under Him is excepted.
46_15_28: When all things are subjected to Him, then the Son Himself will also be subjected to Him who put all things under Him, so that God may be all in all.
46_15_29: Otherwise, what do people mean by being baptized on behalf of the dead? If the dead are not raised at all, why are people baptized on their behalf?
46_15_30: Why are we in danger every hour?
46_15_31: I protest, brothers — as surely as I glory over you in Christ Jesus our Lord — I die every day!
46_15_32: What do I gain if, humanly speaking, I fought with wild beasts at Ephesus? If the dead are not raised, 'Let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we die.'
46_15_33: Do not be deceived: 'Bad company corrupts good character.'
46_15_34: Come to your senses, as you ought, and stop sinning. For some have no knowledge of God. I say this to your shame.
46_15_35: But someone will ask, 'How are the dead raised? With what kind of body do they come?'
46_15_36: You foolish person! What you sow does not come to life unless it dies.
46_15_37: And what you sow is not the body that is to be, but a bare kernel — perhaps of wheat or some other grain.
46_15_38: But God gives it a body as He has chosen, and to each kind of seed its own body.
46_15_39: For not all flesh is the same: there is one kind for humans, another for animals, another for birds, and another for fish.
46_15_40: There are heavenly bodies and earthly bodies, but the glory of the heavenly is one kind, and the glory of the earthly is another.
46_15_41: There is one glory of the sun, and another glory of the moon, and another glory of the stars; for star differs from star in glory.
46_15_42: So is it with the resurrection of the dead. What is sown is perishable; what is raised is imperishable.
46_15_43: It is sown in dishonor; it is raised in glory. It is sown in weakness; it is raised in power.
46_15_44: It is sown a natural body; it is raised a spiritual body. If there is a natural body, there is also a spiritual body.
46_15_45: Thus it is written, 'The first man Adam became a living being'; the last Adam became a life-giving spirit.
46_15_46: But it is not the spiritual that is first but the natural, and then the spiritual.
46_15_47: The first man was of the earth, made of dust; the second man is the Lord from heaven.
46_15_48: As was the man of dust, so also are those who are of the dust, and as is the man of heaven, so also are those who are of heaven.
46_15_49: Just as we have borne the image of the man of dust, we shall also bear the image of the man of heaven.
46_15_50: I tell you this, brothers: flesh and blood cannot inherit the kingdom of God, nor does the perishable inherit the imperishable.
46_15_51: Behold! I tell you a mystery. We shall not all sleep, but we shall all be changed —
46_15_52: in a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trumpet. For the trumpet will sound, and the dead will be raised imperishable, and we shall be changed.
46_15_53: For this perishable body must put on the imperishable, and this mortal body must put on immortality.
46_15_54: When the perishable puts on the imperishable, and the mortal puts on immortality, then shall come to pass the saying that is written: 'Death is swallowed up in victory.'
46_15_55: 'O death, where is your victory? O death, where is your sting?'
46_15_56: The sting of death is sin, and the power of sin is the law.
46_15_57: But thanks be to God, who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.
46_15_58: Therefore, my beloved brothers, be steadfast, immovable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, knowing that in the Lord your labor is not in vain.
46_16_1: Now concerning the collection for the saints: as I directed the churches of Galatia, so you also are to do.
46_16_2: On the first day of every week, each of you is to set something aside and store it up, as he may prosper, so that there will be no collecting when I come.
46_16_3: And when I arrive, I will send those whom you approve with letters to carry your gift to Jerusalem.
46_16_4: If it seems advisable that I should go also, they will accompany me.
46_16_5: I will visit you after passing through Macedonia, for I intend to pass through Macedonia,
46_16_6: and perhaps I will stay with you or even spend the winter, so that you may help me on my journey, wherever I go.
46_16_7: For I do not want to see you now just in passing. I hope to spend some time with you, if the Lord permits.
46_16_8: But I will stay in Ephesus until Pentecost,
46_16_9: for a wide and effective door has opened to me, and there are many adversaries.
46_16_10: When Timothy comes, see that you put him at ease among you, for he is doing the work of the Lord, as I am.
46_16_11: So let no one despise him. Help him on his way in peace, so that he may return to me, for I am expecting him with the brothers.
46_16_12: Now concerning our brother Apollos, I strongly urged him to visit you with the other brothers, but it was not at all his will to come now. He will come when he has opportunity.
46_16_13: Be watchful, stand firm in the faith, act like men, be strong.
46_16_14: Let all that you do be done in love.
46_16_15: Now I urge you, brothers — you know that the household of Stephanas were the firstfruits of Achaia, and that they have devoted themselves to serving the saints —
46_16_16: be subject to such as these, and to every fellow worker and laborer.
46_16_17: I rejoice at the coming of Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus, because they have made up for your absence,
46_16_18: for they refreshed my spirit as well as yours. Give recognition to such men.
46_16_19: The churches of Asia send you greetings. Aquila and Priscilla, together with the church that meets in their house, send you warm greetings in the Lord.
46_16_20: All the brothers send you greetings. Greet one another with a holy kiss.
46_16_21: I, Paul, write this greeting with my own hand.
46_16_22: If anyone has no love for the Lord, let him be accursed. Come, Lord!
46_16_23: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.
46_16_24: My love be with you all in Christ Jesus. Amen.
47_1_1: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, and Timothy our brother, to the church of God that is at Corinth, with all the saints who are throughout Achaia:
47_1_2: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
47_1_3: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercies and God of all comfort,
47_1_4: who comforts us in all our affliction, so that we may be able to comfort those who are in any affliction, with the comfort with which we ourselves are comforted by God.
47_1_5: For as the sufferings of Christ overflow to us, so also our comfort overflows through Christ.
47_1_6: If we are afflicted, it is for your comfort and salvation; if we are comforted, it is for your comfort, which is effective as you patiently endure the same sufferings that we suffer.
47_1_7: Our hope for you is unshaken, for we know that as you share in our sufferings, you will also share in our comfort.
47_1_8: For we do not want you to be unaware, brothers, of the affliction we experienced in Asia. We were so utterly burdened beyond our strength that we despaired even of life itself.
47_1_9: Indeed, we felt that we had received the sentence of death, so that we would not trust in ourselves but in God who raises the dead.
47_1_10: He delivered us from such a deadly peril, and He will deliver us again. On Him we have set our hope that He will deliver us again,
47_1_11: as you also join in helping us through your prayers, so that many will give thanks on our behalf for the gracious gift granted to us through the prayers of many.
47_1_12: For our boast is this, the testimony of our conscience, that we have conducted ourselves in the world with simplicity and godly sincerity, not by earthly wisdom but by the grace of God, and supremely so toward you.
47_1_13: For we are writing to you nothing other than what you can read and understand. I hope you will understand fully,
47_1_14: just as you have understood us in part, that on the day of our Lord Jesus you will boast of us as we will boast of you.
47_1_15: Because I was confident of this, I intended to come to you first, so that you might have a second experience of grace.
47_1_16: I wanted to visit you on my way to Macedonia, and to come back to you from Macedonia and have you send me on my way to Judea.
47_1_17: Was I vacillating when I intended to do this? Do I make my plans according to the flesh, ready to say 'Yes, yes' and 'No, no' at the same time?
47_1_18: As surely as God is faithful, our word to you has not been Yes and No.
47_1_19: For the Son of God, Jesus Christ, who was proclaimed among you by us — by me and Silvanus and Timothy — was not Yes and No; but in Him it has always been Yes.
47_1_20: For all the promises of God find their Yes in Him. That is why it is through Him that we utter our Amen to God for His glory.
47_1_21: And it is God who establishes us with you in Christ, and has anointed us,
47_1_22: and who has also put His seal on us and given us His Spirit in our hearts as a deposit, guaranteeing what is to come.
47_1_23: But I call God as my witness — it was to spare you that I refrained from coming to Corinth again.
47_1_24: Not that we lord it over your faith, but we work with you for your joy, for you stand firm in your faith.
47_2_1: For I made up my mind not to make another painful visit to you.
47_2_2: For if I cause you pain, who is there to make me glad but the one whom I have pained?
47_2_3: And I wrote as I did, so that when I came I might not suffer pain from those who should have made me rejoice, for I felt sure of all of you, that my joy would be the joy of you all.
47_2_4: For I wrote to you out of great distress and anguish of heart and with many tears — not to cause you pain, but to let you know the deep love that I have for you.
47_2_5: Now if anyone has caused pain, he has caused it not to me, but in some measure — not to put it too severely — to all of you.
47_2_6: For such a person, the punishment inflicted by the majority is enough,
47_2_7: so that on the contrary you should rather turn and forgive and comfort him, or he may be overwhelmed by excessive sorrow.
47_2_8: So I urge you to reaffirm your love for him.
47_2_9: For this is why I wrote — that I might test you and know whether you are obedient in everything.
47_2_10: Anyone whom you forgive, I also forgive. Indeed, what I have forgiven, if I have forgiven anything, has been for your sake in the presence of Christ,
47_2_11: so that we would not be outwitted by Satan; for we are not ignorant of his schemes.
47_2_12: When I came to Troas to preach the gospel of Christ, even though a door was opened for me in the Lord,
47_2_13: my spirit was not at rest because I did not find my brother Titus there. So I took leave of them and went on to Macedonia.
47_2_14: But thanks be to God, who in Christ always leads us in triumphal procession, and through us spreads the fragrance of the knowledge of Him everywhere.
47_2_15: For we are the aroma of Christ to God among those who are being saved and among those who are perishing,
47_2_16: to one a fragrance from death to death, to the other a fragrance from life to life. Who is sufficient for these things?
47_2_17: For we are not, like so many, peddlers of God's word, but as men of sincerity, as commissioned by God, we speak in Christ in the sight of God.
47_3_1: Are we beginning to commend ourselves again? Or do we need, as some do, letters of recommendation to you, or from you?
47_3_2: You yourselves are our letter, written on our hearts, known and read by everyone.
47_3_3: And you show that you are a letter from Christ delivered by us, written not with ink but with the Spirit of the living God, not on tablets of stone but on tablets of human hearts.
47_3_4: Such is the confidence that we have through Christ toward God.
47_3_5: Not that we are sufficient in ourselves to claim anything as coming from us, but our sufficiency is from God,
47_3_6: who has made us sufficient as ministers of a new covenant — not of the letter but of the Spirit. For the letter kills, but the Spirit gives life.
47_3_7: Now if the ministry of death, carved in letters on stone, came with such glory that the Israelites could not gaze at Moses' face because of its glory — which was fading away —
47_3_8: will not the ministry of the Spirit have even more glory?
47_3_9: For if there was glory in the ministry of condemnation, how much more does the ministry of righteousness overflow with glory!
47_3_10: Indeed, in this case, what once had glory has come to have no glory at all, because of the surpassing glory that exceeds it.
47_3_11: For if what was being brought to an end came with glory, much more will what is permanent have glory.
47_3_12: Since we have such a hope, we are very bold,
47_3_13: not like Moses, who would put a veil over his face so that the Israelites might not gaze at the outcome of what was being brought to an end.
47_3_14: But their minds were hardened. For to this day, when they read the old covenant, that same veil remains unlifted, because only through Christ is it taken away.
47_3_15: Yes, to this day whenever Moses is read a veil lies over their hearts.
47_3_16: But when one turns to the Lord, the veil is removed.
47_3_17: Now the Lord is the Spirit, and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is freedom.
47_3_18: And we all, with unveiled faces, beholding as in a mirror the glory of the Lord, are being transformed into the same image from one degree of glory to another. For this comes from the Lord who is the Spirit.
47_4_1: Therefore, since we have this ministry by the mercy of God, we do not lose heart.
47_4_2: But we have renounced the hidden things of shame, refusing to walk in craftiness or to tamper with God's word, but by the open statement of the truth we commend ourselves to everyone's conscience in the sight of God.
47_4_3: And even if our gospel is veiled, it is veiled to those who are perishing.
47_4_4: In their case the god of this world has blinded the minds of the unbelievers, to keep them from seeing the light of the gospel of the glory of Christ, who is the image of God.
47_4_5: For what we proclaim is not ourselves, but Jesus Christ as Lord, with ourselves as your servants for Jesus' sake.
47_4_6: For God, who said, 'Let light shine out of darkness,' has shone in our hearts to give the light of the knowledge of the glory of God in the face of Jesus Christ.
47_4_7: But we have this treasure in jars of clay, to show that the surpassing power belongs to God and not to us.
47_4_8: We are hard pressed on every side, but not crushed; perplexed, but not in despair;
47_4_9: persecuted, but not forsaken; struck down, but not destroyed —
47_4_10: always carrying in the body the death of Jesus, so that the life of Jesus may also be manifested in our bodies.
47_4_11: For we who live are always being given over to death for Jesus' sake, so that the life of Jesus also may be manifested in our mortal flesh.
47_4_12: So death is at work in us, but life is at work in you.
47_4_13: Since we have the same spirit of faith as he had who wrote, 'I believed, and so I spoke,' we also believe, and so we also speak,
47_4_14: knowing that He who raised the Lord Jesus will raise us also with Jesus and bring us with you into His presence.
47_4_15: For it is all for your sake, so that as grace extends to more and more people it may increase thanksgiving, to the glory of God.
47_4_16: So we do not lose heart. Though our outer self is wasting away, our inner self is being renewed day by day.
47_4_17: For this light and momentary affliction is preparing for us an eternal weight of glory beyond all comparison,
47_4_18: as we look not to the things that are seen but to the things that are unseen. For the things that are seen are temporary, but the things that are unseen are eternal.
47_5_1: For we know that if the earthly tent we live in is destroyed, we have a building from God, a house not made with hands, eternal in the heavens.
47_5_2: For in this tent we groan, longing to put on our heavenly dwelling,
47_5_3: since by putting it on we will not be found naked.
47_5_4: For while we are still in this tent, we groan, being burdened — not that we would be unclothed, but that we would be further clothed, so that what is mortal may be swallowed up by life.
47_5_5: He who has prepared us for this very thing is God, who has given us the Spirit as a guarantee.
47_5_6: So we are always of good courage. We know that while we are at home in the body we are away from the Lord,
47_5_7: for we walk by faith, not by sight.
47_5_8: Yes, we are of good courage, and we would rather be away from the body and at home with the Lord.
47_5_9: So whether we are at home or away, we make it our aim to please Him.
47_5_10: For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ, so that each one may receive what is due for what he has done in the body, whether good or evil.
47_5_11: Therefore, knowing the fear of the Lord, we persuade others. What we are is known to God, and I hope it is also known to your consciences.
47_5_12: We are not commending ourselves to you again but giving you cause to boast about us, so that you may have something to say to those who boast in outward appearance and not in the heart.
47_5_13: For if we are beside ourselves, it is for God; if we are in our right mind, it is for you.
47_5_14: For the love of Christ compels us, because we have concluded this: that one has died for all, therefore all have died;
47_5_15: and He died for all, that those who live might no longer live for themselves but for Him who died and was raised for their sake.
47_5_16: From now on, therefore, we regard no one according to the flesh. Even though we once regarded Christ according to the flesh, we regard Him that way no longer.
47_5_17: Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, he is a new creation. The old has passed away; behold, the new has come.
47_5_18: All this is from God, who through Christ reconciled us to Himself and gave us the ministry of reconciliation;
47_5_19: that is, in Christ God was reconciling the world to Himself, not counting their trespasses against them, and entrusting to us the message of reconciliation.
47_5_20: Therefore, we are ambassadors for Christ, God making His appeal through us. We implore you on behalf of Christ, be reconciled to God.
47_5_21: For our sake He made Him to be sin who knew no sin, so that in Him we might become the righteousness of God.
47_6_1: Working together with Him, then, we appeal to you not to receive the grace of God in vain.
47_6_2: For He says, 'In a favorable time I listened to you, and in a day of salvation I have helped you.' Behold, now is the favorable time; behold, now is the day of salvation.
47_6_3: We put no obstacle in anyone's way, so that no fault may be found with our ministry,
47_6_4: but as servants of God we commend ourselves in every way: through great endurance, in afflictions, hardships, calamities,
