60_3_1: Wives, likewise, be submissive to your own husbands, that even if some do not obey the word, they may be won without a word by the conduct of their wives,
60_3_2: when they observe your chaste and respectful conduct.
60_3_3: Do not let your adornment be merely outward—arranging the hair, wearing gold, or putting on fine apparel—
60_3_4: rather let it be the hidden person of the heart, with the incorruptible beauty of a gentle and quiet spirit, which is very precious in the sight of God.
60_3_5: For in this manner, in former times, the holy women who trusted in God also adorned themselves, being submissive to their own husbands,
60_3_6: as Sarah obeyed Abraham, calling him lord, whose daughters you are if you do good and are not afraid with any terror.
60_3_7: Husbands, likewise, dwell with them with understanding, giving honor to the wife, as to the weaker vessel, and as being heirs together of the grace of life, that your prayers may not be hindered.
60_3_8: Finally, all of you be of one mind, having compassion for one another; love as brothers, be tenderhearted, be courteous;
60_3_9: not returning evil for evil or reviling for reviling, but on the contrary blessing, knowing that you were called to this, that you may inherit a blessing.
60_3_10: For “He who would love life and see good days, let him refrain his tongue from evil, and his lips from speaking deceit.
60_3_11: Let him turn away from evil and do good; let him seek peace and pursue it.
60_3_12: For the eyes of the LORD are on the righteous, and His ears are open to their prayers; but the face of the LORD is against those who do evil.
60_3_13: And who is he who will harm you if you are zealous for what is good?
60_3_14: But even if you should suffer for righteousness' sake, you are blessed. Do not be afraid of their threats, nor be troubled.
60_3_15: But sanctify Christ as Lord in your hearts, always be ready to give a defense to everyone who asks you a reason for the hope that is in you, with gentleness and respect.
60_3_16: Having a good conscience, that when they defame you as evildoers, those who revile your good conduct in Christ may be ashamed.
60_3_17: For it is better, if it is the will of God, to suffer for doing good than for doing evil.
60_3_18: For Christ also suffered once for sins, the righteous for the unrighteous, that He might bring us to God, being put to death in the flesh but made alive by the Spirit.
60_3_19: By whom also He went and preached to the spirits in prison.
60_3_20: Who formerly were disobedient, when once the Divine longsuffering waited in the days of Noah, while the ark was being prepared, in which a few, that is, eight souls, were saved through water.
60_3_21: There is also an antitype which now saves us—baptism (not the removal of the filth of the flesh, but the answer of a good conscience toward God), through the resurrection of Jesus Christ.
60_3_22: Who has gone into heaven and is at the right hand of God, angels and authorities and powers having been made subject to Him.
60_4_1: Therefore, since Christ suffered for us in the flesh, arm yourselves also with the same mind, for he who has suffered in the flesh has ceased from sin.
60_4_2: That he no longer should live the rest of his time in the flesh for the lusts of men, but for the will of God.
60_4_3: For we have spent enough of our past lifetime in doing the will of the Gentiles—when we walked in lewdness, lusts, drunkenness, revelries, drinking parties, and abominable idolatries.
60_4_4: In regard to these, they think it strange that you do not run with them in the same flood of dissipation, speaking evil of you.
60_4_5: They will give an account to Him who is ready to judge the living and the dead.
60_4_6: For this reason the gospel was preached also to those who are dead, that they might be judged according to men in the flesh, but live according to God in the spirit.
60_4_7: But the end of all things is at hand; therefore be serious and watchful in your prayers.
60_4_8: And above all things have fervent love for one another, for love will cover a multitude of sins.
60_4_9: Be hospitable to one another without grumbling or complaining.
60_4_10: As each one has received a gift, use it to serve one another, as good stewards of God's varied grace.
60_4_11: If anyone speaks, let him speak as the oracles of God. If anyone serves, let him do it with the strength God supplies, that in all things God may be glorified through Jesus Christ, to whom belong the glory and dominion forever and ever. Amen.
60_4_12: Beloved, do not be surprised at the fiery trial which is to test you, as though something strange were happening to you.
60_4_13: But rejoice to the extent that you share in Christ’s sufferings, so that you may also be glad with exceeding joy when His glory is revealed.
60_4_14: If you are insulted for the name of Christ, blessed are you, for the Spirit of glory and of God rests upon you.
60_4_15: But let none of you suffer as a murderer, a thief, an evildoer, or as a meddler in other people's matters.
60_4_16: Yet if anyone suffers as a Christian, let him not be ashamed, but let him glorify God in this matter.
60_4_17: For the time has come for judgment to begin at the house of God; and if it begins with us, what will be the outcome for those who do not obey the gospel of God?
60_4_18: Now, if the righteous one is scarcely saved, what will become of the ungodly and the sinner?
60_4_19: Therefore let those who suffer according to the will of God commit their souls to Him in doing good, as to a faithful Creator.
60_5_1: The elders who are among you I exhort, I who am a fellow elder and a witness of the sufferings of Christ, and also a partaker of the glory that will be revealed.
60_5_2: Shepherd the flock of God which is among you, serving as overseers, not by compulsion but willingly, not for dishonest gain but eagerly.
60_5_3: Nor as being lords over those entrusted to you, but being examples to the flock.
60_5_4: And when the Chief Shepherd appears, you will receive the unfading crown of glory.
60_5_5: Likewise you younger people, submit yourselves to your elders. Yes, all of you be submissive to one another, and be clothed with humility, for God resists the proud but gives grace to the humble.
60_5_6: Therefore humble yourselves under the mighty hand of God, that He may exalt you in due time.
60_5_7: Casting all your care upon Him, for He cares for you.
60_5_8: Be sober-minded, be vigilant; because your adversary the devil prowls around like a roaring lion, seeking whom he may devour.
60_5_9: Resist him, steadfast in the faith, knowing that the same sufferings are experienced by your brotherhood throughout the world.
60_5_10: But may the God of all grace, who called you to His eternal glory in Christ Jesus, after you have suffered a little while, perfect, establish, strengthen, and restore you.
60_5_11: To Him be the glory and the dominion forever and ever. Amen.
60_5_12: By Silvanus, our faithful brother as I consider him, I have written briefly to you, exhorting and testifying that this is the true grace of God. Stand firm in it.
60_5_13: She who is in Babylon, chosen together with you, greets you; and so does Mark, my son.
60_5_14: Greet one another with a kiss of love. Peace to all of you who are in Christ Jesus. Amen.
66_1_1: The Revelation of Jesus Christ, which God gave Him to show His servants the things which must soon take place. And He sent and signified it by His angel to His servant John,
66_1_2: who bore witness to the word of God and to the testimony of Jesus Christ, to all things that he saw.
66_1_3: Blessed is he who reads and those who hear the words of this prophecy, and keep what is written in it; for the time is near.
66_1_4: John, to the seven churches which are in Asia: Grace to you and peace from Him who is and who was and who is to come, and from the seven Spirits who are before His throne,
66_1_5: and from Jesus Christ, the faithful witness, the firstborn from the dead, and the ruler over the kings of the earth. To Him who loves us and washed us from our sins in His own blood,
66_1_6: and has made us a kingdom, priests to His God and Father; to Him be glory and dominion forever and ever. Amen.
66_1_7: Behold, He is coming with the clouds, and every eye will see Him, even those who pierced Him. And all the tribes of the earth will mourn because of Him. Even so, Amen.
66_1_8: I am the Alpha and the Omega, the Beginning and the End, says the Lord, who is and who was and who is to come, the Almighty.
66_1_9: I, John, both your brother and companion in the tribulation and kingdom and patience of Jesus Christ, was on the island that is called Patmos for the word of God and for the testimony of Jesus Christ.
66_1_10: I was in the Spirit on the Lord's Day, and I heard behind me a loud voice like a trumpet,
66_1_11: saying, Write what you see in a book and send it to the seven churches which are in Asia: to Ephesus, to Smyrna, to Pergamos, to Thyatira, to Sardis, to Philadelphia, and to Laodicea.
66_1_12: Then I turned to see the voice that spoke with me. And having turned I saw seven golden lampstands,
66_1_13: and in the midst of the seven lampstands One like the Son of Man, clothed with a garment down to the feet and girded about the chest with a golden band.
66_1_14: His head and hair were white like wool, as white as snow, and His eyes like a flame of fire;
66_1_15: His feet were like fine brass, as if refined in a furnace, and His voice as the sound of many waters;
66_1_16: He had in His right hand seven stars, out of His mouth went a sharp two-edged sword, and His countenance was like the sun shining in its strength.
66_1_17: And when I saw Him, I fell at His feet as dead. But He laid His right hand on me, saying to me, Do not be afraid; I am the First and the Last.
66_1_18: I am He who lives, and was dead, and behold, I am alive forevermore. Amen. And I have the keys of Hades and of Death.
66_1_19: Write the things which you have seen, and the things which are, and the things which will take place after this.
66_1_20: The mystery of the seven stars which you saw in My right hand, and the seven golden lampstands: The seven stars are the angels of the seven churches, and the seven lampstands are the seven churches.
66_2_1: To the angel of the church of Ephesus write: These things says He who holds the seven stars in His right hand, who walks in the midst of the seven golden lampstands:
66_2_2: I know your works, your labor, your patience, and that you cannot bear those who are evil. And you have tested those who say they are apostles and are not, and have found them liars;
66_2_3: and you have persevered and have patience, and have labored for My name's sake and have not become weary.
66_2_4: Nevertheless I have this against you, that you have left your first love.
66_2_5: Remember therefore from where you have fallen; repent and do the first works, or else I will come to you quickly and remove your lampstand from its place unless you repent.
66_2_6: But this you have, that you hate the deeds of the Nicolaitans, which I also hate.
66_2_7: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches. To him who overcomes I will give to eat from the tree of life, which is in the midst of the Paradise of God.
66_2_8: And to the angel of the church in Smyrna write: These things says the First and the Last, who was dead, and came to life:
66_2_9: I know your works, tribulation, and poverty (but you are rich); and I know the blasphemy of those who say they are Jews and are not, but are a synagogue of Satan.
66_2_10: Do not fear any of those things which you are about to suffer. Indeed, the devil is about to throw some of you into prison, that you may be tested, and you will have tribulation ten days. Be faithful until death, and I will give you the crown of life.
66_2_11: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches. The one who conquers shall not be hurt by the second death.
66_2_12: And to the angel of the church in Pergamum write: These things says the One who has the sharp, double-edged sword:
66_2_13: I know your works, and where you dwell, where Satan's throne is. Yet you hold fast to My name, and did not deny My faith even in the days of Antipas, My faithful witness, who was killed among you, where Satan dwells.
66_2_14: But I have a few things against you, because you have there some who hold the teaching of Balaam, who taught Balak to put a stumbling block before the sons of Israel, so that they might eat food sacrificed to idols and practice sexual immorality.
66_2_15: Thus you also have those who hold the teaching of the Nicolaitans, which thing I hate.
66_2_16: Repent, or else I will come to you quickly and will fight against them with the sword of My mouth.
66_2_17: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches. To the one who conquers I will give some of the hidden manna to eat, and I will give him a white stone, and on the stone a new name written which no one knows except him who receives it.
66_2_18: And to the angel of the church in Thyatira write: These things says the Son of God, who has eyes like a flame of fire, and whose feet are like burnished bronze:
66_2_19: I know your works, your love and faith and service and patient endurance, and that your latter works exceed the first.
66_2_20: Nevertheless I have a few things against you, because you tolerate that woman Jezebel, who calls herself a prophetess and is teaching and seducing My servants to commit sexual immorality and eat food sacrificed to idols.
66_2_21: And I gave her time to repent of her sexual immorality, but she refuses to repent.
66_2_22: Indeed I will cast her into a sickbed, and those who commit adultery with her into great tribulation, unless they repent of her works.
66_2_23: I will kill her children with death, and all the churches shall know that I am He who searches the minds and hearts. And I will give to each one of you according to your works.
66_2_24: Now to you I say, and to the rest in Thyatira, as many as do not have this doctrine, who have not known the so-called secrets of Satan, I will put on you no other burden.
66_2_25: But hold fast what you have until I come.
66_2_26: And he who overcomes, and keeps My works until the end, to him I will give authority over the nations.
66_2_27: He shall rule them with a rod of iron; they shall be dashed to pieces like the potter's vessels, as I also have received from My Father.
66_2_28: And I will give him the morning star.
66_2_29: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches.
66_3_1: And to the angel of the church in Sardis write: These things says He who has the seven Spirits of God and the seven stars: I know your works, that you have a name that you are alive, but you are dead.
66_3_2: Be watchful, and strengthen what remains, which is about to die, for I have not found your works complete before My God.
66_3_3: Remember therefore how you have received and heard; hold fast and repent. If you will not wake up, I will come upon you as a thief, and you will not know at what hour I will come upon you.
66_3_4: You have a few names even in Sardis who have not defiled their garments; and they shall walk with Me in white, for they are worthy.
66_3_5: He who overcomes shall be clothed in white garments, and I will not blot out his name from the Book of Life; but I will confess his name before My Father and before His angels.
66_3_6: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches.
66_3_7: And to the angel of the church in Philadelphia write: These things says He who is holy, He who is true, the One who has the key of David, who opens and no one shuts, and shuts and no one opens:
66_3_8: I know your works. Look, I have set before you an open door, and no one can shut it; for you have a little strength, have kept My word, and have not denied My name.
66_3_9: Indeed I will make those of the synagogue of Satan, who say they are Jews and are not, but lie—behold, I will make them come and bow down before your feet, and they will learn that I have loved you.
66_3_10: Because you have kept My command to persevere, I also will keep you from the hour of trial that is coming on the whole world, to test those who dwell on the earth.
66_3_11: Behold, I am coming quickly! Hold fast what you have, that no one may take your crown.
66_3_12: He who overcomes, I will make him a pillar in the temple of My God, and he shall go out no more. I will write on him the name of My God and the name of the city of My God, the New Jerusalem, which comes down out of heaven from My God. And I will write on him My new name.
66_3_13: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches.
66_3_14: And to the angel of the church in Laodicea write: These things says the Amen, the faithful and true witness, the beginning of God's creation:
66_3_15: I know your works, that you are neither cold nor hot. I could wish you were cold or hot.
66_3_16: So then, because you are lukewarm, and neither cold nor hot, I will vomit you out of My mouth.
66_3_17: Because you say, I am rich, have become wealthy, and have need of nothing—and do not know that you are wretched, miserable, poor, blind, and naked—
66_3_18: I counsel you to buy from Me gold refined in the fire, that you may be rich; and white garments, that you may be clothed, that the shame of your nakedness may not be revealed; and anoint your eyes with eye salve, that you may see.
66_3_19: As many as I love, I rebuke and chasten. Therefore be zealous and repent.
66_3_20: Behold, I stand at the door and knock. If anyone hears My voice and opens the door, I will come in to him and dine with him, and he with Me.
66_3_21: To him who overcomes I will grant to sit with Me on My throne, as I also overcame and sat down with My Father on His throne.
66_3_22: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches.
66_4_1: After these things I looked, and behold, a door standing open in heaven. And the first voice which I heard was like a trumpet speaking with me, saying, Come up here, and I will show you things which must take place after this.
66_4_2: Immediately I was in the Spirit; and behold, a throne set in heaven, and One sat on the throne.
66_4_3: And He who sat there was like a jasper and a sardius stone in appearance; and there was a rainbow around the throne, in appearance like an emerald.
66_4_4: Around the throne were twenty-four thrones, and on the thrones I saw twenty-four elders sitting, clothed in white robes; and they had crowns of gold on their heads.
66_4_5: And from the throne proceeded lightnings, thunderings, and voices. Seven lamps of fire were burning before the throne, which are the seven Spirits of God.
66_4_6: Before the throne there was a sea of glass, like crystal. And in the midst of the throne, and around the throne, were four living creatures full of eyes in front and in back.
66_4_7: The first living creature was like a lion, the second living creature like a calf, the third living creature had a face like a man, and the fourth living creature was like a flying eagle.
66_4_8: The four living creatures, each having six wings, were full of eyes around and within. And they do not rest day or night, saying: Holy, holy, holy, Lord God Almighty, Who was and is and is to come!
66_4_9: Whenever the living creatures give glory and honor and thanks to Him who sits on the throne, who lives forever and ever,
66_4_10: the twenty-four elders fall down before Him who sits on the throne and worship Him who lives forever and ever, and cast their crowns before the throne, saying:
66_4_11: You are worthy, O Lord, to receive glory and honor and power; For You created all things, and by Your will they exist and were created.
66_5_1: And I saw in the right hand of Him who sat on the throne a scroll written inside and on the back, sealed with seven seals.
66_5_2: Then I saw a strong angel proclaiming with a loud voice, Who is worthy to open the scroll and to loose its seals?
66_5_3: And no one in heaven or on the earth or under the earth was able to open the scroll, or to look at it.
66_5_4: So I wept much, because no one was found worthy to open and read the scroll, or to look at it.
66_5_5: But one of the elders said to me, Do not weep. Behold, the Lion of the tribe of Judah, the Root of David, has prevailed to open the scroll and to loose its seven seals.
66_5_6: And I looked, and behold, in the midst of the throne and of the four living creatures, and in the midst of the elders, stood a Lamb as though it had been slain, having seven horns and seven eyes, which are the seven Spirits of God sent out into all the earth.
66_5_7: Then He came and took the scroll out of the right hand of Him who sat on the throne.
66_5_8: Now when He had taken the scroll, the four living creatures and the twenty-four elders fell down before the Lamb, each having a harp, and golden bowls full of incense, which are the prayers of the saints.
66_5_9: And they sang a new song, saying, “You are worthy to take the scroll and to open its seals, for You were slain, and by Your blood You ransomed people for God from every tribe and language and people and nation,
66_5_10: and have made us a kingdom and priests to our God; and we shall reign on the earth.”
66_5_11: Then I looked, and I heard the voice of many angels around the throne, the living creatures, and the elders; and the number of them was myriads of myriads and thousands of thousands,
66_5_12: saying with a loud voice, “Worthy is the Lamb who was slain to receive power and wealth and wisdom and might and honor and glory and blessing!”
66_5_13: And every creature which is in heaven and on the earth and under the earth and in the sea, and all that is in them, I heard saying, “Blessing and honor and glory and power be to Him who sits on the throne, and to the Lamb, forever and ever!”
66_5_14: Then the four living creatures said, “Amen!” And the elders fell down and worshiped.
66_6_1: Now I saw when the Lamb opened one of the seals; and I heard one of the four living creatures saying with a voice like thunder, “Come!”
66_6_2: And I looked, and behold, a white horse! He who sat on it had a bow; and a crown was given to him, and he went out conquering and to conquer.
66_6_3: When He opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature say, “Come!”
66_6_4: Another horse, fiery red, went out. And it was granted to the one who sat on it to take peace from the earth, so that people should slay one another; and there was given to him a great sword.
66_6_5: When He opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature say, “Come!” So I looked, and behold, a black horse, and he who sat on it had a pair of scales in his hand.
66_6_6: And I heard something like a voice in the midst of the four living creatures saying, “A quart of wheat for a denarius, and three quarts of barley for a denarius; and do not harm the oil and the wine.”
66_6_7: When He opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature saying, “Come!”
66_6_8: So I looked, and behold, a pale horse. And the name of him who sat on it was Death, and Hades followed with him. And authority was given to them over a fourth of the earth, to kill with sword, with famine, with plague, and by the wild beasts of the earth.
66_6_9: When He opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar the souls of those who had been slain for the word of God and for the testimony which they held.
66_6_10: And they cried with a loud voice, saying, “How long, O Sovereign Lord, holy and true, until You judge and avenge our blood on those who dwell on the earth?”
66_6_11: Then a white robe was given to each of them; and it was said to them that they should rest a little while longer, until the number of their fellow servants and their brothers and sisters, who were to be killed as they had been, was completed.
66_6_12: I looked when He opened the sixth seal, and behold, there was a great earthquake; and the sun became black as sackcloth, and the moon became like blood.
66_6_13: And the stars of heaven fell to the earth, as a fig tree drops its unripe figs when shaken by a mighty wind.
66_6_14: Then the sky was split apart like a scroll being rolled up, and every mountain and island was moved from its place.
66_6_15: And the kings of the earth, the great men, the rich men, the commanders, the mighty men, every slave and every free man, hid themselves in the caves and among the rocks of the mountains,
66_6_16: and said to the mountains and rocks, “Fall on us and hide us from the face of Him who sits on the throne and from the wrath of the Lamb!
66_6_17: For the great day of their wrath has come, and who is able to stand?”
66_7_1: After these things I saw four angels standing at the four corners of the earth, holding back the four winds of the earth, that no wind might blow on the earth or sea or against any tree.
66_7_2: Then I saw another angel ascending from the rising of the sun, having the seal of the living God. And he cried with a loud voice to the four angels who had been given power to harm the earth and the sea,
66_7_3: saying, “Do not harm the earth, the sea, or the trees until we have sealed the servants of our God on their foreheads.”
66_7_4: And I heard the number of those who were sealed: one hundred and forty-four thousand sealed from every tribe of the children of Israel:
66_7_5: of the tribe of Judah twelve thousand were sealed; of the tribe of Reuben twelve thousand were sealed; of the tribe of Gad twelve thousand were sealed;
66_7_6: of the tribe of Asher twelve thousand were sealed; of the tribe of Naphtali twelve thousand were sealed; of the tribe of Manasseh twelve thousand were sealed;
66_7_7: of the tribe of Simeon twelve thousand were sealed; of the tribe of Levi twelve thousand were sealed; of the tribe of Issachar twelve thousand were sealed;
66_7_8: of the tribe of Zebulun twelve thousand were sealed; of the tribe of Joseph twelve thousand were sealed; of the tribe of Benjamin twelve thousand were sealed.
66_7_9: After these things I looked, and behold, a great multitude that no one could number, from every nation, tribes, peoples, and languages, standing before the throne and before the Lamb, clothed in white robes, with palm branches in their hands,
66_7_10: and crying out with a loud voice, saying, Salvation belongs to our God who sits on the throne, and to the Lamb!
66_7_11: All the angels stood around the throne and the elders and the four living creatures, and they fell on their faces before the throne and worshiped God,
66_7_12: saying: Amen! Blessing and glory and wisdom and thanksgiving and honor and power and might be to our God forever and ever. Amen.
66_7_13: Then one of the elders answered, saying to me, Who are these clothed in white robes, and where did they come from?
66_7_14: And I said to him, Sir, you know. So he said to me, These are the ones coming out of the great tribulation, and they have washed their robes and made them white in the blood of the Lamb.
66_7_15: Therefore they are before the throne of God, and serve Him day and night in His temple; and He who sits on the throne will shelter them.
66_7_16: They shall neither hunger anymore nor thirst anymore; the sun shall not strike them, nor any scorching heat;
66_7_17: for the Lamb who is in the midst of the throne will shepherd them and lead them to springs of living water, and God will wipe away every tear from their eyes.
66_8_1: When He opened the seventh seal, there was silence in heaven for about half an hour.
66_8_2: And I saw the seven angels who stand before God, and to them were given seven trumpets.
66_8_3: Then another angel, having a golden censer, came and stood at the altar. He was given much incense to offer with the prayers of all the saints upon the golden altar which was before the throne.
66_8_4: And the smoke of the incense, with the prayers of the saints, ascended before God from the angel's hand.
66_8_5: Then the angel took the censer, filled it with fire from the altar, and threw it to the earth. And there were noises, thunderings, lightnings, and an earthquake.
66_8_6: So the seven angels who had the seven trumpets prepared themselves to sound.
66_8_7: The first angel sounded: And hail and fire followed, mingled with blood, and they were thrown to the earth. A third of the trees were burned up, and all green grass was burned up.
66_8_8: Then the second angel sounded: And something like a great mountain burning with fire was thrown into the sea, and a third of the sea became blood.
66_8_9: And a third of the living creatures in the sea died, and a third of the ships were destroyed.
66_8_10: Then the third angel sounded: And a great star fell from heaven, burning like a torch, and it fell on a third of the rivers and on the springs of water.
66_8_11: The name of the star is Wormwood. A third of the waters became wormwood, and many people died from the waters, because they had been made bitter.
66_8_12: Then the fourth angel sounded: And a third of the sun was struck, a third of the moon, and a third of the stars, so that a third of them were darkened. A third of the day did not shine, and likewise the night.
66_8_13: And I looked, and I heard an eagle flying through the midst of heaven, saying with a loud voice, Woe, woe, woe to the inhabitants of the earth, because of the remaining blasts of the trumpet of the three angels who are about to sound!
66_9_1: Then the fifth angel sounded: And I saw a star fallen from heaven to the earth. To him was given the key to the bottomless pit.
66_9_2: And he opened the bottomless pit, and smoke arose out of the pit like the smoke of a great furnace. So the sun and the air were darkened because of the smoke of the pit.
66_9_3: Then out of the smoke locusts came upon the earth. And to them was given power, as the scorpions of the earth have power.
66_9_4: They were commanded not to harm the grass of the earth, or any green thing, or any tree, but only those people who do not have the seal of God on their foreheads.
66_9_5: And they were not given authority to kill them, but to torment them for five months. Their torment was like the torment of a scorpion when it strikes a man.
66_9_6: In those days people will seek death and will not find it; they will long to die, but death will flee from them.
66_9_7: The shape of the locusts was like horses prepared for battle. On their heads were crowns of something like gold, and their faces were like the faces of men.
66_9_8: They had hair like women's hair, and their teeth were like lions' teeth.
66_9_9: And they had breastplates like breastplates of iron, and the sound of their wings was like the noise of many chariots with horses rushing into battle.
66_9_10: They had tails like scorpions, and there were stings in their tails; their power was to hurt people for five months.
66_9_11: And they had as king over them the angel of the bottomless pit, whose name in Hebrew is Abaddon, and in Greek he is called Apollyon.
66_9_12: One woe is past; behold, two more woes are still to come.
66_9_13: Then the sixth angel sounded, and I heard a voice from the four horns of the golden altar which is before God,
66_9_14: saying to the sixth angel who had the trumpet, 'Release the four angels who are bound at the great river Euphrates.'
66_9_15: So the four angels, who had been prepared for the hour, the day, the month, and the year, were released to kill a third of mankind.
66_9_16: Now the number of the army of the horsemen was two hundred million; I heard their number.
66_9_17: And thus I saw the horses in the vision: those who sat on them had breastplates of fiery red, hyacinth blue, and sulfur yellow; and the heads of the horses were like the heads of lions, and out of their mouths came fire, smoke, and brimstone.
66_9_18: By these three plagues a third of mankind was killed, by the fire and the smoke and the brimstone which came out of their mouths.
66_9_19: For their power is in their mouths and in their tails; for their tails are like serpents with heads, and with them they do harm.
66_9_20: But the rest of mankind, who were not killed by these plagues, did not repent of the works of their hands, nor give up worshiping demons and idols of gold, silver, bronze, stone, and wood, which cannot see or hear or walk.
66_9_21: And they did not repent of their murders or their sorceries or their sexual immorality or their thefts.
66_10_1: I saw still another mighty angel coming down from heaven, clothed with a cloud; and a rainbow was on his head, his face was like the sun, and his feet like pillars of fire.
66_10_2: He had a little book open in his hand. And he set his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land,
66_10_3: and cried with a loud voice, as when a lion roars; when he cried out, seven thunders uttered their voices.
66_10_4: Now when the seven thunders had sounded, I was about to write; but I heard a voice from heaven saying to me, 'Seal up the things which the seven thunders uttered, and do not write them.'
66_10_5: The angel whom I saw standing on the sea and on the land raised his right hand to heaven
66_10_6: and swore by Him who lives forever and ever, who created heaven and what is in it, the earth and what is in it, and the sea and what is in it, that there would be no more delay,
66_10_7: but in the days of the sounding of the seventh angel, when he is about to sound, the mystery of God would be finished, as He declared to His servants the prophets.
66_10_8: Then the voice which I heard from heaven spoke to me again and said, 'Go, take the little book which is open in the hand of the angel who stands on the sea and on the earth.'
66_10_9: So I went to the angel and said to him, 'Give me the little book.' And he said to me, 'Take and eat it; it will make your stomach bitter, but it will be as sweet as honey in your mouth.'
66_10_10: Then I took the little book out of the angel's hand and ate it, and it was as sweet as honey in my mouth; but when I had eaten it, my stomach became bitter.
66_10_11: And he said to me, 'You must prophesy again about many peoples, nations, languages, and kings.'
66_11_1: Then I was given a reed like a measuring rod. And the angel stood, saying, 'Rise and measure the temple of God, the altar, and those who worship there.
66_11_2: But leave out the court which is outside the temple, and do not measure it, for it has been given to the nations; and they will trample the holy city for forty-two months.
66_11_3: And I will give power to my two witnesses, and they will prophesy one thousand two hundred and sixty days, clothed in sackcloth.
66_11_4: These are the two olive trees and the two lampstands that stand before the Lord of the earth.
66_11_5: And if anyone wants to harm them, fire proceeds from their mouth and devours their enemies; if anyone wants to harm them, he must be killed in this manner.
66_11_6: These have power to shut heaven, so that no rain falls during the days of their prophecy; and they have power over the waters to turn them into blood and to strike the earth with every kind of plague, as often as they desire.
66_11_7: When they finish their testimony, the beast that ascends out of the abyss will make war against them, conquer them, and kill them.
66_11_8: And their dead bodies will lie in the street of the great city which symbolically is called Sodom and Egypt, where also their Lord was crucified.
66_11_9: Then those from the peoples, tribes, languages, and nations will gaze at their dead bodies for three and a half days, and will not permit their dead bodies to be laid in a tomb.
66_11_10: And those who dwell on the earth will rejoice over them, make merry, and send gifts to one another, because these two prophets had been a torment to those who dwell on the earth.
66_11_11: Now after the three and a half days the breath of life from God entered them, and they stood on their feet, and great fear fell on those who were watching them.
66_11_12: And they heard a loud voice from heaven saying to them, 'Come up here.' And they went up to heaven in a cloud, while their enemies watched them.
66_11_13: In that hour there was a great earthquake, and a tenth of the city fell; seven thousand people were killed in the earthquake, and the rest were terrified and gave glory to the God of heaven.
66_11_14: The second woe is past; behold, the third woe is coming quickly.
66_11_15: Then the seventh angel sounded; and there were loud voices in heaven, saying, 'The kingdom of the world has become the kingdom of our Lord and of His Christ, and He shall reign forever and ever!'
66_11_16: And the twenty-four elders who sat before God on their thrones fell on their faces and worshiped God,
66_11_17: saying, 'We give You thanks, O Lord God Almighty, who is and who was, because You have taken Your great power and begun to reign.'
66_11_18: The nations were angry, but Your wrath has come, and the time for the dead to be judged, and for rewarding Your servants, the prophets and saints, and those who fear Your name, both small and great, and for destroying the destroyers of the earth.
66_11_19: Then the temple of God was opened in heaven, and the ark of His covenant was seen in His temple. And there were flashes of lightning, rumblings, peals of thunder, an earthquake, and severe hail.
66_12_1: Now a great sign appeared in heaven: a woman clothed with the sun, with the moon under her feet, and a crown of twelve stars on her head.
66_12_2: Then being with child, she cried out in labor and in the agony of giving birth.
66_12_3: And another sign appeared in heaven: behold, a great fiery red dragon having seven heads and ten horns, and on his heads seven diadems.
66_12_4: His tail swept away a third of the stars of heaven and threw them to the earth. And the dragon stood before the woman who was about to give birth, so that when she bore her child he might devour it.
66_12_5: She bore a male Child who is to rule all nations with a rod of iron. And her Child was caught up to God and to His throne.
66_12_6: Then the woman fled into the wilderness, where she had a place prepared by God, to be nourished there for one thousand two hundred and sixty days.
66_12_7: And war broke out in heaven: Michael and his angels fought against the dragon; and the dragon and his angels fought back,
66_12_8: but he was defeated, and there was no longer any place for them in heaven.
66_12_9: So the great dragon was cast out, that ancient serpent called the Devil and Satan, who deceives the whole world; he was thrown to the earth, and his angels were cast out with him.
66_12_10: Then I heard a loud voice in heaven saying, 'Now the salvation and the power and the kingdom of our God and the authority of His Christ have come, for the accuser of our brethren has been thrown down, who accuses them day and night before our God.'
66_12_11: And they overcame him by the blood of the Lamb and by the word of their testimony, for they did not love their lives to the point of death.
66_12_12: Therefore rejoice, O heavens, and you who dwell in them! Woe to the inhabitants of the earth and the sea! For the devil has come down to you with great wrath, because he knows that he has a short time.
66_12_13: Now when the dragon saw that he had been cast to the earth, he persecuted the woman who had given birth to the male Child.
66_12_14: But the woman was given two wings of a great eagle, so that she might fly into the wilderness to her place, where she is to be nourished for a time, times, and half a time, from the presence of the serpent.
66_12_15: So the serpent spewed water out of his mouth like a river after the woman, that he might cause her to be swept away with the flood.
66_12_16: But the earth helped the woman, and the earth opened its mouth and swallowed the river that the dragon had spewed out of his mouth.
66_12_17: And the dragon was enraged with the woman, and he went to make war with the rest of her offspring, who keep the commandments of God and hold to the testimony of Jesus Christ.
66_12_18: Then the dragon took his stand on the shore beside the sea.
66_13_1: Then I stood on the sand of the sea, and I saw a beast rising up out of the sea, having seven heads and ten horns, and on his horns ten crowns, and on his heads a blasphemous name.
66_13_2: Now the beast which I saw was like a leopard, his feet were like those of a bear, and his mouth like the mouth of a lion. The dragon gave him his power, his throne, and great authority.
66_13_3: And I saw one of his heads as if it had been mortally wounded, and his deadly wound was healed. And all the world marveled and followed the beast.
66_13_4: So they worshiped the dragon who gave authority to the beast, and they worshiped the beast, saying, 'Who is like the beast? Who is able to make war with him?'
66_13_5: And he was given a mouth speaking great things and blasphemies, and he was given authority to continue for forty-two months.
66_13_6: Then he opened his mouth in blasphemy against God, to blaspheme His name, His tabernacle, and those who dwell in heaven.
66_13_7: It was granted to him to make war with the saints and to overcome them, and authority was given him over every tribe, tongue, and nation.
66_13_8: All who dwell on the earth will worship him, whose names have not been written in the Book of Life of the Lamb slain from the foundation of the world.
66_13_9: If anyone has an ear, let him hear.
66_13_10: He who leads into captivity shall go into captivity; he who kills with the sword must be killed with the sword. Here is the patience and the faith of the saints.
66_13_11: Then I saw another beast coming up out of the earth, and he had two horns like a lamb and spoke like a dragon.
66_13_12: And he exercises all the authority of the first beast in his presence, and causes the earth and those who dwell in it to worship the first beast, whose deadly wound was healed.
66_13_13: He performs great signs, so that he even makes fire come down from heaven on the earth in the sight of men.
66_13_14: And he deceives those who dwell on the earth by those signs which he was granted to do in the sight of the beast, telling those who dwell on the earth to make an image to the beast who was wounded by the sword and lived.
66_13_15: He was granted power to give breath to the image of the beast, that the image of the beast should both speak and cause as many as would not worship the image of the beast to be killed.
66_13_16: He causes all, both small and great, rich and poor, free and slave, to receive a mark on their right hand or on their foreheads,
66_13_17: and that no one may buy or sell except one who has the mark, the name of the beast, or the number of his name.
66_13_18: Here is wisdom. Let him who has understanding calculate the number of the beast, for it is the number of a man: His number is 666.
66_14_1: Then I looked, and behold, a Lamb standing on Mount Zion, and with Him one hundred and forty-four thousand, having His Father’s name written on their foreheads.
66_14_2: And I heard a voice from heaven, like the sound of many waters, and like the sound of loud thunder. And I heard the sound of harpists playing their harps.
66_14_3: They sang a new song before the throne, before the four living creatures, and the elders; and no one could learn that song except the hundred and forty-four thousand who were redeemed from the earth.
66_14_4: These are the ones who were not defiled with women, for they are virgins. These are the ones who follow the Lamb wherever He goes. They were redeemed from among men, being firstfruits to God and to the Lamb.
66_14_5: And in their mouth was found no deceit, for they are without fault before the throne of God.
66_14_6: Then I saw another angel flying in the midst of heaven, having the everlasting gospel to preach to those who dwell on the earth—to every nation, tribe, tongue, and people—
66_14_7: saying with a loud voice, 'Fear God and give glory to Him, for the hour of His judgment has come; and worship Him who made heaven and earth, the sea and springs of water.'
66_14_8: And another angel followed, saying, 'Babylon is fallen, is fallen, that great city, because she has made all nations drink of the wine of the wrath of her fornication.'
66_14_9: Then a third angel followed them, saying with a loud voice, 'If anyone worships the beast and his image, and receives his mark on his forehead or on his hand,'
66_14_10: he himself shall also drink of the wine of the wrath of God, which is poured out full strength into the cup of His indignation. He shall be tormented with fire and brimstone in the presence of the holy angels and in the presence of the Lamb.
66_14_11: And the smoke of their torment ascends forever and ever; and they have no rest day or night, these worshipers of the beast and its image, and whoever receives the mark of its name.
66_14_12: Here is the patience of the saints; here are those who keep the commandments of God and their faith in Jesus.
66_14_13: Then I heard a voice from heaven saying to me, 'Write: Blessed are the dead who die in the Lord from now on.' 'Yes,' says the Spirit, 'that they may rest from their labors, for their deeds follow them.'
66_14_14: Then I looked, and behold, a white cloud, and on the cloud sat One like the Son of Man, having on His head a golden crown, and in His hand a sharp sickle.
66_14_15: And another angel came out of the temple, crying with a loud voice to Him who sat on the cloud, 'Thrust in Your sickle and reap, for the hour to reap has come, for the harvest of the earth is fully ripe.'
66_14_16: So He who sat on the cloud thrust in His sickle on the earth, and the earth was reaped.
66_14_17: Then another angel came out of the temple which is in heaven, he also having a sharp sickle.
66_14_18: And another angel came out from the altar, who had power over fire, and he cried with a loud voice to him who had the sharp sickle, saying, 'Thrust in your sharp sickle and gather the clusters from the vine of the earth, for its grapes are ripe.'
66_14_19: So the angel thrust his sickle into the earth and gathered the vine of the earth, and threw it into the great winepress of the wrath of God.
66_14_20: And the winepress was trampled outside the city, and blood came out of the winepress, up to the horses' bridles, for about 180 miles.
66_15_1: Then I saw another sign in heaven, great and marvelous: seven angels having the seven last plagues, for with them the wrath of God is finished.
66_15_2: And I saw something like a sea of glass mingled with fire, and those who had conquered the beast and its image and the number of its name, standing on the sea of glass, holding harps from God.
66_15_3: They sing the song of Moses, the servant of God, and the song of the Lamb, saying: 'Great and marvelous are Your works, Lord God Almighty! Just and true are Your ways, O King of the nations!'
66_15_4: Who shall not fear You, O Lord, and glorify Your name? For You alone are holy. All nations shall come and worship before You, for Your righteous acts have been revealed.
66_15_5: After these things I looked, and behold, the temple of the tabernacle of the testimony in heaven was opened.
66_15_6: And out of the temple came the seven angels having the seven plagues, clothed in pure bright linen, and having their chests girded with golden bands.
66_15_7: Then one of the four living creatures gave to the seven angels seven golden bowls full of the wrath of God who lives forever and ever.
66_15_8: The temple was filled with smoke from the glory of God and from His power, and no one was able to enter the temple until the seven plagues of the seven angels were completed.
66_16_1: Then I heard a loud voice from the temple saying to the seven angels, 'Go and pour out the seven bowls of the wrath of God on the earth.'
66_16_2: So the first went and poured out his bowl upon the earth, and a foul and loathsome sore came upon the people who had the mark of the beast and those who worshiped his image.
66_16_3: Then the second angel poured out his bowl on the sea, and it became like the blood of a corpse, and every living thing in the sea died.
66_16_4: Then the third angel poured out his bowl on the rivers and springs of water, and they became blood.
66_16_5: And I heard the angel of the waters saying: 'You are righteous, O Lord, the One who is and who was, because You have judged these things.'
66_16_6: For they have shed the blood of saints and prophets, and You have given them blood to drink; they deserve it.
66_16_7: And I heard another from the altar saying, 'Yes, Lord God Almighty, true and righteous are Your judgments.'
66_16_8: Then the fourth angel poured out his bowl on the sun, and it was allowed to scorch people with fire.
66_16_9: And men were scorched with great heat, and they blasphemed the name of God who has power over these plagues; and they did not repent and give Him glory.
66_16_10: Then the fifth angel poured out his bowl on the throne of the beast, and its kingdom was plunged into darkness; and they gnawed their tongues because of the pain.
66_16_11: They blasphemed the God of heaven because of their pains and their sores, and did not repent of their deeds.
66_16_12: Then the sixth angel poured out his bowl on the great river Euphrates, and its water was dried up, to prepare the way for the kings from the east.
66_16_13: And I saw three unclean spirits like frogs coming out of the mouth of the dragon, out of the mouth of the beast, and out of the mouth of the false prophet.
66_16_14: For they are demonic spirits, performing signs, who go out to the kings of the whole world, to gather them to the battle of that great day of God Almighty.
66_16_15: Behold, I am coming as a thief. Blessed is he who watches and keeps his garments, lest he walk naked and they see his shame.
66_16_16: And they gathered them together to the place called in Hebrew, Armageddon.
66_16_17: Then the seventh angel poured out his bowl into the air, and a loud voice came out of the temple of heaven, from the throne, saying, It is done!
66_16_18: And there were flashes of lightning, rumblings, and peals of thunder; and there was a great earthquake, such as there had never been since man was on the earth, so great was that earthquake.
66_16_19: Now the great city was divided into three parts, and the cities of the nations fell. And great Babylon was remembered before God, to give her the cup of the wine of the fierceness of His wrath.
66_16_20: Then every island fled away, and the mountains were not found.
66_16_21: And great hail from heaven fell upon men, each hailstone about the weight of a talent. Men blasphemed God because of the plague of the hail, since that plague was exceedingly great.
66_17_1: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls came and talked with me, saying to me, Come, I will show you the judgment of the great harlot who sits on many waters,
66_17_2: with whom the kings of the earth have committed sexual immorality, and with the wine of whose sexual immorality the dwellers on earth have become drunk.
66_17_3: So he carried me away in the Spirit into the wilderness. And I saw a woman sitting on a scarlet beast which was full of names of blasphemy, having seven heads and ten horns.
66_17_4: The woman was arrayed in purple and scarlet, and adorned with gold and precious stones and pearls, having in her hand a golden cup full of abominations and the impurities of her sexual immorality.
66_17_5: And on her forehead a name was written: MYSTERY, BABYLON THE GREAT, THE MOTHER OF HARLOTS AND OF THE ABOMINATIONS OF THE EARTH.
66_17_6: I saw the woman, drunk with the blood of the saints and with the blood of the martyrs of Jesus. And when I saw her, I marveled with great amazement.
66_17_7: But the angel said to me, Why do you marvel? I will tell you the mystery of the woman and of the beast that carries her, which has the seven heads and the ten horns.
66_17_8: The beast that you saw was, and is not, and is about to rise from the bottomless pit and go to destruction. And those who dwell on the earth will marvel, whose names are not written in the Book of Life from the foundation of the world, when they see the beast that was, and is not, and yet is.
66_17_9: Here is the mind which has wisdom: The seven heads are seven mountains on which the woman sits.
66_17_10: There are also seven kings. Five have fallen, one is, and the other has not yet come. And when he comes, he must continue a short time.
66_17_11: The beast that was, and is not, is himself also the eighth, and is of the seven, and is going to perdition.
66_17_12: The ten horns which you saw are ten kings who have not yet received a kingdom, but they receive authority for one hour as kings with the beast.
66_17_13: These are of one mind, and they will give their power and authority to the beast.
66_17_14: These will make war with the Lamb, and the Lamb will overcome them, for He is Lord of lords and King of kings; and those who are with Him are called, chosen, and faithful.
66_17_15: Then he said to me, The waters which you saw, where the harlot sits, are peoples, multitudes, nations, and tongues.
66_17_16: And the ten horns which you saw on the beast, these will hate the harlot, make her desolate and naked, eat her flesh and burn her with fire.
66_17_17: For God has put it into their hearts to fulfill His purpose, to be of one mind, and to give their kingdom to the beast, until the words of God are fulfilled.
66_17_18: And the woman whom you saw is that great city which reigns over the kings of the earth.
66_18_1: After these things I saw another angel coming down from heaven, having great authority, and the earth was illuminated with his glory.
66_18_2: And he cried mightily with a loud voice, saying, Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great! She has become a dwelling place for demons, a haunt for every unclean spirit, a haunt for every unclean bird, and a haunt for every unclean and detestable beast.
66_18_3: For all the nations have drunk of the wine of the wrath of her fornication, the kings of the earth have committed fornication with her, and the merchants of the earth have become rich through the abundance of her luxury.
66_18_4: And I heard another voice from heaven saying, Come out of her, my people, lest you share in her sins and receive of her plagues.
66_18_5: For her sins have piled up as high as heaven, and God has remembered her iniquities.
66_18_6: Render to her just as she rendered to you, and repay her double according to her works; in the cup which she has mixed, mix a double portion for her.
66_18_7: In the measure that she glorified herself and lived luxuriously, in the same measure give her torment and sorrow; for she says in her heart, I sit as queen, and am no widow, and will never see mourning.
66_18_8: Therefore her plagues will come in one day—death and mourning and famine. And she will be utterly burned with fire, for mighty is the Lord God who judges her.
66_18_9: The kings of the earth who committed sexual immorality and lived luxuriously with her will weep and lament for her when they see the smoke of her burning,
66_18_10: standing at a distance for fear of her torment, saying, Alas, alas, that great city Babylon, that mighty city! For in one hour your judgment has come.
66_18_11: And the merchants of the earth will weep and mourn over her, for no one buys their merchandise anymore.
66_18_12: merchandise of gold and silver, precious stones and pearls, fine linen and purple, silk and scarlet, every kind of fragrant wood, every kind of object of ivory, every kind of object of costly wood, bronze, iron, and marble;
66_18_13: and cinnamon and incense, fragrant oil and frankincense, wine and oil, fine flour and wheat, cattle and sheep, horses and chariots, and bodies and souls of men.
66_18_14: The fruit that your soul longed for has gone from you, and all the things which are rich and splendid have gone from you, and you shall find them no more at all.
66_18_15: The merchants of these things, who became rich by her, will stand at a distance for fear of her torment, weeping and mourning,
66_18_16: and saying, Alas, alas, that great city that was clothed in fine linen, purple, and scarlet, and adorned with gold and precious stones and pearls!
66_18_17: For in one hour such great wealth has been laid waste. And every shipmaster, all who travel by ship, sailors, and as many as trade on the sea, stood at a distance
66_18_18: and cried out when they saw the smoke of her burning, saying, What city was like the great city?
66_18_19: They threw dust on their heads and cried out, weeping and wailing, and saying, Alas, alas, that great city, in which all who had ships on the sea became rich by her wealth! For in one hour she is made desolate.
66_18_20: Rejoice over her, O heaven, and you holy apostles and prophets, for God has avenged you on her!
66_18_21: Then a mighty angel took up a stone like a great millstone and threw it into the sea, saying, Thus with violence the great city Babylon shall be thrown down, and shall not be found anymore.
66_18_22: The sound of harpists, musicians, flutists, and trumpeters shall not be heard in you anymore. No craftsman of any craft shall be found in you anymore, and the sound of a millstone shall not be heard in you anymore.
66_18_23: The light of a lamp shall not shine in you anymore, and the voice of bridegroom and bride shall not be heard in you anymore. For your merchants were the great men of the earth, and by your sorcery all the nations were deceived.
66_18_24: And in her was found the blood of prophets and saints, and of all who were slain on the earth.
66_19_1: After these things I heard a loud voice of a great multitude in heaven, saying, Alleluia! Salvation and glory and honor and power belong to the Lord our God!
66_19_2: For true and righteous are His judgments, because He has judged the great harlot who corrupted the earth with her fornication; and He has avenged on her the blood of His servants shed by her.
66_19_3: Again they said, Alleluia! Her smoke rises up forever and ever!
66_19_4: And the twenty-four elders and the four living creatures fell down and worshiped God who sat on the throne, saying, Amen! Alleluia!
66_19_5: Then a voice came from the throne, saying, Praise our God, all you His servants and those who fear Him, both small and great!
66_19_6: And I heard, as it were, the voice of a great multitude, as the sound of many waters and as the sound of mighty thunderings, saying, Alleluia! For the Lord our God the Almighty reigns!
66_19_7: Let us be glad and rejoice and give Him glory, for the marriage of the Lamb has come, and His wife has made herself ready.
66_19_8: And to her it was granted to be arrayed in fine linen, clean and bright, for the fine linen is the righteous acts of the saints.
66_19_9: Then he said to me, Write: Blessed are those who are called to the marriage supper of the Lamb! And he said to me, These are the true sayings of God.
66_19_10: And I fell at his feet to worship him. But he said to me, 'You must not do that! I am your fellow servant, and of your brethren who hold to the testimony of Jesus. Worship God! For the testimony of Jesus is the spirit of prophecy.'
66_19_11: Now I saw heaven opened, and behold, a white horse! He who sat on it was called Faithful and True, and in righteousness He judges and makes war.
66_19_12: His eyes were like a flame of fire, and on His head were many crowns. He had a name written that no one knows except Himself.
66_19_13: He was clothed with a robe dipped in blood, and His name is called The Word of God.
66_19_14: And the armies in heaven, clothed in fine linen, white and pure, followed Him on white horses.
66_19_15: Now out of His mouth goes a sharp sword, that with it He should strike the nations. And He Himself will rule them with a rod of iron. He treads the winepress of the fierce wrath of Almighty God.
66_19_16: And He has on His robe and on His thigh a name written: KING OF KINGS AND LORD OF LORDS.
66_19_17: Then I saw an angel standing in the sun; and he cried with a loud voice, saying to all the birds that fly in the midst of heaven, 'Come and gather together for the great supper of God,
66_19_18: that you may eat the flesh of kings, the flesh of captains, the flesh of mighty men, the flesh of horses and their riders, and the flesh of all people, both free and slave, both small and great.'
66_19_19: And I saw the beast, the kings of the earth, and their armies gathered together to make war against Him who sat on the horse and against His army.
66_19_20: Then the beast was captured, and with him the false prophet who performed the signs in his presence, by which he deceived those who received the mark of the beast and those who worshiped his image. These two were thrown alive into the lake of fire burning with sulfur.
66_19_21: And the rest were killed with the sword which proceeded from the mouth of Him who sat on the horse. And all the birds were filled with their flesh.
66_20_1: Then I saw an angel coming down from heaven, having the key to the abyss and a great chain in his hand.
66_20_2: He laid hold of the dragon, that ancient serpent, who is the Devil and Satan, and bound him for a thousand years;
66_20_3: and he cast him into the abyss, and shut it and sealed it over him, so that he should deceive the nations no more until the thousand years were finished. After these things he must be released for a little while.
66_20_4: And I saw thrones, and they sat on them, and judgment was committed to them. Then I saw the souls of those who had been beheaded for their testimony of Jesus and for the word of God, who had not worshiped the beast or his image, and had not received his mark on their foreheads or on their hands. They came to life and reigned with Christ for a thousand years.
66_20_5: But the rest of the dead did not come to life until the thousand years were finished. This is the first resurrection.
66_20_6: Blessed and holy is the one who shares in the first resurrection! Over such the second death has no power, but they will be priests of God and of Christ, and they will reign with Him for a thousand years.
66_20_7: Now when the thousand years have expired, Satan will be released from his prison
66_20_8: and will go out to deceive the nations which are in the four corners of the earth, Gog and Magog, to gather them together for battle, their number like the sand of the sea.
66_20_9: They went up on the breadth of the earth and surrounded the camp of the saints and the beloved city. And fire came down from God out of heaven and devoured them.
66_20_10: The devil, who deceived them, was cast into the lake of fire and sulfur where the beast and the false prophet are. And they will be tormented day and night forever and ever.
66_20_11: Then I saw a great white throne and Him who sat on it, from whose face the earth and the heaven fled away. And there was found no place for them.
66_20_12: And I saw the dead, small and great, standing before God, and books were opened. And another book was opened, which is the Book of Life. And the dead were judged according to their works, by the things which were written in the books.
66_20_13: The sea gave up the dead who were in it, and Death and Hades delivered up the dead who were in them. And they were judged, each one according to his works.
66_20_14: Then Death and Hades were cast into the lake of fire. This is the second death.
66_20_15: And anyone not found written in the Book of Life was cast into the lake of fire.
66_21_1: Now I saw a new heaven and a new earth, for the first heaven and the first earth had passed away. Also there was no more sea.
66_21_2: Then I, John, saw the holy city, New Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God, prepared as a bride adorned for her husband.
66_21_3: And I heard a loud voice from heaven saying, 'Behold, the dwelling place of God is with man. He will dwell with them, and they shall be His people. God Himself will be with them and be their God.'
66_21_4: And God will wipe away every tear from their eyes; there shall be no more death, nor sorrow, nor crying, nor pain, for the former things have passed away.
66_21_5: Then He who sat on the throne said, 'Behold, I am making all things new.' And He said to me, 'Write, for these words are trustworthy and true.'
66_21_6: And He said to me, 'It is done! I am the Alpha and the Omega, the Beginning and the End. To the thirsty I will give from the spring of the water of life without cost.'
66_21_7: He who overcomes shall inherit all things, and I will be his God and he shall be My son.
66_21_8: But the cowardly, unbelieving, abominable, murderers, sexually immoral, sorcerers, idolaters, and all liars shall have their part in the lake which burns with fire and sulfur, which is the second death.
66_21_9: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls filled with the seven last plagues came to me and talked with me, saying, 'Come, I will show you the bride, the wife of the Lamb.'
66_21_10: And he carried me away in the Spirit to a great and high mountain, and showed me the holy city, Jerusalem, descending out of heaven from God,
66_21_11: having the glory of God. Her radiance was like a precious jewel, like a jasper stone, clear as crystal.
66_21_12: It had a great and high wall with twelve gates, and at the gates twelve angels, and names written on them, which are the names of the twelve tribes of the sons of Israel.
66_21_13: on the east three gates, on the north three gates, on the south three gates, and on the west three gates.
66_21_14: Now the wall of the city had twelve foundations, and on them were the names of the twelve apostles of the Lamb.
66_21_15: And he who talked with me had a golden measuring rod to measure the city, its gates, and its wall.
66_21_16: The city is laid out as a square; its length is as great as its breadth. And he measured the city with the rod: twelve thousand stadia. Its length, breadth, and height are equal.
66_21_17: Then he measured its wall: one hundred and forty-four cubits, according to human measurement, which is also an angel's measurement.
66_21_18: The construction of its wall was of jasper; and the city was pure gold, like clear glass.
66_21_19: The foundations of the wall of the city were adorned with every kind of jewel: the first was jasper, the second sapphire, the third agate, the fourth emerald,
66_21_20: the fifth sardonyx, the sixth carnelian, the seventh chrysolite, the eighth beryl, the ninth topaz, the tenth chrysoprase, the eleventh jacinth, and the twelfth amethyst.
66_21_21: The twelve gates were twelve pearls; each individual gate was made of a single pearl. And the street of the city was pure gold, like transparent glass.
66_21_22: But I saw no temple in it, for the Lord God Almighty and the Lamb are its temple.
66_21_23: The city had no need of the sun or of the moon to shine in it, for the glory of God illuminated it, and its lamp is the Lamb.
66_21_24: And the nations of those who are saved shall walk in its light, and the kings of the earth will bring their glory into it.
66_21_25: Its gates shall not be shut at all by day, for there will be no night there.
66_21_26: And they shall bring the glory and the honor of the nations into it.
66_21_27: But there shall by no means enter it anything that defiles, or causes an abomination or a lie, but only those whose names are written in the Lamb's Book of Life.
66_22_1: And he showed me a pure river of water of life, clear as crystal, flowing from the throne of God and of the Lamb.
66_22_2: In the middle of its street, and on either side of the river, was the tree of life, which bore twelve kinds of fruit, yielding its fruit every month. The leaves of the tree were for the healing of the nations.
66_22_3: And there shall be no more curse, but the throne of God and of the Lamb shall be in it, and His servants shall serve Him.
66_22_4: They shall see His face, and His name shall be on their foreheads.
66_22_5: There shall be no night there; they need no lamp nor light of the sun, for the Lord God gives them light. And they shall reign forever and ever.
66_22_6: Then he said to me, 'These words are trustworthy and true.' And the Lord God of the holy prophets sent His angel to show His servants the things which must soon take place.
66_22_7: Behold, I am coming quickly! Blessed is he who keeps the words of the prophecy of this book.
66_22_8: Now I, John, saw and heard these things. And when I heard and saw them, I fell down to worship at the feet of the angel who showed me these things.
66_22_9: Then he said to me, See that you do not do that! For I am your fellow servant, and of your brethren the prophets, and of those who keep the words of this book. Worship God.
66_22_10: And he said to me, Do not seal the words of the prophecy of this book, for the time is near.
66_22_11: He who is unjust, let him be unjust still; he who is filthy, let him be filthy still; he who is righteous, let him be righteous still; he who is holy, let him be holy still.
66_22_12: And behold, I am coming quickly, and My reward is with Me, to give to everyone according to his work.
66_22_13: I am the Alpha and the Omega, the Beginning and the End, the First and the Last.
66_22_14: Blessed are those who wash their robes, that they may have the right to the tree of life and may enter through the gates into the city.
66_22_15: But outside are the dogs and sorcerers and the sexually immoral and murderers and idolaters, and everyone who loves and practices falsehood.
66_22_16: I, Jesus, have sent My angel to testify to you these things for the churches. I am the root and descendant of David, the bright morning star.
66_22_17: And the Spirit and the bride say, Come! And let him who hears say, Come! And let him who thirsts come. Whoever desires, let him take the water of life freely.
66_22_18: For I testify to everyone who hears the words of the prophecy of this book: If anyone adds to these things, God will add to him the plagues that are written in this book;
66_22_19: and if anyone takes away from the words of the book of this prophecy, God will take away his part from the tree of life, from the holy city, and from the things which are written in this book.
66_22_20: He who testifies to these things says, Surely I am coming quickly. Amen. Even so, come, Lord Jesus!
66_22_21: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.
40_1_1: The book of the genealogy of Jesus Christ, the Son of David, the Son of Abraham:
40_1_2: Abraham was the father of Isaac, Isaac the father of Jacob, and Jacob the father of Judah and his brothers.
40_1_3: Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah by Tamar, and Perez was the father of Hezron, and Hezron the father of Ram.
40_1_4: Ram was the father of Amminadab, Amminadab the father of Nahshon, and Nahshon the father of Salmon.
40_1_5: Salmon was the father of Boaz by Rahab, Boaz was the father of Obed by Ruth, and Obed was the father of Jesse,
40_1_6: and Jesse fathered King David. David was the father of Solomon by her who had been the wife of Uriah.
40_1_7: Solomon was the father of Rehoboam, Rehoboam the father of Abijah, and Abijah the father of Asa.
40_1_8: Asa was the father of Jehoshaphat, Jehoshaphat the father of Joram, and Joram the father of Uzziah,
40_1_9: Uzziah was the father of Jotham, Jotham the father of Ahaz, and Ahaz the father of Hezekiah,
40_1_10: Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh, Manasseh the father of Amon, and Amon the father of Josiah.
40_1_11: Josiah became the father of Jeconiah and his brothers at the time of the deportation to Babylon.
40_1_12: After the deportation to Babylon, Jeconiah was the father of Shealtiel, and Shealtiel the father of Zerubbabel.
40_1_13: Zerubbabel was the father of Abiud, Abiud the father of Eliakim, and Eliakim the father of Azor,
40_1_14: Azor was the father of Zadok, Zadok the father of Achim, and Achim the father of Eliud,
40_1_15: Eliud was the father of Eleazar, Eleazar the father of Matthan, and Matthan the father of Jacob.
40_1_16: And Jacob fathered Joseph the husband of Mary, who gave birth to Jesus who is called the Christ.
40_1_17: So all the generations from Abraham to David were fourteen generations; and from David until the exile to Babylon, fourteen generations; and from the exile to Babylon until the Christ, fourteen generations.
40_1_18: Now the birth of Jesus Christ came about this way: After his mother Mary had been engaged to Joseph, it was discovered before they came together that she was pregnant from the Holy Spirit.
40_1_19: Then Joseph her husband, being a righteous man and not wanting to disgrace her publicly, decided to divorce her secretly.
40_1_20: But while he thought about these things, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream, saying, 'Joseph, son of David, do not be afraid to take Mary as your wife, for that which is conceived in her is from the Holy Spirit.
40_1_21: And she will give birth to a son, and you are to name him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.
40_1_22: Now all this took place to fulfill what was spoken by the Lord through the prophet:
40_1_23: Behold, the virgin shall conceive and bear a son, and they shall call his name Immanuel, which means, God with us.
40_1_24: When Joseph woke from sleep, he did as the angel of the Lord commanded him and took Mary as his wife,
40_1_25: but did not have sexual relations with her until she gave birth to a son. And he named him Jesus.
40_2_1: Now after Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea in the days of King Herod, wise men from the east arrived in Jerusalem,
40_2_2: saying, 'Where is he who has been born king of the Jews? For we saw his star at its rising and have come to worship him.'
40_2_3: When King Herod heard this, he was deeply disturbed, and all Jerusalem with him.
40_2_4: So he assembled all the chief priests and scribes of the people and asked them where the Christ would be born.
40_2_5: They told him, 'In Bethlehem of Judea, because this is what was written by the prophet:
40_2_6: But you, Bethlehem, in the land of Judah, are by no means least among the rulers of Judah; for out of you shall come a Ruler who will shepherd My people Israel.
40_2_7: Then Herod, when he had secretly called the wise men, determined from them what time the star had appeared.
40_2_8: And he sent them to Bethlehem and said, Go and search diligently for the young Child, and when you have found Him, bring back word to me, that I may come and worship Him also.
40_2_9: When they heard the king, they departed; and behold, the star which they had seen in the East went before them until it came and stood over where the young Child was.
40_2_10: When they saw the star, they rejoiced with exceedingly great joy.
40_2_11: And when they had come into the house, they saw the young Child with Mary His mother, and fell down and worshiped Him. Then, opening their treasures, they presented gifts to Him: gold, frankincense, and myrrh.
40_2_12: Then, being warned in a dream not to return to Herod, they departed for their own country by another way.
40_2_13: Now when they had departed, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream, saying, Arise, take the young Child and His mother, flee to Egypt, and stay there until I bring you word; for Herod will seek the young Child to destroy Him.
40_2_14: When he arose, he took the young Child and His mother by night and departed for Egypt,
40_2_15: and was there until the death of Herod, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the Lord through the prophet, saying, Out of Egypt I called My Son.
40_2_16: Then Herod, when he saw that he was deceived by the wise men, was exceedingly angry; and he sent forth and put to death all the male children who were in Bethlehem and in all its districts, from two years old and under, according to the time which he had determined from the wise men.
40_2_17: Then was fulfilled what was spoken by Jeremiah the prophet, saying:
40_2_18: A voice was heard in Ramah, weeping and great mourning, Rachel weeping for her children, refusing to be comforted, because they are no more.
40_2_19: Now when Herod was dead, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt,
40_2_20: saying, Arise, take the young Child and His mother, and go to the land of Israel, for those who sought the young Child's life are dead.
40_2_21: Then he arose, took the young Child and His mother, and came into the land of Israel.
40_2_22: But when he heard that Archelaus was reigning over Judea instead of his father Herod, he was afraid to go there. And being warned in a dream, he turned aside into the region of Galilee.
40_2_23: And he came and dwelt in a city called Nazareth, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophets, He shall be called a Nazarene.
40_3_1: In those days John the Baptist came preaching in the wilderness of Judea,
40_3_2: and saying, Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand!
40_3_3: For this is he who was spoken of by the prophet Isaiah, saying: The voice of one crying in the wilderness: Prepare the way of the Lord; make His paths straight.
40_3_4: Now John himself was clothed in camel's hair, with a leather belt around his waist; and his food was locusts and wild honey.
40_3_5: Then Jerusalem, all Judea, and all the region around the Jordan went out to him
40_3_6: and were baptized by him in the Jordan, confessing their sins.
40_3_7: But when he saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees coming to his baptism, he said to them, Brood of vipers! Who warned you to flee from the wrath to come?
40_3_8: Therefore bear fruits worthy of repentance,
40_3_9: and do not think to say to yourselves, We have Abraham as our father. For I say to you that God is able to raise up children to Abraham from these stones.
40_3_10: And even now the ax is laid to the root of the trees. Therefore every tree which does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
40_3_11: I indeed baptize you with water for repentance, but He who is coming after me is mightier than I, whose sandals I am not worthy to carry. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.
40_3_12: His winnowing fork is in His hand, and He will thoroughly clean out His threshing floor, and gather His wheat into the barn; but He will burn up the chaff with unquenchable fire.
40_3_13: Then Jesus came from Galilee to John at the Jordan to be baptized by him.
40_3_14: And John tried to prevent Him, saying, 'I need to be baptized by You, and do You come to me?'
40_3_15: But Jesus answered and said to him, 'Permit it to be so now, for thus it is fitting for us to fulfill all righteousness.' Then he allowed Him.
40_3_16: When He had been baptized, Jesus came up immediately from the water; and behold, the heavens were opened to Him, and He saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove and coming to rest on Him.
40_3_17: And suddenly a voice came from heaven, saying, 'This is My beloved Son, with whom I am well pleased.'
40_4_1: Then Jesus was led up by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil.
40_4_2: And when He had fasted forty days and forty nights, afterward He was hungry.
40_4_3: Now when the tempter came to Him, he said, 'If You are the Son of God, command that these stones become bread.'
40_4_4: But He answered and said, 'It is written, 'Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that comes from the mouth of God.'
40_4_5: Then the devil took Him up into the holy city, set Him on the pinnacle of the temple,
40_4_6: and said to Him, 'If You are the Son of God, throw Yourself down. For it is written: 'He will command His angels concerning you, and on their hands they will bear you up, lest you strike your foot against a stone.'
