42_21_24: And they will fall by the edge of the sword, and be led away captive into all nations. And Jerusalem will be trampled by Gentiles until the times of the Gentiles are fulfilled.
42_21_25: "And there will be signs in the sun, in the moon, and in the stars; and on the earth distress of nations, with perplexity, the sea and the waves roaring;
42_21_26: men's hearts failing them from fear and the expectation of those things which are coming on the earth, for the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
42_21_27: Then they will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.
42_21_28: Now when these things begin to happen, look up and lift up your heads, because your redemption draws near.
42_21_29: Then He spoke to them a parable: "Look at the fig tree, and all the trees."
42_21_30: When they are already budding, you see and know for yourselves that summer is now near.
42_21_31: So you also, when you see these things happening, know that the kingdom of God is near.
42_21_32: Assuredly, I say to you, this generation will by no means pass away till all things take place.
42_21_33: Heaven and earth will pass away, but My words will by no means pass away.
42_21_34: But take heed to yourselves, lest your hearts be weighed down with carousing, drunkenness, and cares of this life, and that Day come on you unexpectedly.
42_21_35: For it will come as a snare on all those who dwell on the face of the whole earth.
42_21_36: Watch therefore, and pray always that you may be counted worthy to escape all these things that will come to pass, and to stand before the Son of Man.
42_21_37: And in the daytime He was teaching in the temple, but at night He went out and stayed on the mountain called Olivet.
42_21_38: Then early in the morning all the people came to Him in the temple to hear Him.
42_22_1: Now the Feast of Unleavened Bread drew near, which is called Passover.
42_22_2: And the chief priests and the scribes sought how they might kill Him, for they feared the people.
42_22_3: Then Satan entered Judas, surnamed Iscariot, who was numbered among the twelve.
42_22_4: So he went his way and conferred with the chief priests and captains, how he might betray Him to them.
42_22_5: And they were glad, and agreed to give him money.
42_22_6: So he promised and sought opportunity to betray Him to them in the absence of the multitude.
42_22_7: Then came the Day of Unleavened Bread, when the Passover lamb must be sacrificed.
42_22_8: And He sent Peter and John, saying, "Go and prepare the Passover for us, that we may eat it."
42_22_9: So they said to Him, "Where do You want us to prepare it?"
42_22_10: And He said to them, "Behold, when you have entered the city, a man will meet you carrying a pitcher of water; follow him into the house which he enters."
42_22_11: Then you shall say to the master of the house, 'The Teacher says to you, "Where is the guest room where I may eat the Passover with My disciples?"'
42_22_12: Then he will show you a large, furnished upper room; there make ready."
42_22_13: So they went and found it just as He had said to them, and they prepared the Passover.
42_22_14: When the hour had come, He sat down, and the twelve apostles with Him.
42_22_15: Then He said to them, "With fervent desire I have desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer;"
42_22_16: for I say to you, I will no longer eat of it until it is fulfilled in the kingdom of God."
42_22_17: Then He took the cup, and gave thanks, and said, "Take this and divide it among yourselves;"
42_22_18: for I say to you, I will not drink of the fruit of the vine until the kingdom of God comes."
42_22_19: And He took bread, gave thanks and broke it, and gave it to them, saying, "This is My body which is given for you; do this in remembrance of Me."
42_22_20: Likewise He also took the cup after supper, saying, "This cup is the new covenant in My blood, which is poured out for you."
42_22_21: But behold, the hand of My betrayer is with Me on the table.
42_22_22: And truly the Son of Man goes as it has been determined, but woe to that man by whom He is betrayed!"
42_22_23: Then they began to question among themselves, which of them it was who would do this thing.
42_22_24: Now there was also a dispute among them, as to which of them should be considered the greatest.
42_22_25: And He said to them, "The kings of the Gentiles exercise lordship over them, and those who exercise authority over them are called 'benefactors.'"
42_22_26: But not so among you; on the contrary, he who is greatest among you, let him be as the younger, and he who governs as he who serves.
42_22_27: For who is greater, he who sits at the table, or he who serves? Is it not he who sits at the table? Yet I am among you as the One who serves.
42_22_28: But you are those who have continued with Me in My trials.
42_22_29: And I bestow upon you a kingdom, just as My Father bestowed one upon Me,
42_22_30: that you may eat and drink at My table in My kingdom, and sit on thrones judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
42_22_31: And the Lord said, "Simon, Simon! Indeed, Satan has asked for you, that he may sift you like wheat.
42_22_32: But I have prayed for you, that your faith may not fail; and when you have turned again, strengthen your brethren.
42_22_33: But he said to Him, "Lord, I am ready to go with You, both to prison and to death."
42_22_34: Then He said, "I tell you, Peter, the rooster shall not crow this day before you will deny three times that you know Me."
42_22_35: And He said to them, "When I sent you without money bag, knapsack, and sandals, did you lack anything?" So they said, "Nothing."
42_22_36: Then He said to them, "But now, he who has a money bag, let him take it, and likewise a knapsack; and he who has no sword, let him sell his garment and buy one."
42_22_37: For I say to you that this which is written must still be accomplished in Me: 'And He was numbered with the transgressors.' For the things concerning Me have their fulfillment."
42_22_38: So they said, "Lord, look, here are two swords." And He said to them, "It is enough."
42_22_39: Coming out, He went to the Mount of Olives, as He was accustomed, and His disciples also followed Him.
42_22_40: When He came to the place, He said to them, "Pray that you may not enter into temptation."
42_22_41: And He was withdrawn from them about a stone's throw, and He knelt down and prayed,
42_22_42: saying, "Father, if it is Your will, take this cup away from Me; nevertheless not My will, but Yours, be done."
42_22_43: Then an angel appeared to Him from heaven, strengthening Him.
42_22_44: And being in agony, He prayed more earnestly. Then His sweat became like great drops of blood falling down to the ground.
42_22_45: When He rose up from prayer, and had come to His disciples, He found them sleeping from sorrow.
42_22_46: Then He said to them, "Why do you sleep? Rise and pray, lest you enter into temptation."
42_22_47: And while He was still speaking, behold, a multitude; and he who was called Judas, one of the twelve, went before them and drew near to Jesus to kiss Him.
42_22_48: But Jesus said to him, "Judas, are you betraying the Son of Man with a kiss?"
42_22_49: When those around Him saw what was going to happen, they said to Him, "Lord, shall we strike with the sword?"
42_22_50: And one of them struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his right ear.
42_22_51: But Jesus answered and said, "No more of this." And He touched his ear and healed him.
42_22_52: Then Jesus said to the chief priests, captains of the temple, and the elders who had come to Him, "Have you come out, as against a robber, with swords and clubs?"
42_22_53: When I was with you daily in the temple, you did not try to seize Me. But this is your hour, and the power of darkness."
42_22_54: Having arrested Him, they led Him and brought Him into the high priest's house. But Peter followed at a distance.
42_22_55: Now when they had kindled a fire in the midst of the courtyard and sat down together, Peter sat among them.
42_22_56: And a certain servant girl, seeing him as he sat by the fire, looked intently at him and said, "This man was also with Him."
42_22_57: But he denied Him, saying, "Woman, I do not know Him."
42_22_58: And after a little while another saw him and said, "You also are of them." But Peter said, "Man, I am not!"
42_22_59: Then after about an hour had passed, another confidently affirmed, saying, "Surely this fellow also was with Him, for he is a Galilean."
42_22_60: But Peter said, "Man, I do not know what you are saying!" Immediately, while he was still speaking, the rooster crowed.
42_22_61: And the Lord turned and looked at Peter. Then Peter remembered the word of the Lord, how He had said to him, "Before the rooster crows, you will deny Me three times."
42_22_62: So Peter went out and wept bitterly.
42_22_63: Now the men who held Jesus mocked Him and beat Him.
42_22_64: And having blindfolded Him, they struck Him on the face and asked Him, saying, "Prophesy! Who is the one who struck You?"
42_22_65: And many other things they blasphemously spoke against Him.
42_22_66: As soon as it was day, the elders of the people, both chief priests and scribes, came together and led Him into their council, saying,
42_22_67: "If You are the Christ, tell us." But He said to them, "If I tell you, you will by no means believe."
42_22_68: And if I also ask you, you will by no means answer Me or let Me go."
42_22_69: Hereafter the Son of Man will sit on the right hand of the power of God."
42_22_70: Then they all said, "Are You then the Son of God?" So He said to them, "You rightly say that I am."
42_22_71: And they said, "What further testimony do we need? For we have heard it ourselves from His own mouth."
42_23_1: Then the whole multitude of them arose and led Him to Pilate.
42_23_2: And they began to accuse Him, saying, "We found this fellow perverting the nation, and forbidding to pay taxes to Caesar, saying that He Himself is Christ, a King."
42_23_3: Then Pilate asked Him, saying, "Are You the King of the Jews?" He answered him and said, "It is as you say."
42_23_4: So Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowd, "I find no fault in this Man."
42_23_5: But they were the more fierce, saying, "He stirs up the people, teaching throughout all Judea, beginning from Galilee to this place."
42_23_6: When Pilate heard of Galilee, he asked if the Man were a Galilean.
42_23_7: And as soon as he knew that He belonged to Herod's jurisdiction, he sent Him to Herod, who was also in Jerusalem at that time.
42_23_8: Now when Herod saw Jesus, he was exceedingly glad; for he had desired for a long time to see Him, because he had heard many things about Him, and he was hoping to see some miracle done by Him.
42_23_9: Then he questioned Him with many words, but He answered him nothing.
42_23_10: And the chief priests and scribes stood and vehemently accused Him.
42_23_11: Then Herod, with his men of war, treated Him with contempt and mocked Him, arrayed Him in a gorgeous robe, and sent Him back to Pilate.
42_23_12: That very day Pilate and Herod became friends with each other, for previously they had been at enmity with each other.
42_23_13: Then Pilate, when he had called together the chief priests, the rulers, and the people,
42_23_14: said to them, "You have brought this Man to me, as one who misleads the people. And indeed, having examined Him in your presence, I have found no fault in this Man concerning those things of which you accuse Him;"
42_23_15: no, neither did Herod, for I sent you back to him; and indeed nothing deserving of death has been done by Him."
42_23_16: I will therefore chastise Him and release Him."
42_23_17: (for it was necessary for him to release one to them at the feast).
42_23_18: And they all cried out at once, saying, "Away with this Man, and release to us Barabbas"—
42_23_19: who had been thrown into prison for a certain rebellion made in the city, and for murder.
42_23_20: Pilate, therefore, wishing to release Jesus, again called out to them.
42_23_21: But they shouted, saying, "Crucify Him, crucify Him!"
42_23_22: Then he said to them the third time, "Why, what evil has He done? I have found no reason for death in Him. I will therefore chastise Him and let Him go."
42_23_23: But they were insistent, demanding with loud voices that He be crucified. And the voices of these men and of the chief priests prevailed.
42_23_24: So Pilate gave sentence that it should be as they requested.
42_23_25: And he released to them the one they requested, who for rebellion and murder had been thrown into prison; but he delivered Jesus to their will.
42_23_26: Now as they led Him away, they laid hold of a certain man, Simon a Cyrenian, who was coming from the country, and on him they laid the cross that he might bear it after Jesus.
42_23_27: And a great multitude of the people followed Him, and women who also mourned and lamented Him.
42_23_28: But Jesus, turning to them, said, "Daughters of Jerusalem, do not weep for Me, but weep for yourselves and for your children."
42_23_29: For indeed the days are coming in which they will say, 'Blessed are the barren, wombs that never bore, and breasts which never nursed!'"
42_23_30: Then they will begin to say to the mountains, "Fall on us!" and to the hills, "Cover us!"
42_23_31: For if they do these things in the green wood, what will be done in the dry?"
42_23_32: There were also two others, criminals, led with Him to be put to death.
42_23_33: And when they had come to the place called Calvary, there they crucified Him, and the criminals, one on the right hand and the other on the left.
42_23_34: Then Jesus said, "Father, forgive them, for they do not know what they do." And they divided His garments and cast lots.
42_23_35: And the people stood looking on. But even the rulers with them sneered, saying, "He saved others; let Him save Himself if He is the Christ, the chosen of God."
42_23_36: The soldiers also mocked Him, coming and offering Him sour wine,
42_23_37: and saying, "If You are the King of the Jews, save Yourself."
42_23_38: And an inscription also was written over Him in letters of Greek, Latin, and Hebrew: THIS IS THE KING OF THE JEWS.
42_23_39: Then one of the criminals who were hanged blasphemed Him, saying, "If You are the Christ, save Yourself and us."
42_23_40: But the other, answering, rebuked him, saying, "Do you not even fear God, seeing you are under the same condemnation?"
42_23_41: And we indeed justly, for we receive the due reward of our deeds; but this Man has done nothing wrong."
42_23_42: Then he said to Jesus, "Lord, remember me when You come into Your kingdom."
42_23_43: And Jesus said to him, "Assuredly, I say to you, today you will be with Me in Paradise."
42_23_44: Now it was about the sixth hour, and darkness fell over all the earth until the ninth hour.
42_23_45: Then the sun was darkened, and the veil of the temple was torn in two.
42_23_46: And when Jesus had cried out with a loud voice, He said, "Father, 'into Your hands I commit My spirit.'" Having said this, He breathed His last.
42_23_47: So when the centurion saw what had happened, he glorified God, saying, "Certainly this was a righteous Man!"
42_23_48: And the whole crowd who came together to that sight, seeing what had been done, beat their breasts and returned.
42_23_49: But all His acquaintances, and the women who followed Him from Galilee, stood at a distance, watching these things.
42_23_50: Now behold, there was a man named Joseph, a council member, a good and righteous man.
42_23_51: He had not consented to their decision and deed. He was from Arimathea, a city of the Jews, who himself was also waiting for the kingdom of God.
42_23_52: This man went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus.
42_23_53: Then he took it down, wrapped it in linen, and laid it in a tomb that was hewn out of the rock, where no one had ever lain before.
42_23_54: That day was the Preparation, and the Sabbath drew near.
42_23_55: And the women who had come with Him from Galilee followed after, and they observed the tomb and how His body was laid.
42_23_56: Then they returned and prepared spices and fragrant oils. And they rested on the Sabbath according to the commandment.
42_24_1: Now on the first day of the week, very early in the morning, they, and certain other women with them, came to the tomb bringing the spices which they had prepared.
42_24_2: But they found the stone rolled away from the tomb.
42_24_3: Then they went in and did not find the body of the Lord Jesus.
42_24_4: And it happened, as they were greatly perplexed about this, that behold, two men stood by them in shining garments.
42_24_5: Then, as they were afraid and bowed their faces to the earth, they said to them, "Why do you seek the living among the dead?"
42_24_6: He is not here, but is risen! Remember how He spoke to you when He was still in Galilee,
42_24_7: saying, 'The Son of Man must be delivered into the hands of sinful men, and be crucified, and the third day rise again.'"
42_24_9: Then they returned from the tomb and told all these things to the eleven and to all the rest.
42_24_10: It was Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the other women with them, who told these things to the apostles.
42_24_11: And their words seemed to them like idle tales, and they did not believe them.
42_24_12: But Peter arose and ran to the tomb; and stooping down, he saw the linen cloths lying by themselves; and he departed, marveling to himself at what had happened.
42_24_13: Now behold, two of them were traveling that same day to a village called Emmaus, which was seven miles from Jerusalem.
42_24_14: And they talked together of all these things which had happened.
42_24_15: So it was, while they conversed and reasoned, that Jesus Himself drew near and went with them.
42_24_16: But their eyes were kept from recognizing Him.
42_24_17: And He said to them, "What kind of conversation is this that you have with one another as you walk and are sad?"
42_24_18: Then the one whose name was Cleopas answered and said to Him, "Are You the only stranger in Jerusalem, and have You not known the things which happened there in these days?"
42_24_19: And He said to them, "What things?" So they said to Him, "The things concerning Jesus of Nazareth, who was a Prophet mighty in deed and word before God and all the people,"
42_24_20: and how the chief priests and our rulers delivered Him to be condemned to death, and crucified Him.
42_24_21: But we were hoping that it was He who was going to redeem Israel. Indeed, besides all this, today is the third day since these things happened.
42_24_22: Yes, and certain women of our company, who arrived at the tomb early, astonished us.
42_24_23: When they did not find His body, they came saying that they had also seen a vision of angels who said He was alive.
42_24_24: And certain of those who were with us went to the tomb and found it just as the women had said; but Him they did not see.
42_24_25: Then He said to them, 'O foolish ones, and slow of heart to believe in all that the prophets have spoken!'
42_24_26: Ought not the Christ to have suffered these things and to enter into His glory?
42_24_27: And beginning at Moses and all the Prophets, He explained to them in all the Scriptures the things concerning Himself.
42_24_28: Then they drew near to the village where they were going, and He acted as if He were going farther.
42_24_29: But they urged Him, saying, 'Abide with us, for it is toward evening, and the day is far spent.' And He went in to stay with them.
42_24_30: Now it came to pass, as He sat at the table with them, that He took bread, blessed and broke it, and gave it to them.
42_24_31: Then their eyes were opened and they recognized Him; and He vanished from their sight.
42_24_32: And they said to one another, 'Did not our heart burn within us while He talked with us on the road, and while He opened the Scriptures to us?'
42_24_33: So they rose up that very hour and returned to Jerusalem, and found the eleven and those who were with them gathered together,
42_24_34: saying, 'The Lord has risen indeed, and has appeared to Simon!'
42_24_35: And they told what had happened on the road, and how He was known to them in the breaking of bread.
42_24_36: Now as they said these things, Jesus Himself stood in the midst of them, and said to them, 'Peace to you.'
42_24_37: But they were startled and terrified, and supposed they had seen a spirit.
42_24_38: And He said to them, 'Why are you troubled? And why do doubts arise in your hearts?'
42_24_39: Behold My hands and My feet, that it is I Myself. Touch Me and see, for a spirit does not have flesh and bones as you see I have.'
42_24_40: When He had said this, He showed them His hands and His feet.
42_24_41: But while they still did not believe for joy, and marveled, He said to them, 'Have you any food here?'
42_24_42: So they gave Him a piece of broiled fish and some honeycomb.
42_24_43: And He took it and ate in their presence.
42_24_44: Then He said to them, 'These are the words which I spoke to you while I was still with you, that all things must be fulfilled which were written in the Law of Moses and the Prophets and the Psalms concerning Me.'
42_24_45: And He opened their minds to understand the Scriptures.
42_24_46: Then He said to them, 'Thus it is written, and thus it was necessary for the Christ to suffer and to rise from the dead the third day,'
42_24_47: and that repentance and remission of sins should be proclaimed in His name to all nations, beginning at Jerusalem.
42_24_48: And you are witnesses of these things.
42_24_49: Behold, I send the Promise of My Father upon you; but stay in the city of Jerusalem until you are clothed with power from on high.'
42_24_50: And He led them out as far as Bethany, and lifting up His hands He blessed them.
42_24_51: Now it came to pass, while He blessed them, that He was parted from them and was carried up into heaven.
42_24_52: And they worshiped Him, and returned to Jerusalem with great joy,
42_24_53: and were continually in the temple praising and blessing God.
42_10_11: 'Even the dust of your city which clings to our feet we wipe off against you. Nevertheless know this, that the kingdom of God has come near you.'
42_10_12: But I say to you that it will be more tolerable in that Day for Sodom than for that city.
42_10_13: Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the mighty works which were done in you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.
42_10_14: But it will be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon at the judgment than for you.
42_10_15: And you, Capernaum, who are exalted to heaven, will be brought down to Hades.
42_10_16: He who hears you hears Me, he who rejects you rejects Me, and he who rejects Me rejects Him who sent Me.
42_10_17: Then the seventy returned with joy, saying, "Lord, even the demons are subject to us in Your name."
42_10_18: And He said to them, "I saw Satan fall from heaven like lightning."
42_10_19: Behold, I give you the authority to trample on serpents and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy, and nothing at all shall hurt you.
42_10_20: Nevertheless do not rejoice in this, that the spirits are subject to you, but rather rejoice because your names are written in heaven.
42_10_21: In that hour Jesus rejoiced in the Holy Spirit and said, "I thank You, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that You have hidden these things from the wise and prudent and revealed them to babes. Yes, Father, for so it pleased You to do it this way."
42_10_22: All things have been delivered to Me by My Father, and no one knows who the Son is except the Father, and who the Father is except the Son, and anyone to whom the Son wills to reveal Him.
42_10_23: Then turning to His disciples He said privately, "Blessed are the eyes that see the things you see;"
42_10_24: for I tell you that many prophets and kings have desired to see what you see, and have not seen it, and to hear what you hear, and have not heard it.
42_10_25: And behold, a certain lawyer stood up and tested Him, saying, "Teacher, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?"
42_10_26: He said to him, "What is written in the law? How do you read it?"
42_10_27: So he answered and said, "You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, with all your strength, and with all your mind, and your neighbor as yourself."
42_10_28: And He said to him, "You have answered correctly; do this and you will live."
42_10_29: But he, wanting to justify himself, said to Jesus, "And who is my neighbor?"
42_10_30: Then Jesus answered and said: "A certain man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, and fell among robbers, who stripped him and beat him, and departed, leaving him half dead."
42_10_31: Now by chance a certain priest came down that road, and when he saw him, he passed by on the other side.
42_10_32: Likewise a Levite, when he arrived at the place and saw him, came and passed by on the other side.
42_10_33: But a certain Samaritan, as he journeyed, came where he was. And when he saw him, he had compassion.
42_10_34: So he went to him and bandaged his wounds, pouring on oil and wine; and he set him on his own animal, brought him to an inn, and took care of him.
42_10_35: On the next day, when he departed, he took out two denarii and gave them to the innkeeper, and said to him, 'Take care of him; and whatever more you spend, when I come again, I will repay you.'
42_10_36: So which of these three do you think proved to be a neighbor to him who fell among the thieves?
42_10_37: And he said, "The one who showed mercy toward him." Then Jesus said to him, "Go and do likewise."
42_10_38: Now as they went on their way, He entered a certain village; and a woman named Martha welcomed Him into her home.
42_10_39: And she had a sister called Mary, who also sat at Jesus' feet and listened to His word.
42_10_40: But Martha was distracted with much serving, and she came to Him and said, "Lord, do You not care that my sister has left me to serve alone? Therefore tell her to help me."
42_10_41: And Jesus answered and said to her, "Martha, Martha, you are worried and troubled about many things."
42_10_42: But one thing is necessary, and Mary has chosen that good part, which will not be taken away from her.
42_11_1: Now it came to pass, as Jesus was praying in a certain place, when He finished, that one of His disciples said to Him, "Lord, teach us to pray, as John also taught his disciples."
42_11_2: So He said to them, "When you pray, say: Father in heaven, hallowed be Your name. Your kingdom come. Your will be done on earth as it is in heaven."
42_11_3: Give us each day our daily bread.
42_11_4: And forgive us our sins, for we ourselves also forgive everyone who is indebted to us. And do not lead us into temptation, but deliver us from the evil one.
42_11_5: And He said to them, "Which of you shall have a friend and go to him at midnight and say to him, 'Friend, lend me three loaves;
42_11_6: for a friend of mine has come to me on his journey, and I have nothing to set before him';
42_11_7: and he will answer from within and say, 'Do not bother me; the door is now shut, and my children are with me in bed; I cannot rise and give to you'?
42_11_8: I say to you, though he will not rise and give to him because he is his friend, yet because of his shameless persistence he will rise and give him as many as he needs.
43_1_1: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
43_1_2: He was in the beginning with God.
43_1_3: All things were made through Him, and without Him nothing was made that was made.
43_1_4: In Him was life, and that life was the light of men.
43_1_5: And the light shines in the darkness, and the darkness has not overcome it.
43_1_6: There was a man sent from God, whose name was John.
43_1_7: This man came as a witness, to bear witness about the light, so that all might believe through him.
43_1_8: He was not the light, but he came to testify about the light.
43_1_9: The true light, which gives light to everyone, was coming into the world.
43_1_10: He was in the world, and the world was made through Him, yet the world did not know Him.
43_1_11: He came to His own, and His own people did not receive him.
43_1_12: But to all who did receive him, who believed in his name, he gave the right to become children of God.
43_1_13: who were born, not of blood, nor of the will of the flesh, nor of the will of man, but of God.
43_1_14: And the Word became flesh and dwelt among us, and we have seen his glory, the glory as the one and only Son from the Father, full of grace and truth.
43_1_15: John bore witness about him and cried out, saying, “This was he of whom I said, ‘He who comes after me ranks before me, because he was before me.’”
43_1_16: For from his fullness we have all received, grace upon grace.
43_1_17: For the law was given through Moses; grace and truth came through Jesus Christ.
43_1_18: No one has ever seen God; the one and only Son, who is himself God and is at the Father’s side — he has revealed him.
43_1_19: Now this is the testimony of John, when the Jews sent priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, “Who are you?”
43_1_20: He confessed, and did not deny, but confessed, “I am not the Messiah.”
43_1_21: And they asked him, “What then? Are you Elijah?” He said, “I am not.” “Are you the Prophet?” And he answered, “No.”
43_1_22: Then they said to him, “Who are you, that we may give an answer to those who sent us? What do you say about yourself?”
43_1_23: He said: “I am the voice of one crying in the wilderness: ‘Make straight the way of the Lord,’ as the prophet Isaiah said.”
43_1_24: Now they had been sent from the Pharisees.
43_1_25: And they asked him, saying, “Why then do you baptize if you are not the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the Prophet?”
43_1_26: John answered them, saying, “I baptize with water, but among you stands one you do not know.
43_1_27: It is he who, coming after me, is preferred before me, whose sandal strap I am not worthy to untie.”
43_1_28: These things took place in Bethany across the Jordan, where John was baptizing.
43_1_29: The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him, and said, “Behold, the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world!
43_1_30: This is he of whom I said, ‘After me comes a man who ranks before me, because he was before me.’
43_1_31: I did not recognize him, but for this purpose I came baptizing with water, that he might be revealed to Israel.”
43_1_32: And John bore witness, saying, “I saw the Spirit descending from heaven like a dove, and he remained upon him.
43_1_33: I did not know him, but he who sent me to baptize with water said to me, ‘Upon whom you see the Spirit descending and remaining on him, this is he who baptizes with the Holy Spirit.’
43_1_34: And I have seen and testified that this is the Son of God.”
43_1_35: Again, the next day, John was standing with two of his disciples.
43_1_36: And looking at Jesus as he walked, he said, “Behold, the Lamb of God!”
43_1_37: The two disciples heard him say this, and they followed Jesus.
43_1_38: Then Jesus turned, and seeing them following, said to them, “What do you seek?” They said to him, “Rabbi” (which means Teacher), “where are you staying?”
43_1_39: He said to them, “Come and see.” They came and saw where he was staying, and remained with him that day, for it was about the tenth hour.
43_1_40: One of the two who heard John speak and followed him was Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother.
43_1_41: He first found his own brother Simon and said to him, “We have found the Messiah” (which means Christ).
43_1_42: And he brought him to Jesus. When Jesus looked at him, he said, “You are Simon the son of John. You shall be called Cephas” (which is translated Peter).
43_1_43: The next day Jesus decided to go to Galilee, and he found Philip and said to him, “Follow me.”
43_1_44: Now Philip was from Bethsaida, the hometown of Andrew and Peter.
43_1_45: Philip found Nathanael and said to him, “We have found him of whom Moses in the Law and also the prophets wrote—Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph.”
43_1_46: And Nathanael said to him, “Can anything good come out of Nazareth?” Philip said to him, “Come and see.”
43_1_47: Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him and said of him, “Behold, an Israelite indeed, in whom there is no deceit!”
43_1_48: Nathanael said to him, “How do you know me?” Jesus answered and said to him, “Before Philip called you, when you were under the fig tree, I saw you.”
43_1_49: Nathanael answered and said to him, “Rabbi, you are the Son of God! You are the King of Israel!”
43_1_50: Jesus answered and said to him, “Because I said to you, ‘I saw you under the fig tree,’ do you believe? You will see greater things than these.”
43_1_51: And he said to him, “Most assuredly, I say to you, you will see heaven opened, and the angels of God ascending and descending on the Son of Man.”
43_2_1: On the third day a wedding took place in Cana of Galilee, and Jesus’s mother was there.
43_2_2: Jesus and his disciples were invited to the wedding as well.
43_2_3: When the wine ran out, the mother of Jesus told him, “They have no wine.”
43_2_4: Jesus said to her, “Woman, what does that have to do with me? My hour has not yet come.”
43_2_5: His mother said to the servants, “Do whatever he tells you.”
43_2_6: Now there were six stone water jars there for the Jewish rites of purification, each holding twenty or thirty gallons.
43_2_7: Jesus said to them, “Fill the jars with water.” So they filled them to the brim.
43_2_8: And he said to them, “Now draw some out and take it to the master of the feast.” And they did.
43_2_9: When the master of the feast tasted the water now become wine, and did not know where it came from (though the servants who had drawn the water knew), the master of the feast called the bridegroom.
43_2_10: And he said to him, “Everyone serves the good wine first, and when people have drunk freely, then the inferior. But you have kept the good wine until now.”
43_2_11: This, the first of his signs, Jesus did at Cana in Galilee, and manifested his glory. And his disciples believed in him.
43_2_12: After this, he went down to Capernaum, together with his mother, his brothers, and his disciples, and they stayed there only a few days.
43_2_13: The Jewish Passover was near, and so Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
43_2_14: In the temple he found people selling oxen, sheep, and doves, and he also found the money changers sitting there.
43_2_15: After making a whip out of cords, he drove everyone out of the temple with their sheep and oxen. He also poured out the money changers’ coins and overturned the tables.
43_2_16: He told those who were selling doves, “Get these things out of here! Stop turning my Father’s house into a marketplace!”
43_2_17: Then his disciples remembered that it was written, “Zeal for your house will consume me.”
43_2_18: So the Jews replied to him, “What sign will you show us for doing these things?”
43_2_19: Jesus answered, “Destroy this temple, and I will raise it up in three days.”
43_2_20: Therefore the Jews said, “This temple took forty-six years to build, and will you raise it up in three days?”
43_2_21: But he was speaking about the temple of his body.
43_2_22: So when he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered that he had said this, and they believed the Scripture and the statement Jesus had made.
43_2_23: While he was in Jerusalem during the Passover Festival, many believed in his name when they saw the signs he was doing.
43_2_24: Jesus, however, would not entrust himself to them, since he knew them all.
43_2_25: and because he did not need anyone to testify about man; for he himself knew what was in man.
43_3_1: There was a man from the Pharisees named Nicodemus, a ruler of the Jews.
43_3_2: This man came to him at night and said, “Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher who has come from God, for no one could perform these signs you do unless God were with him.”
43_3_3: Jesus replied, “Truly I tell you, unless someone is born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God.”
43_3_4: Nicodemus said to him, “How can anyone be born when he is old? Can he enter his mother’s womb a second time and be born?”
43_3_5: Jesus answered, “Truly, truly, I say to you, unless one is born of water and the Spirit, he cannot enter the kingdom of God.
43_3_6: That which is born of the flesh is flesh, and that which is born of the Spirit is spirit.
43_3_7: Do not be amazed that I said to you, ‘You must be born again.’
43_3_8: The wind blows where it pleases, and you hear its sound, but you don’t know where it comes from or where it is going. So it is with everyone born of the Spirit.”
43_3_9: Nicodemus answered and said to Him, “How can these things be?”
43_3_10: Jesus answered and said to him, “Are you the teacher of Israel and yet you do not understand these things?
43_3_11: Truly, truly, I say to you, we speak what we know and testify to what we have seen, but you do not accept our testimony.
43_3_12: If I have told you earthly things and you do not believe, how will you believe if I tell you heavenly things?
43_3_13: No one has ascended into heaven except he who descended from heaven, the Son of Man.
43_3_14: And as Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, so must the Son of Man be lifted up,
43_3_15: that whoever believes in Him should not perish but have eternal life.
43_3_16: For God so loved the world that He gave His only begotten Son, that whoever believes in Him should not perish but have everlasting life.
43_3_17: For God did not send His Son into the world to condemn the world, but to save the world through Him.
43_3_18: He who believes in Him is not condemned; but he who does not believe is already condemned, because he has not believed in the name of the only begotten Son of God.
43_3_19: And this is the judgment: the light has come into the world, and people loved darkness rather than the light because their deeds were evil.
43_3_20: For everyone who does evil hates the light and avoids it, so that his deeds may not be exposed.
43_3_21: But whoever does what is true comes to the light, so that it may be clearly seen that his works have been accomplished by God.
43_3_22: After this, Jesus and His disciples went to the Judean countryside, where He spent time with them and baptized.
43_3_23: Now John also was baptizing in Aenon near Salim, because there was plenty of water there, and people were coming and being baptized.
43_3_24: For John had not yet been thrown into prison.
43_3_25: Then a dispute arose between some of John’s disciples and a Jew about purification.
43_3_26: And they came to John and said to him, “Rabbi, he who was with you across the Jordan, to whom you bore witness — look, he is baptizing, and everyone is going to him.”
43_3_27: John answered and said, “A person cannot receive even one thing unless it is given him from heaven.
43_3_28: You yourselves can testify that I said, ‘I am not the Messiah, but I’ve been sent ahead of him.’
43_3_29: He who has the bride is the groom. But the groom’s friend, who stands by and listens for him, rejoices greatly at the groom’s voice. So this joy of mine is complete.
43_3_30: He must increase, but I must decrease.
43_3_31: He who comes from above is above all; he who is of the earth belongs to the earth and speaks in an earthly way. He who comes from heaven is above all.
43_3_32: And what He has seen and heard, that He testifies; and yet no one receives His testimony.
43_3_33: Whoever receives his testimony sets his seal to this, that God is true.
43_3_34: For He whom God has sent speaks the words of God, for He gives the Spirit without measure.
43_3_35: The Father loves the Son and has given all things into His hand.
43_3_36: He who believes in the Son has everlasting life; but he who does not obey the Son will not see life, but the wrath of God remains on him.
43_4_1: Therefore, when the Lord knew that the Pharisees had heard that Jesus was making and baptizing more disciples than John,
43_4_2: (though Jesus Himself did not baptize, but His disciples),
43_4_3: He left Judea and went again to Galilee.
43_4_4: But He had to pass through Samaria.
43_4_5: So He came to a town of Samaria called Sychar, near the property that Jacob had given his son Joseph.
43_4_6: Now Jacob’s well was there, and Jesus, worn out from his journey, sat down at the well. It was about noon.
43_4_7: A woman of Samaria came to draw water. “Give me a drink,” Jesus said to her,
43_4_8: because His disciples had gone into town to buy food.
43_4_9: Then the woman of Samaria said to Him, “How is it that You, being a Jew, ask a drink from me, a Samaritan woman?” For Jews have no dealings with Samaritans.
43_4_10: Jesus answered and said to her, “If you knew the gift of God, and who it is who says to you, ‘Give Me a drink,’ you would have asked Him, and He would have given you living water.”
43_4_11: The woman said to Him, “Sir, You have nothing to draw with, and the well is deep. Where then do You get that living water?
43_4_12: Are You greater than our father Jacob, who gave us the well, and drank from it himself, as well as his sons and his livestock?”
43_4_13: Jesus answered and said to her, “Everyone who drinks of this water will be thirsty again,
43_4_14: but whoever drinks of the water that I shall give him will never thirst. The water that I shall give him will become in him a fountain of water springing up into everlasting life.”
43_4_15: The woman said to Him, “Sir, give me this water, that I may not thirst, nor come here to draw.”
43_4_16: Jesus said to her, “Go, call your husband, and come here.”
43_4_17: The woman answered and said, “I have no husband.” Jesus said to her, “You have well said, ‘I have no husband,’
43_4_18: for you have had five husbands, and the one whom you now have is not your husband; in that you spoke truly.”
43_4_19: The woman said to Him, “Sir, I perceive that You are a prophet.
43_4_20: Our fathers worshiped on this mountain, and you Jews say that in Jerusalem is the place where one ought to worship.”
43_4_21: Jesus said to her, “Woman, believe Me, the hour is coming when you will neither on this mountain, nor in Jerusalem, worship the Father.
43_4_22: You worship what you do not know; we worship what we know, for salvation is from the Jews.
43_4_23: But the hour is coming, and now is, when the true worshipers will worship the Father in spirit and truth; for the Father is seeking such people to worship him.
43_4_24: God is spirit, and those who worship Him must worship in spirit and truth.”
43_4_25: The woman said to Him, “I know that Messiah is coming” (who is called Christ). “When He comes, He will tell us all things.”
43_4_26: Jesus said to her, “I who speak to you am He.”
43_4_27: And at this point His disciples came, and they marveled that He talked with a woman; yet no one said, “What do You seek?” or, “Why are You talking with her?”
43_4_28: The woman then left her waterpot, went her way into the city, and said to the men,
43_4_29: “Come, see a Man who told me all things that I ever did. Could this be the Christ?”
43_4_30: Then they went out of the city and came to Him.
43_4_31: In the meantime His disciples urged Him, saying, “Rabbi, eat.”
43_4_32: But He said to them, “I have food to eat of which you do not know.”
43_4_33: Therefore the disciples said to one another, “Has anyone brought Him anything to eat?”
43_4_34: Jesus said to them, “My food is to do the will of Him who sent Me, and to finish His work.
43_4_35: Do you not say, ‘There are still four months and then comes the harvest’? Behold, I say to you, lift up your eyes and look at the fields, for they are already white for harvest!
43_4_36: And he who reaps receives wages, and gathers fruit for eternal life, that both he who sows and he who reaps may rejoice together.
43_4_37: For in this the saying is true: ‘One sows and another reaps.’
43_4_38: I sent you to reap that for which you have not labored; others have labored, and you have entered into their labors.”
43_4_39: And many of the Samaritans of that city believed in Him because of the word of the woman who testified, “He told me all that I ever did.”
43_4_40: So when the Samaritans had come to Him, they urged Him to stay with them; and He stayed there two days.
43_4_41: And many more believed because of His own word.
43_4_42: Then they said to the woman, “Now we believe, not because of what you said, for we ourselves have heard Him and we know that this is indeed the Christ, the Savior of the world.”
43_4_43: Now after the two days He departed from there and went to Galilee.
43_4_44: For Jesus Himself testified that a prophet has no honor in his own country.
43_4_45: So when He came to Galilee, the Galileans received Him, having seen all the things He did in Jerusalem at the feast; for they also had gone to the feast.
43_4_46: So Jesus came again to Cana of Galilee where He had made the water wine. And there was a certain nobleman whose son was sick at Capernaum.
43_4_47: When he heard that Jesus had come out of Judea into Galilee, he went to Him and implored Him to come down and heal his son, for he was at the point of death.
43_4_48: Then Jesus said to him, “Unless you people see signs and wonders, you will by no means believe.”
43_4_49: The nobleman said to Him, “Sir, come down before my child dies!”
43_4_50: Jesus said to him, “Go your way; your son will live.” So the man believed the word that Jesus spoke to him and went his way.
43_4_51: And as he was now going down, his servants met him and told him, saying, “Your son is alive!”
43_4_52: Then he inquired of them the hour when he began to get better. And they said to him, “Yesterday at the seventh hour the fever left him.”
43_4_53: So the father knew that it was at the same hour in which Jesus said to him, “Your son will live.” And he himself believed, along with his whole household.
43_4_54: This was now the second sign that Jesus did when He had come from Judea to Galilee.
43_5_1: After this, a Jewish festival took place, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
43_5_2: Now there is in Jerusalem by the Sheep Gate a pool, called Bethesda in Aramaic, which has five colonnades.
43_5_3: In these lay a large number of the disabled — blind, lame, and paralyzed, waiting for the moving of the water.
43_5_4: For an angel of the Lord went down at certain seasons into the pool and stirred up the water; whoever then first, after the stirring up of the water, stepped in was made well from whatever disease with which he was afflicted.
43_5_5: Now a certain man was there who had been disabled for thirty-eight years.
43_5_6: When Jesus saw him lying there and realized he had already been there a long time, he said to him, “Do you want to get well?”
43_5_7: The sick man answered Him, “Sir, I have no one to put me into the pool when the water is stirred up, but while I am coming, another steps down before me.”
43_5_8: Jesus said to him, “Get up, pick up your mat and walk.”
43_5_9: And immediately the man was made well, picked up his mat, and started to walk. Now that day was the Sabbath.
43_5_10: So the Jews said to the man who had been healed, “It is the Sabbath, and it is not lawful for you to take up your bed.”
43_5_11: He answered them, “The man who made me well said to me, ‘Pick up your mat and walk.’”
43_5_12: Then they asked him, “Who is the man who said to you, ‘Take up your bed and walk’?”
43_5_13: But the man who was healed did not know who it was, because Jesus had slipped away into the crowd that was there.
43_5_14: After this, Jesus found him in the temple and said to him, “See, you are well! Sin no more, that nothing worse may happen to you.”
43_5_15: The man went away and told the Jews that it was Jesus who had made him well.
43_5_16: Therefore, the Jews began persecuting Jesus because he was doing these things on the Sabbath.
43_5_17: But Jesus answered them, “My Father is working until now, and I am working.”
43_5_18: This is why the Jews began trying all the more to kill him: Not only was he breaking the Sabbath, but he was even calling God his own Father, making himself equal to God.
43_5_19: Then Jesus answered and said to them, “Truly, truly, I say to you, the Son can do nothing of Himself, but what He sees the Father doing; for whatever He does, the Son also does in like manner.
43_5_20: For the Father loves the Son and shows him everything he is doing, and he will show him greater works than these so that you will be amazed.
43_5_21: For just as the Father raises the dead and gives them life, so also the Son gives life to whom he wishes.
43_5_22: For the Father judges no one but has given all judgment to the Son,
43_5_23: so that all may honor the Son, just as they honor the Father. Whoever does not honor the Son does not honor the Father who sent him.
43_5_24: “Truly, truly, I say to you, whoever hears my word and believes him who sent me has eternal life and will not come under judgment but has passed from death to life.
43_5_25: “Truly I tell you, an hour is coming, and is now here, when the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God, and those who hear will live.
43_5_26: For just as the Father has life in himself, so also he has granted to the Son to have life in himself.
43_5_27: And he has granted him the right to pass judgment, because he is the Son of Man.
43_5_28: Do not be amazed at this, because a time is coming when all who are in the graves will hear his voice
43_5_29: and come out — those who have done good things, to the resurrection of life, but those who have done wicked things, to the resurrection of condemnation.
43_5_30: I can do nothing on my own. I judge only as I hear, and my judgment is just, because I do not seek my own will, but the will of him who sent me.
43_5_31: “If I testify about myself, my testimony is not true.
43_5_32: There is another who testifies about me, and I know that the testimony he gives about me is true.
43_5_33: You sent messengers to John, and he testified to the truth.
43_5_34: Not that the testimony that I receive is from man, but I say these things so that you may be saved.
43_5_35: He was a burning and shining lamp, and you were willing to rejoice for a while in his light.
43_5_36: But the testimony that I have is greater than that of John, for the works that the Father has given me to accomplish, the very works that I am doing, bear witness about me that the Father has sent me.
43_5_37: And the Father who sent me has himself borne witness about me; you have neither heard his voice at any time, nor seen his form.
43_5_38: But you do not have his word abiding in you, for you do not believe the one whom he has sent.
43_5_39: You search the Scriptures because you think that in them you have eternal life; and it is they that bear witness about me.
43_5_40: But you are not willing to come to me so that you may have life.
43_5_41: I do not accept glory from people.
43_5_42: But I know you — that you have no love for God within you.
43_5_43: I have come in my Father’s name, and yet you don’t accept me; if someone else comes in his own name, you will accept him.
43_5_44: How can you believe, since you accept glory from one another but don’t seek the glory that comes from the only God?
43_5_45: Do not think that I will accuse you to the Father; your accuser is Moses, on whom you have set your hope.
43_5_46: For if you believed Moses, you would believe me, because he wrote about me.
43_5_47: But if you don’t believe what he wrote, how will you believe my words?
43_6_1: After this, Jesus went away to the other side of the Sea of Galilee, which is the Sea of Tiberias.
43_6_2: A large crowd was following him because they saw the signs that he was performing by healing the sick.
43_6_3: Jesus went up a mountain and sat down there with his disciples.
43_6_4: Now the Passover, a Jewish festival, was near.
43_6_5: So when Jesus looked up and noticed a huge crowd coming toward him, he asked Philip, “Where will we buy bread so that these people can eat?”
43_6_6: He asked this to test him, for he himself knew what he was going to do.
43_6_7: Philip answered him, “Two hundred denarii worth of bread wouldn’t be enough for each of them to have a little.”
43_6_8: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, said to him,
43_6_9: “There’s a boy here who has five barley loaves and two fish — but what are they for so many?”
43_6_10: Jesus said, “Have the people sit down.” There was plenty of grass in that place; so they sat down. The men numbered about five thousand.
43_6_11: Then Jesus took the loaves, and after giving thanks he distributed them to those who were seated — so also with the fish, as much as they wanted.
43_6_12: When they were full, he told his disciples, “Collect the leftovers so that nothing is wasted.”
43_6_13: So they collected them and filled twelve baskets with the pieces from the five barley loaves that were left over by those who had eaten.
43_6_14: When the people saw the sign he had done, they said, “This truly is the Prophet who is to come into the world.”
43_6_15: Therefore, when Jesus realized that they were about to come and take him by force to make him king, he withdrew again to the mountain by himself.
43_6_16: When evening came, his disciples went down to the sea,
43_6_17: got into a boat, and started across the sea to Capernaum. Darkness had already set in, but Jesus had not yet come to them.
43_6_18: A high wind arose, and the sea began to churn.
43_6_19: After they had rowed about three or four miles, they saw Jesus walking on the sea. He was coming near the boat, and they were afraid.
43_6_20: But he said to them, “It is I; don’t be afraid.”
43_6_21: Then they were willing to take him on board, and at once the boat was at the shore where they were heading.
43_6_22: The next day, the crowd that had stayed on the other side of the sea saw there had been only one boat. They also saw that Jesus had not boarded the boat with his disciples, but that his disciples had gone off alone.
43_6_23: Some boats from Tiberias came near the place where they had eaten the bread after the Lord had given thanks.
43_6_24: When the crowd saw that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they got into the boats and went to Capernaum looking for Jesus.
43_6_25: When they found him on the other side of the sea, they said to him, “Rabbi, when did you get here?”
43_6_26: Jesus answered, “Truly I tell you, you are looking for me, not because you saw the signs, but because you ate the loaves and were filled.
43_6_27: Don’t work for the food that perishes but for the food that lasts for eternal life, which the Son of Man will give you, because God the Father has set his seal of approval on him.
43_6_28: Then they said to Him, “What must we do, that we may work the works of God?”
43_6_29: Jesus answered and said to them, “This is the work of God, that you believe in Him whom He has sent.”
43_6_30: Therefore they said to Him, “What sign will You perform then, that we may see it and believe You? What work will You do?
43_6_31: Our fathers ate the manna in the wilderness; as it is written, ‘He gave them bread from heaven to eat.’”
43_6_32: Then Jesus said to them, “Most assuredly, I say to you, it was not Moses who gave you the bread from heaven, but My Father gives you the true bread from heaven.
43_6_33: For the bread of God is He who comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.”
43_6_34: Then they said to Him, “Sir, give us this bread always.”
43_6_35: And Jesus said to them, “I am the bread of life; whoever comes to Me shall never hunger, and whoever believes in Me shall never thirst.
43_6_36: But I said to you that you have seen Me and yet do not believe.
43_6_37: All that the Father gives Me will come to Me, and the one who comes to Me I will never cast out.
43_6_38: For I have come down from heaven, not to do My own will, but the will of Him who sent Me.
43_6_39: This is the will of the Father who sent Me, that of all He has given Me I should lose nothing, but should raise it up at the last day.
43_6_40: And this is the will of Him who sent Me, that everyone who sees the Son and believes in Him may have everlasting life; and I will raise him up at the last day.”
43_6_41: The Jews then complained about Him, because He said, “I am the bread which came down from heaven.”
43_6_42: And they said, “Is not this Jesus, the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? How is it then that He says, ‘I have come down from heaven’?”
43_6_43: Jesus therefore answered and said to them, “Do not grumble among yourselves.
43_6_44: No one can come to Me unless the Father who sent Me draws him; and I will raise him up at the last day.
43_6_45: It is written in the prophets, ‘And they shall all be taught by God.’ Therefore everyone who has heard and learned from the Father comes to Me.
43_6_46: Not that anyone has seen the Father, except He who is from God; He has seen the Father.
43_6_47: Most assuredly, I say to you, he who believes in Me has everlasting life.
43_6_48: I am the bread of life.
43_6_49: Your fathers ate the manna in the wilderness, and they died.
43_6_50: This is the bread which comes down from heaven, that one may eat of it and not die.
43_6_51: I am the living bread which came down from heaven. If anyone eats of this bread, he will live forever; and the bread that I shall give is My flesh, which I shall give for the life of the world.”
43_6_52: The Jews therefore quarreled among themselves, saying, “How can this Man give us His flesh to eat?”
43_6_53: Then Jesus said to them, “Most assuredly, I say to you, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink His blood, you have no life in you.
43_6_54: Whoever eats My flesh and drinks My blood has eternal life, and I will raise him up at the last day.
43_6_55: For My flesh is true food, and My blood is true drink.
43_6_56: He who eats My flesh and drinks My blood abides in Me, and I in him.
43_6_57: As the living Father sent Me, and I live because of the Father, so he who feeds on Me will live because of Me.
43_6_58: This is the bread which came down from heaven—not as your fathers ate the manna, and are dead. He who eats this bread will live forever.”
43_6_59: These things He said in the synagogue as He taught in Capernaum.
43_6_60: Therefore many of His disciples, when they heard this, said, “This is a hard saying; who can understand it?”
43_6_61: When Jesus knew in Himself that His disciples complained about this, He said to them, “Does this offend you?
43_6_62: What then if you should see the Son of Man ascend where He was before?
43_6_63: It is the Spirit who gives life; the flesh profits nothing. The words that I speak to you are spirit, and they are life.
43_6_64: But there are some of you who do not believe.” For Jesus knew from the beginning who they were who did not believe, and who would betray Him.
43_6_65: And He said, “Therefore I have said to you that no one can come to Me unless it has been granted to him by My Father.”
43_6_66: From that time many of His disciples went back and walked with Him no more.
43_6_67: Then Jesus said to the twelve, “Do you also want to go away?”
43_6_68: But Simon Peter answered Him, “Lord, to whom shall we go? You have the words of eternal life.
43_6_69: Also we have come to believe and know that You are the Holy One of God.”
43_6_70: Jesus answered them, “Did I not choose you, the twelve? And yet one of you is a devil?”
43_6_71: He spoke of Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot, for it was he who would betray Him, being one of the twelve.
43_7_1: After these things Jesus walked in Galilee; for He did not want to walk in Judea, because the Jews were seeking to kill Him.
43_7_2: Now the Jews' Feast of Booths was at hand.
43_7_3: His brothers therefore said to Him, “Depart from here and go into Judea, that Your disciples also may see the works that You are doing.
43_7_4: For no one works in secret if he seeks to be known openly. If You do these things, show Yourself to the world.”
43_7_5: For not even His brothers believed in Him.
43_7_6: Then Jesus said to them, “My time has not yet come, but your time is always ready.
43_7_7: The world cannot hate you, but it hates Me because I testify about it that its works are evil.
43_7_8: You go up to this feast. I am not yet going up to this feast, for My time has not yet fully come.”
43_7_9: When He had said these things to them, He remained in Galilee.
43_7_10: But when His brothers had gone up, then He also went up to the feast, not openly, but as it were in secret.
43_7_11: Then the Jews sought Him at the feast, and said, “Where is He?”
43_7_12: And there was much murmuring among the people concerning Him. Some said, “He is a good man”; others said, “No, on the contrary, He deceives the people.”
43_7_13: However, no one spoke openly of Him for fear of the Jews.
43_7_14: Now about the middle of the feast Jesus went up into the temple and taught.
43_7_15: And the Jews marveled, saying, “How does this Man know letters, having never studied?”
43_7_16: Jesus answered them and said, “My doctrine is not Mine, but His who sent Me.
43_7_17: If anyone's will is to do God's will, he shall know concerning the doctrine, whether it is from God or whether I speak on My own authority.
43_7_18: He who speaks from himself seeks his own glory; but He who seeks the glory of the One who sent Him is true, and no unrighteousness is in Him.
43_7_19: Did not Moses give you the law, yet none of you keeps the law? Why do you seek to kill Me?”
43_7_20: The people answered and said, “You have a demon. Who is seeking to kill You?”
43_7_21: Jesus answered and said to them, “I did one work, and you all marvel.
43_7_22: Moses therefore gave you circumcision (not that it is from Moses, but from the fathers), and you circumcise a man on the Sabbath.
43_7_23: If a man receives circumcision on the Sabbath, so that the law of Moses should not be broken, are you angry with Me because I made a man completely well on the Sabbath?
43_7_24: Do not judge according to appearance, but judge with righteous judgment.”
43_7_25: Now some of them from Jerusalem said, “Is this not He whom they seek to kill?
43_7_26: But look! He speaks boldly, and they say nothing to Him. Do the rulers know indeed that this is truly the Christ?
43_7_27: However, we know where this Man is from; but when the Christ comes, no one knows where He is from.”
43_7_28: Then Jesus cried out, as He taught in the temple, saying, “You both know Me, and you know where I am from; and I have not come of Myself, but He who sent Me is true, whom you do not know.
43_7_29: But I know Him, for I am from Him, and He sent Me.”
43_7_30: Therefore they sought to take Him; but no one laid a hand on Him, because His hour had not yet come.
43_7_31: And many of the people believed in Him, and said, “When the Christ comes, will He do more signs than these which this Man has done?”
43_7_32: The Pharisees heard the crowd murmuring these things concerning Him, and the Pharisees and the chief priests sent officers to take Him.
43_7_33: Then Jesus said to them, “I shall be with you a little while longer, and then I go to Him who sent Me.
43_7_34: You will seek Me and not find Me, and where I am you cannot come.”
43_7_35: Then the Jews said among themselves, “Where does He intend to go that we shall not find Him? Does He intend to go to the Dispersion among the Greeks and teach the Greeks?
43_7_36: What is this thing that He said, ‘You will seek Me and not find Me, and where I am you cannot come’?
43_7_37: On the last day, that great day of the feast, Jesus stood and cried out, saying, “If anyone thirsts, let him come to me and drink.
43_7_38: He who believes in me, as the Scripture has said, out of his heart will flow rivers of living water.”
43_7_39: But this he spoke concerning the Spirit, whom those believing in him would receive; for the Holy Spirit was not yet given, because Jesus was not yet glorified.
43_7_40: Therefore many from the crowd, when they heard this saying, said, “Truly this is the Prophet.”
43_7_41: Others said, “This is the Christ.” But some said, “Will the Christ come out of Galilee?
43_7_42: Has not the Scripture said that the Christ comes from the seed of David and from the town of Bethlehem, where David was?”
43_7_43: So there was a division among the people because of him.
43_7_44: Now some of them wanted to take him, but no one laid hands on him.
43_7_45: Then the officers came to the chief priests and Pharisees, who said to them, “Why have you not brought him?”
43_7_46: The officers answered, “No man ever spoke like this man!”
43_7_47: Then the Pharisees answered them, “Are you also deceived?
43_7_48: Have any of the rulers or the Pharisees believed in him?
43_7_49: But this crowd that does not know the law is accursed.”
43_7_50: Nicodemus, who came to Jesus by night, being one of them, said to them,
43_7_51: “Does our law judge a man before it hears him and knows what he is doing?”
43_7_52: They answered and said to him, “Are you also from Galilee? Search and look, for no prophet has arisen out of Galilee.”
43_7_53: And everyone went to his own house.
43_8_1: But Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
43_8_2: Now early in the morning he came again into the temple, and all the people came to him; and he sat down and taught them.
43_8_3: Then the scribes and Pharisees brought to him a woman caught in adultery. And when they had set her in the midst,
43_8_4: they said to him, “Teacher, this woman was caught in adultery, in the very act.
43_8_5: Now Moses, in the law, commanded us that such should be stoned. But what do you say?”
43_8_6: This they said, testing him, that they might have something of which to accuse him. But Jesus stooped down and wrote on the ground with his finger, as though he did not hear.
43_8_7: So when they continued asking him, he raised himself up and said to them, “He who is without sin among you, let him throw a stone at her first.”
43_8_8: And again he stooped down and wrote on the ground.
43_8_9: Then those who heard it, being convicted by their conscience, went out one by one, beginning with the oldest even to the last. And Jesus was left alone, and the woman standing in the midst.
43_8_10: When Jesus had raised himself up and saw no one but the woman, he said to her, “Woman, where are those accusers of yours? Has no one condemned you?”
43_8_11: She said, “No one, Lord.” And Jesus said to her, “Neither do I condemn you; go and sin no more.”
43_8_12: Then Jesus spoke to them again, saying, “I am the light of the world. He who follows me shall not walk in darkness, but have the light of life.”
43_8_13: The Pharisees therefore said to him, “You bear witness of yourself; your witness is not true.”
43_8_14: Jesus answered and said to them, “Even if I bear witness of myself, my witness is true, for I know where I came from and where I am going; but you do not know where I come from and where I am going.
43_8_15: You judge according to the flesh; I judge no one.
43_8_16: And yet if I do judge, my judgment is true; for I am not alone, but I am with the Father who sent me.
43_8_17: It is also written in your law that the testimony of two men is true.
43_8_18: I am one who bears witness of myself, and the Father who sent me bears witness of me.”
43_8_19: Then they said to him, “Where is your Father?” Jesus answered, “You know neither me nor my Father. If you had known me, you would have known my Father also.”
43_8_20: These words Jesus spoke in the treasury, as he taught in the temple; and no one laid hands on him, for his hour had not yet come.
43_8_21: Then Jesus said to them again, “I am going away, and you will seek me, and will die in your sin. Where I go you cannot come.”
43_8_22: So the Jews said, “Will he kill himself, because he says, ‘Where I go you cannot come’?”
43_8_23: And he said to them, “You are from beneath; I am from above. You are of this world; I am not of this world.
43_8_24: Therefore I said to you that you will die in your sins; for unless you believe that I am he, you will die in your sins.
43_8_25: Then they said to him, “Who are you?” And Jesus said to them, “Just what I have been telling you from the beginning.
43_8_26: I have many things to say and to judge concerning you, but he who sent me is true; and I speak to the world those things which I heard from him.
43_8_27: They did not understand that he had been speaking to them about the Father.
43_8_28: Then Jesus said to them, “When you lift up the Son of Man, then you will know that I am he, and that I do nothing on my own authority, but just as the Father taught me, I say these things.
43_8_29: And he who sent me is with me. The Father has not left me alone, for I always do the things that are pleasing to him.
43_8_30: As he was saying these things, many believed in him.
43_8_31: Then Jesus said to those Jews who believed him, “If you continue in my word, you are truly my disciples.
43_8_32: And you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.”
43_8_33: They answered him, “We are offspring of Abraham and have never been enslaved to anyone. How is it that you say, ‘You will become free’?
43_8_34: Jesus answered them, “Truly, truly, I say to you, everyone who practices sin is a slave to sin.
43_8_35: And a slave does not remain in the house forever, but a son remains forever.
43_8_36: Therefore if the Son sets you free, you will be free indeed.
43_8_37: I know that you are Abraham’s descendants, but you seek to kill me, because my word finds no place in you.
43_8_38: I speak what I have seen in the presence of the Father; so then, you do what you have heard from your father.
43_8_39: They answered and said to him, “Abraham is our father.” Jesus said to them, “If you were Abraham’s children, you would do the works of Abraham.
43_8_40: But now you seek to kill me, a man who has told you the truth that I heard from God. Abraham did not do this.
43_8_41: You are doing the works your father did.” They said to him, “We were not born of sexual immorality; we have one Father — even God.
43_8_42: Jesus said to them, “If God were your Father, you would love me, for I came from God and I am here. I did not come on my own, but he sent me.
43_8_43: Why do you not understand what I say? It is because you cannot bear to hear my word.
43_8_44: You are of your father the devil, and your will is to do your father's desires. He was a murderer from the beginning and does not stand in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he lies, he speaks out of his own character, for he is a liar and the father of lies.
43_8_45: Yet because I tell the truth, you do not believe me.
43_8_46: Which one of you convicts me of sin? If I am telling the truth, why don’t you believe me?
43_8_47: The one who is from God listens to God’s words. This is why you don’t listen, because you are not from God.
43_8_48: Then the Jews answered and said to him, “Are we not right in saying that you are a Samaritan and have a demon?”
43_8_49: Jesus answered, “I do not have a demon; but I honor my Father, and you dishonor me.
43_8_50: I do not seek my own glory; there is one who seeks it and judges.
43_8_51: Truly, truly, I say to you, if anyone keeps my word, he will never see death.
43_8_52: Then the Jews said to him, “Now we know that you have a demon! Abraham died, as did the prophets, yet you say, ‘If anyone keeps my word, he will never taste death.’
43_8_53: Are you greater than our father Abraham who died? And the prophets died. Who do you claim to be?
43_8_54: Jesus answered, “If I glorify myself, my glory is nothing. It is my Father who glorifies me, of whom you say, ‘He is our God.’
43_8_55: But you do not know him; I know him. If I were to say that I do not know him, I would be a liar like you, but I do know him and keep his word.
43_8_56: Your father Abraham rejoiced to see my day; he saw it and was glad.
43_8_57: Then the Jews said to him, “You are not yet fifty years old, and have you seen Abraham?”
43_8_58: Jesus said to them, “Truly, truly, I say to you, before Abraham was, I am.”
43_8_59: Then they took up stones to throw at him; but Jesus hid himself and went out of the temple.
43_9_1: Now as Jesus passed by, he saw a man who was blind from birth.
43_9_2: And his disciples asked him, “Rabbi, who sinned, this man or his parents, that he was born blind?”
43_9_3: Jesus answered, “Neither this man nor his parents sinned, but that the works of God should be revealed in him.
43_9_4: We must work the works of him who sent me while it is day; night is coming when no one can work.
43_9_5: As long as I am in the world, I am the light of the world.
43_9_6: When He had said these things, He spat on the ground and made mud with the saliva; and He anointed the eyes of the blind man with the mud.
43_9_7: And He said to him, “Go, wash in the pool of Siloam” (which means Sent). So he went and washed, and came back seeing.
43_9_8: Therefore the neighbors and those who had seen him before as a beggar said, “Isn’t this the one who used to sit and beg?”
43_9_9: Some said, “This is he.” Others said, “No, but he is like him.” He said, “I am the man.”
43_9_10: Therefore they said to him, “How were your eyes opened?”
43_9_11: He answered and said, “A man called Jesus made mud and anointed my eyes and said to me, ‘Go to the pool of Siloam and wash.’ So I went and washed, and I received sight.”
43_9_12: Then they said to him, “Where is He?” He said, “I do not know.”
43_9_13: They brought to the Pharisees the man who had formerly been blind.
43_9_14: Now it was a Sabbath when Jesus made the mud and opened his eyes.
43_9_15: Then the Pharisees also asked him again how he had received his sight. He said to them, “He put mud on my eyes, and I washed, and I see.”
43_9_16: Therefore some of the Pharisees said, “This man is not from God, because He does not keep the Sabbath.” Others said, “How can a man who is a sinner do such signs?” And there was a division among them.
43_9_17: They said to the blind man again, “What do you say about Him because He opened your eyes?” He said, “He is a prophet.”
43_9_18: But the Jews did not believe concerning him, that he had been blind and received his sight, until they called the parents of the man who had received his sight.
43_9_19: And they asked them, saying, “Is this your son, who you say was born blind? How then does he now see?”
43_9_20: His parents answered them and said, “We know that this is our son, and that he was born blind;
43_9_21: but how he now sees we do not know, or who opened his eyes we do not know. He is of age; ask him. He will speak for himself.”
43_9_22: His parents said these things because they feared the Jews, for the Jews had already agreed that if anyone confessed Him to be Christ, he would be put out of the synagogue.
43_9_23: Therefore his parents said, “He is of age; ask him.”
43_9_24: So they again called the man who was blind, and said to him, “Give glory to God! We know that this man is a sinner.”
43_9_25: He answered and said, “Whether He is a sinner or not I do not know. One thing I know: that though I was blind, now I see.”
43_9_26: Then they said to him again, “What did He do to you? How did He open your eyes?”
43_9_27: He answered them, “I told you already, and you did not listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you also want to become His disciples?”
43_9_28: Then they reviled him and said, “You are His disciple, but we are disciples of Moses.
43_9_29: We know that God spoke to Moses; but as for this man, we do not know where He is from.”
43_9_30: The man answered and said to them, “Why, this is a marvelous thing, that you do not know where He is from; yet He has opened my eyes!
43_9_31: Now we know that God does not hear sinners; but if anyone is a worshiper of God and does His will, He hears him.
43_9_32: Since the world began it has been unheard of that anyone opened the eyes of one who was born blind.
43_9_33: If this man were not from God, he could do nothing.”
43_9_34: They answered and said to him, “You were completely born in sins, and are you teaching us?” And they cast him out.
43_9_35: Jesus heard that they had cast him out; and when He had found him, He said to him, “Do you believe in the Son of Man?”
43_9_36: He answered and said, “Who is He, Lord, that I may believe in Him?”
43_9_37: And Jesus said to him, “You have both seen Him and it is He who is talking with you.”
43_9_38: Then he said, “Lord, I believe!” And he worshiped Him.
43_9_39: And Jesus said, “For judgment I have come into this world, that those who do not see may see, and that those who see may be made blind.”
43_9_40: Then some of the Pharisees who were with Him heard these words, and said to Him, “Are we blind also?”
43_9_41: Jesus said to them, “If you were blind, you would have no sin; but now you say, ‘We see.’ Therefore your sin remains.
43_10_1: “Most assuredly, I say to you, he who does not enter the sheepfold by the door, but climbs up some other way, the same is a thief and a robber.
43_10_2: But he who enters by the door is the shepherd of the sheep.
43_10_3: To him the gatekeeper opens, and the sheep hear his voice; and he calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
43_10_4: And when he has brought out all his own sheep, he goes before them; and the sheep follow him, for they know his voice.
43_10_5: Yet they will by no means follow a stranger, but will flee from him, for they do not know the voice of strangers.
43_10_6: Jesus used this figure of speech, but they did not understand what he was saying to them.
43_10_7: Then Jesus said to them again, “Truly, truly, I say to you, I am the door of the sheep.
43_10_8: All who came before Me are thieves and robbers, but the sheep did not listen to them.
43_10_9: I am the door. If anyone enters by Me, he will be saved and will go in and out and find pasture.
43_10_10: The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy. I have come that they may have life and have it abundantly.
43_10_11: I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
43_10_12: But a hired hand, who is not the shepherd and does not own the sheep, sees the wolf coming and leaves the sheep and flees; and the wolf snatches them and scatters them.
43_10_13: The hired hand flees because he is a hired hand and cares nothing for the sheep.
43_10_14: I am the good shepherd; and I know My sheep, and am known by My own.
43_10_15: Just as the Father knows Me, even so I know the Father; and I lay down My life for the sheep.
43_10_16: And I have other sheep that are not of this fold; I must bring them also, and they will listen to my voice; and there will be one flock, one shepherd.
43_10_17: Therefore My Father loves Me, because I lay down My life that I may take it again.
43_10_18: No one takes it from Me, but I lay it down of My own accord. I have authority to lay it down, and I have authority to take it up again. This command I have received from My Father.
43_10_19: Therefore there was a division again among the Jews because of these words.
43_10_20: And many of them said, “He has a demon and is mad. Why do you listen to Him?”
43_10_21: Others said, “These are not the words of one who is oppressed by a demon. Can a demon open the eyes of the blind?”
43_10_22: Now it was the Feast of Dedication in Jerusalem, and it was winter.
43_10_23: And Jesus was walking in the temple, in Solomon’s Colonnade.
43_10_24: Then the Jews surrounded Him and said to Him, “How long do You keep us in suspense? If You are the Messiah, tell us plainly.”
43_10_25: Jesus answered them, “I told you, and you do not believe. The works that I do in My Father’s name bear witness about Me.
