43_10_26: But you do not believe because you are not among My sheep, as I said to you.
43_10_27: My sheep hear My voice, and I know them, and they follow Me.
43_10_28: And I give them eternal life, and they shall never perish; no one will snatch them out of My hand.
43_10_29: My Father, who has given them to Me, is greater than all; and no one is able to snatch them out of My Father’s hand.
43_10_30: I and My Father are one.
43_10_31: Then the Jews took up stones again to stone Him.
43_10_32: Jesus answered them, “I have shown you many good works from the Father; for which of them are you going to stone Me?”
43_10_33: The Jews answered Him, “We are not stoning you for a good work, but for blasphemy, because you, being a man, make yourself God.”
43_10_34: Jesus answered them, “Is it not written in your Law, ‘I said, you are gods’?
43_10_35: If he called them gods to whom the word of God came—and Scripture cannot be broken—
43_10_36: do you say of Him whom the Father sanctified and sent into the world, ‘You are blaspheming,’ because I said, ‘I am the Son of God’?
43_10_37: If I do not do the works of My Father, do not believe Me;
43_10_38: but if I do them, even though you do not believe Me, believe the works, that you may know and understand that the Father is in Me, and I in the Father.”
43_10_39: Therefore they sought again to seize Him, but He escaped out of their hand.
43_10_40: And He went away again beyond the Jordan to the place where John had been baptizing at first, and there He stayed.
43_10_41: Then many came to Him and said, “John performed no sign, but everything that John said about this man was true.”
43_10_42: And many believed in Him there.
43_11_1: Now a certain man was ill, Lazarus of Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha.
43_11_2: It was Mary who anointed the Lord with fragrant oil and wiped His feet with her hair, whose brother Lazarus was sick.
43_11_3: So the sisters sent to Him, saying, “Lord, behold, he whom You love is sick.”
43_11_4: When Jesus heard that, He said, “This sickness is not unto death, but for the glory of God, that the Son of God may be glorified through it.”
43_11_5: Now Jesus loved Martha, her sister, and Lazarus.
43_11_6: So when He heard that he was sick, He stayed two more days in the place where He was.
43_11_7: Then after this He said to the disciples, “Let us go to Judea again.”
43_11_8: The disciples said to Him, “Rabbi, lately the Jews sought to stone You, and are You going there again?”
43_11_9: Jesus answered, “Are there not twelve hours in the day? If anyone walks in the day, he does not stumble, because he sees the light of this world.
43_11_10: But if anyone walks in the night, he stumbles, because the light is not in him.”
43_11_11: These things He said, and after that He said to them, “Our friend Lazarus sleeps, but I go that I may wake him up.”
43_11_12: Then His disciples said, “Lord, if he sleeps he will get well.”
43_11_13: However, Jesus spoke of his death, but they thought that He was speaking about taking rest in sleep.
43_11_14: Then Jesus said to them plainly, “Lazarus is dead.
43_11_15: And I am glad for your sakes that I was not there, that you may believe. Nevertheless let us go to him.”
43_11_16: Then Thomas, who is called the Twin, said to his fellow disciples, “Let us also go, that we may die with Him.”
43_11_17: So when Jesus came, He found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb four days.
43_11_18: Now Bethany was near Jerusalem, about two miles away.
43_11_19: And many of the Jews had joined the women around Martha and Mary, to comfort them concerning their brother.
43_11_20: Now Martha, as soon as she heard that Jesus was coming, went and met Him, but Mary was sitting in the house.
43_11_21: Now Martha said to Jesus, “Lord, if You had been here, my brother would not have died.
43_11_22: But even now I know that whatever You ask of God, God will give You.”
43_11_23: Jesus said to her, “Your brother will rise again.”
43_11_24: Martha said to Him, “I know that he will rise again in the resurrection at the last day.”
43_11_25: Jesus said to her, “I am the resurrection and the life. He who believes in Me, though he may die, he shall live.
43_11_26: And whoever lives and believes in Me shall never die. Do you believe this?”
43_11_27: She said to Him, “Yes, Lord, I believe that You are the Christ, the Son of God, who is to come into the world.”
43_11_28: And when she had said these things, she went her way and secretly called Mary her sister, saying, “The Teacher has come and is calling for you.”
43_11_29: As soon as she heard that, she arose quickly and came to Him.
43_11_30: Now Jesus had not yet come into the town, but was in the place where Martha met Him.
43_11_31: Then the Jews who were with her in the house, and comforting her, when they saw that Mary rose up quickly and went out, followed her, saying, “She is going to the tomb to weep there.”
43_11_32: Then, when Mary came where Jesus was, and saw Him, she fell down at His feet, saying to Him, “Lord, if You had been here, my brother would not have died.”
43_11_33: Therefore, when Jesus saw her weeping, and the Jews who came with her weeping, He groaned in the spirit and was troubled.
43_11_34: And He said, “Where have you laid him?” They said to Him, “Lord, come and see.”
43_11_36: Then the Jews said, “See how He loved him!”
43_11_37: And some of them said, “Could not this Man, who opened the eyes of the blind, also have kept this man from dying?”
43_11_38: Then Jesus, again groaning in Himself, came to the tomb. It was a cave, and a stone lay against it.
43_11_39: Jesus said, “Take away the stone.” Martha, the sister of him who was dead, said to Him, “Lord, by this time there is a stench, for he has been dead four days.”
43_11_40: Jesus said to her, “Did I not say to you that if you would believe you would see the glory of God?”
43_11_41: Then they took away the stone from the place where the dead man was lying. And Jesus lifted up His eyes and said, “Father, I thank You that You have heard Me.
43_11_42: And I know that You always hear Me, but because of the people who are standing by I said this, that they may believe that You sent Me.
43_11_43: Now when He had said these things, He cried with a loud voice, “Lazarus, come out!”
43_11_44: And the man who had died came out bound hand and foot with graveclothes, and his face was wrapped with a cloth. Jesus said to them, “Unbind him, and let him go.”
43_11_45: Then many of the Jews who had come to Mary, and had seen what Jesus did, believed in Him.
43_11_46: But some of them went to the Pharisees and told them what Jesus had done.
43_11_47: Then the chief priests and the Pharisees gathered a council and said, “What are we to do? For this man performs many signs.
43_11_48: If we let Him alone like this, everyone will believe in Him, and the Romans will come and take away both our place and our nation.”
43_11_49: And one of them, Caiaphas, who was high priest that year, said to them, “You know nothing at all,
43_11_50: nor do you understand that it is better for you that one man should die for the people, not that the whole nation should perish.”
43_11_51: Now this he did not say on his own authority; but being high priest that year he prophesied that Jesus would die for the nation,
43_11_52: and not for that nation only, but also to gather into one the children of God who are scattered abroad.
43_11_53: Then, from that day on, they plotted to put Him to death.
43_11_54: Therefore Jesus no longer walked openly among the Jews, but went from there to the region near the wilderness, to a town called Ephraim, and there he stayed with His disciples.
43_11_55: And the Passover of the Jews was near, and many went up from the country to Jerusalem before the Passover to purify themselves.
43_11_56: Then they sought Jesus, and spoke among themselves as they stood in the temple, “What do you think? He will not come to the feast, will he?”
43_11_57: Now both the chief priests and the Pharisees had given a command, that if anyone knew where He was, he should report it, that they might seize Him.
43_12_1: Then, six days before the Passover, Jesus came to Bethany, where Lazarus was, whom Jesus had raised from the dead.
43_12_2: There they made Him a supper; and Martha served, but Lazarus was one of those reclining at the table with Him.
43_12_3: Then Mary took a pound of very costly oil of spikenard, anointed the feet of Jesus, and wiped His feet with her hair. And the house was filled with the fragrance of the perfume.
43_12_4: But one of His disciples, Judas Iscariot, who was about to betray Him, said,
43_12_5: “Why was this fragrant oil not sold for three hundred denarii and given to the poor?”
43_12_6: This he said, not that he cared for the poor, but because he was a thief, and having charge of the moneybag he used to steal what was put into it.
43_12_7: But Jesus said, “Leave her alone; she has kept this for the day of My burial.
43_12_8: For the poor you always have with you, but you do not always have Me.”
43_12_9: Now a great many of the Jews knew that He was there; and they came, not for Jesus’ sake only, but that they might also see Lazarus, whom He had raised from the dead.
43_12_10: But the chief priests plotted to put Lazarus to death also,
43_12_11: because on account of him many of the Jews were going away and believing in Jesus.
43_12_12: The next day a great multitude that had come to the feast, when they heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem,
43_12_13: took branches of palm trees and went out to meet Him, and cried out: “Hosanna! Blessed is He who comes in the name of the LORD! The King of Israel!”
43_12_14: Then Jesus, when He had found a young donkey, sat on it; as it is written:
43_12_15: “Fear not, daughter of Zion; behold, your King is coming, sitting on a donkey’s colt.”
43_12_16: His disciples did not understand these things at first; but when Jesus was glorified, then they remembered that these things had been written about Him and that they had done these things to Him.
43_12_17: Therefore the people, who were with Him when He called Lazarus out of his tomb and raised him from the dead, bore witness.
43_12_18: For this reason the people also met Him, because they heard that He had done this sign.
43_12_19: The Pharisees therefore said among themselves, “You see that you are accomplishing nothing. Look, the world has gone after Him!”
43_12_20: Now there were certain Greeks among those who came up to worship at the feast.
43_12_21: Then they came to Philip, who was from Bethsaida in Galilee, and asked him, saying, “Sir, we wish to see Jesus.”
43_12_22: Philip went and told Andrew; then Andrew and Philip went and told Jesus.
43_12_23: But Jesus answered them, saying, “The hour has come for the Son of Man to be glorified.
43_12_24: Most assuredly, I say to you, unless a grain of wheat falls into the ground and dies, it remains alone; but if it dies, it produces much grain.
43_12_25: He who loves his life will lose it, and he who hates his life in this world will keep it for eternal life.
43_12_26: If anyone serves Me, let him follow Me; and where I am, there My servant will be also. If anyone serves Me, the Father will honor him.
43_12_27: Now My soul is troubled, and what shall I say? ‘Father, save Me from this hour’? But for this purpose I came to this hour.
43_12_28: Father, glorify Your name.” Then a voice came from heaven, saying, “I have glorified it, and I will glorify it again.
43_12_29: Therefore the people who stood by and heard it said that it had thundered. Others said, “An angel has spoken to Him.
43_12_30: Jesus answered and said, “This voice has come for your sake, not mine.
43_12_31: Now is the judgment of this world; now the ruler of this world will be cast out.
43_12_32: And I, if I am lifted up from the earth, will draw all people to Myself.
43_12_33: This He said, signifying by what kind of death He would die.
43_12_34: The people answered Him, “We have heard from the law that the Christ remains forever; and how can You say, ‘The Son of Man must be lifted up’? Who is this Son of Man?
43_12_35: Then Jesus said to them, “A little while longer the light is with you. Walk while you have the light, lest darkness overtake you; the one who walks in darkness does not know where he is going.
43_12_36: While you have the light, believe in the light, that you may become children of light.” These things Jesus spoke, and departed, and was hidden from them.
43_12_37: But although He had done so many signs before them, they did not believe in Him.
43_12_38: that the word of Isaiah the prophet might be fulfilled, which he spoke: “Lord, who has believed our report? And to whom has the arm of the LORD been revealed?
43_12_39: Therefore they could not believe, because Isaiah said again:
43_12_40: He has blinded their eyes and hardened their hearts, lest they should see with their eyes, lest they should understand with their hearts and turn, so that I should heal them.
43_12_41: These things Isaiah said when he saw His glory and spoke of Him.
43_12_42: Nevertheless, many even of the authorities believed in him, but for fear of the Pharisees they did not confess it, so that they would not be put out of the synagogue.
43_12_43: for they loved the glory that comes from man more than the glory that comes from God.
43_12_44: Then Jesus cried out and said, “He who believes in Me believes not in Me but in Him who sent Me.
43_12_45: And he who sees Me sees Him who sent Me.
43_12_46: I have come as a light into the world, that whoever believes in Me should not abide in darkness.
43_12_47: And if anyone hears My words and does not keep them, I do not judge him; for I did not come to judge the world but to save the world.
43_12_48: He who rejects Me, and does not receive My words, has that which judges him—the word that I have spoken will judge him in the last day.
43_12_49: For I have not spoken on My own authority; but the Father who sent Me has given Me a command, what I should say and what I should speak.
43_12_50: And I know that His command is everlasting life. Therefore, whatever I speak, just as the Father has told Me, so I speak.
43_13_1: Now before the Feast of the Passover, when Jesus knew that His hour had come that He should depart from this world to the Father, having loved His own who were in the world, He loved them to the end.
43_13_2: And supper being ended, the devil having already put it into the heart of Judas Iscariot, Simon’s son, to betray Him.
43_13_3: Jesus, knowing that the Father had given all things into His hands, and that He had come from God and was going to God.
43_13_4: rose from supper and laid aside His garments, took a towel and girded Himself.
43_13_5: After that, He poured water into a basin and began to wash the disciples’ feet, and to wipe them with the towel with which He was girded.
43_13_6: Then He came to Simon Peter. And Peter said to Him, “Lord, are You washing my feet?
43_13_7: Jesus answered and said to him, “What I am doing you do not understand now, but you will know after this.
43_13_8: Peter said to Him, “You shall never wash my feet!” Jesus answered him, “If I do not wash you, you have no part with Me.
43_13_9: Simon Peter said to Him, “Lord, not my feet only, but also my hands and my head!
43_13_10: Jesus said to him, “He who is bathed needs only to wash his feet, but is completely clean; and you are clean, but not all of you.
43_13_11: For He knew who would betray Him; therefore He said, “You are not all clean.
43_13_12: So when He had washed their feet, taken His garments, and sat down again, He said to them, “Do you know what I have done to you?
43_13_13: You call Me Teacher and Lord, and you say well, for so I am.
43_13_14: If I then, your Lord and Teacher, have washed your feet, you also ought to wash one another’s feet.
43_13_15: For I have given you an example, that you also should do just as I have done to you.
43_13_16: Truly, truly, I say to you, a servant is not greater than his master, nor is a messenger greater than the one who sent him.
43_13_17: If you know these things, you are blessed if you do them.
43_13_18: I am not speaking of all of you; I know whom I have chosen; but the Scripture must be fulfilled: The one who eats my bread has raised his heel against me.
43_13_19: Now I tell you before it comes, that when it does come to pass, you may believe that I am he.
43_13_20: Truly, truly, I say to you, whoever receives the one I send receives me, and whoever receives me receives the one who sent me.
43_13_21: When Jesus had said these things, he was troubled in spirit and testified, “Truly, truly, I say to you, one of you will betray me.”
43_13_22: The disciples started looking at one another — uncertain which one he was speaking about.
43_13_23: One of his disciples, the one Jesus loved, was reclining close beside Jesus.
43_13_24: So Simon Peter motioned to him to find out who it was he was talking about.
43_13_25: So he leaned back against Jesus and asked him, “Lord, who is it?”
43_13_26: Jesus replied, “He’s the one I give the piece of bread to after I have dipped it.” When he had dipped the bread, he gave it to Judas, Simon Iscariot’s son.
43_13_27: After Judas ate the piece of bread, Satan entered him. So Jesus told him, “What you’re doing, do quickly.”
43_13_28: Now no one at the table knew why he said this to him.
43_13_29: Since Judas kept the money-bag, some thought that Jesus was telling him, “Buy what we need for the festival,” or that he should give something to the poor.
43_13_30: After receiving the piece of bread, he immediately left. And it was night.
43_13_31: When he had left, Jesus said, “Now the Son of Man is glorified, and God is glorified in him.
43_13_32: If God is glorified in him, God will also glorify him in himself and will glorify him at once.
43_13_33: Little children, I am with you a little while longer. You will look for me, and just as I told the Jews, so now I tell you: ‘Where I am going, you cannot come.’
43_13_34: I give you a new command: Love one another. Just as I have loved you, you are also to love one another.
43_13_35: By this everyone will know that you are my disciples, if you love one another.
43_13_36: Simon Peter said to him, “Lord, where are you going?” Jesus answered him, “Where I am going you cannot follow me now, but you will follow later.”
43_13_37: Lord,” Peter asked, “why can’t I follow you now? I will lay down my life for you.”
43_13_38: Jesus replied, “Will you lay down your life for me? Truly I tell you, a rooster will not crow until you have denied me three times.
43_14_1: Don’t let your heart be troubled. Believe in God; believe also in me.
43_14_2: In my Father’s house are many rooms; if not, I would have told you. I am going away to prepare a place for you.
43_14_3: If I go away and prepare a place for you, I will come again and take you to myself, so that where I am you may be also.
43_14_4: You know the way to where I am going.
43_14_5: Lord,” Thomas said, “we don’t know where you’re going. How can we know the way?
43_14_6: Jesus told him, “I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.
43_14_7: If you know me, you will also know my Father. From now on you do know him and have seen him.
43_14_8: Lord,” said Philip, “show us the Father, and that’s enough for us.
43_14_9: Jesus said to him, “Have I been among you all this time and you do not know me, Philip? The one who has seen me has seen the Father. How can you say, ‘Show us the Father’?
43_14_10: Don’t you believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in me? The words I speak to you I do not speak on my own. The Father who lives in me does his works.
43_14_11: Believe me that I am in the Father and the Father is in me. Otherwise, believe because of the works themselves.
43_14_12: Truly I tell you, the one who believes in me will also do the works that I do. And he will do even greater works than these, because I am going to the Father.
43_14_13: Whatever you ask in my name, I will do it so that the Father may be glorified in the Son.
43_14_14: If you ask me anything in my name, I will do it.
43_14_15: If you love me, you will keep my commandments.
43_14_16: And I will ask the Father, and he will give you another Counselor to be with you forever.
43_14_17: the Spirit of truth, whom the world cannot receive, because it neither sees Him nor knows Him; but you know Him, for He remains with you and will be in you.
43_14_18: I will not leave you as orphans; I will come to you.
43_14_19: A little while longer and the world will see Me no more, but you will see Me. Because I live, you also will live.
43_14_20: In that day you will know that I am in My Father, and you are in Me, and I am in you.
43_14_21: He who has My commandments and keeps them, it is he who loves Me. And he who loves Me will be loved by My Father, and I will love him and reveal Myself to him.
43_14_22: Judas (not Iscariot) said to Him, “Lord, how is it that You will reveal Yourself to us, and not to the world?”
43_14_23: Jesus answered and said to him, “If anyone loves Me, he will keep My word; and My Father will love him, and We will come to him and make Our home with him.
43_14_24: He who does not love Me does not keep My words; and the word which you hear is not Mine but the Father’s who sent Me.
43_14_25: These things I have spoken to you while I am still with you.
43_14_26: But the Helper, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in My name, He will teach you all things and remind you of everything I have said to you.
43_14_27: Peace I leave with you, My peace I give to you; not as the world gives do I give to you. Let not your heart be troubled, neither let it be afraid.
43_14_28: You have heard Me say to you, ‘I am going away and coming back to you.’ If you loved Me, you would rejoice because I said, ‘I am going to the Father,’ for My Father is greater than I.
43_14_29: And now I have told you before it happens, so that when it happens, you may believe.
43_14_30: I will no longer talk much with you, for the ruler of this world is coming, and he has no claim on Me.
43_14_31: But so that the world may know that I love the Father, I do exactly as the Father commanded Me. Get up, let us go from here.
43_15_1: I am the true vine, and My Father is the vinedresser.
43_15_2: Every branch in Me that does not bear fruit He takes away; and every branch that bears fruit He prunes, that it may bear more fruit.
43_15_3: You are already clean because of the word which I have spoken to you.
43_15_4: Abide in Me, and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit of itself, unless it abides in the vine, neither can you, unless you abide in Me.
43_15_5: I am the vine; you are the branches. He who abides in Me, and I in him, bears much fruit; for without Me you can do nothing.
43_15_6: If anyone does not abide in Me, he is cast out as a branch and withers; and they gather them and throw them into the fire, and they are burned.
43_15_7: If you abide in Me, and My words abide in you, you will ask what you desire, and it shall be done for you.
43_15_8: By this My Father is glorified, that you bear much fruit; so you will be My disciples.
43_15_9: As the Father has loved Me, so have I loved you; abide in My love.
43_15_10: If you keep My commandments, you will abide in My love, just as I have kept My Father’s commandments and abide in His love.
43_15_11: These things I have spoken to you, that My joy may be in you, and that your joy may be full.
43_15_12: This is My commandment, that you love one another as I have loved you.
43_15_13: Greater love has no one than this, than to lay down one’s life for his friends.
43_15_14: You are My friends if you do whatever I command you.
43_15_15: No longer do I call you servants, for a servant does not know what his master is doing; but I have called you friends, for all that I have heard from My Father I have made known to you.
43_15_16: You did not choose Me, but I chose you and appointed you that you should go and bear fruit, and that your fruit should remain, that whatever you ask the Father in My name He may give you.
43_15_17: These things I command you, that you love one another.
43_15_18: If the world hates you, know that it has hated Me before it hated you.
43_15_19: If you were of the world, the world would love its own; yet because you are not of the world, but I chose you out of the world, therefore the world hates you.
43_15_20: Remember the word that I said to you, ‘A servant is not greater than his master.’ If they persecuted Me, they will also persecute you. If they kept My word, they will keep yours also.
43_15_21: But all these things they will do to you on account of My name, because they do not know Him who sent Me.
43_15_22: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not have been guilty of sin, but now they have no excuse for their sin.
43_15_23: He who hates Me hates My Father also.
43_15_24: If I had not done among them the works which no one else did, they would not have sin; but now they have seen and hated both Me and My Father.
43_15_25: But this happened so that the statement written in their law might be fulfilled: ‘They hated Me without a cause.’
43_15_26: But when the Helper comes, whom I will send to you from the Father, the Spirit of truth who proceeds from the Father, he will testify about me.
43_15_27: And you also will bear witness, because you have been with me from the beginning.
43_16_1: These things I have spoken to you so that you may be kept from stumbling.
43_16_2: They will put you out of the synagogues; indeed, the hour is coming when whoever kills you will think he is offering service to God.
43_16_3: And they will do these things because they have not known the Father or me.
43_16_4: But I have told you these things so that when their hour comes you will remember I told them to you; I did not say these things from the beginning, because I was with you.
43_16_5: But now I am going to him who sent me, and none of you asks me, 'Where are you going?'
43_16_6: But because I have said these things to you, sorrow has filled your heart.
43_16_7: Nevertheless, I am telling you the truth: it is for your benefit that I go away, because if I don’t go away, the Counselor will not come to you; if I go, I will send him to you.
43_16_8: And when he comes, he will convict the world concerning sin and righteousness and judgment:
43_16_9: about sin, because they do not believe in me;
43_16_10: about righteousness, because I am going to the Father and you will no longer see me;
43_16_11: and about judgment, because the ruler of this world has been judged.
43_16_12: I still have many things to tell you, but you can’t bear them now.
43_16_13: When the Spirit of truth comes, he will guide you into all the truth; for he will not speak on his own authority, but whatever he hears he will speak, and he will declare to you the things that are to come.
43_16_14: He will glorify me, because he will take from what is mine and declare it to you.
43_16_15: Everything the Father has is mine; this is why I told you that he takes from what is mine and will declare it to you.
43_16_16: A little while and you will no longer see me; again a little while and you will see me.
43_16_17: Then some of his disciples said to one another, 'What is this he’s telling us: ‘A little while and you will not see me; again a little while and you will see me’ and, ‘because I am going to the Father’?
43_16_18: They said, 'What is this he is saying, ‘A little while’? We don’t know what he’s talking about.'
43_16_19: Now Jesus knew that they wanted to ask him, and so he said to them, 'Are you asking one another about what I said, ‘A little while and you will not see me; again a little while and you will see me’?
43_16_20: Truly I tell you, you will weep and mourn, but the world will rejoice; you will become sorrowful, but your sorrow will turn to joy.
43_16_21: When a woman is in labor, she has pain because her time has come; but when she has given birth to a child, she no longer remembers the suffering because of the joy that a person has been born into the world.
43_16_22: So you also have sorrow now. But I will see you again. Your hearts will rejoice, and no one will take away your joy from you.
43_16_23: In that day you will not ask me anything; truly I tell you, anything you ask the Father in my name, he will give you.
43_16_24: Until now you have asked for nothing in my name; ask and you will receive, so that your joy may be complete.
43_16_25: I have spoken these things to you in figures of speech; a time is coming when I will no longer speak to you in figures, but I will tell you plainly about the Father.
43_16_26: On that day you will ask in my name, and I am not telling you that I will ask the Father on your behalf.
43_16_27: For the Father himself loves you, because you have loved me and have believed that I came from God.
43_16_28: I came from the Father and have come into the world; again, I am leaving the world and going to the Father.
43_16_29: His disciples said, 'Look, now you’re speaking plainly and not using any figurative language.'
43_16_30: Now we know that you know everything and don’t need anyone to question you; by this we believe that you came from God.
43_16_31: Jesus responded to them, 'Do you now believe?'
43_16_32: Indeed, an hour is coming, and has come, when each of you will be scattered to his own home, and you will leave me alone; yet I am not alone, because the Father is with me.
43_16_33: I have told you these things so that in me you may have peace; in the world you will have tribulation; but take heart, I have overcome the world.
43_17_1: Jesus spoke these things, looked up to heaven, and said: 'Father, the hour has come; glorify your Son so that the Son may glorify you,
43_17_2: since you have given him authority over all flesh, to give eternal life to all whom you have given him.
43_17_3: And this is eternal life: that they may know you, the only true God, and the one you have sent — Jesus Christ.
43_17_4: I have glorified you on the earth by completing the work you gave me to do.
43_17_5: Now, Father, glorify me in your presence with that glory I had with you before the world existed.
43_17_6: I have manifested Your name to the people whom You have given Me out of the world. They were Yours, You gave them to Me, and they have kept Your word.
43_17_7: Now they have come to know that everything You have given Me is from You.
43_17_8: For I have given them the words that You gave Me; and they have received them and have come to know for certain that I came forth from You; and they have believed that You sent Me.
43_17_9: I pray for them. I am not praying for the world but for those whom You have given Me, for they are Yours.
43_17_10: And all Mine are Yours, and Yours are Mine, and I am glorified in them.
43_17_11: Now I am no longer in the world, but these are in the world, and I am coming to You. Holy Father, keep them in Your name, which You have given Me, that they may be one as We are one.
43_17_12: While I was with them, I kept them in Your name, which You have given Me. I have guarded them, and not one of them is lost except the son of destruction, that the Scripture might be fulfilled.
43_17_13: But now I come to You, and I speak these things in the world, that they may have My joy fulfilled in themselves.
43_17_14: I have given them Your word, and the world has hated them because they are not of the world, just as I am not of the world.
43_17_15: I do not ask that You take them out of the world, but that You keep them safe from the evil one.
43_17_16: They are not of the world, just as I am not of the world.
43_17_17: Sanctify them by Your truth; Your word is truth.
43_17_18: As You sent Me into the world, I also have sent them into the world.
43_17_19: And for their sake I sanctify Myself, that they also may be sanctified by the truth.
43_17_20: I do not pray for these alone, but also for those who will believe in Me through their word.
43_17_21: that they all may be one, just as You, Father, are in Me, and I in You; that they also may be one in Us, so that the world may believe that You sent Me.
43_17_22: And the glory which You gave Me I have given to them, that they may be one just as We are one.
43_17_23: I in them, and You in Me; that they may be made perfect in one, and that the world may know that You have sent Me and have loved them even as You have loved Me.
43_17_24: Father, I desire that they also whom You gave Me may be with Me where I am, that they may behold My glory which You have given Me; for You loved Me before the foundation of the world.
43_17_25: O righteous Father! The world has not known You, but I have known You; and these have known that You sent Me.
43_17_26: And I have declared to them Your name, and will continue to make it known, that the love with which You loved Me may be in them, and I in them.
43_18_1: When Jesus had spoken these words, He went out with His disciples across the brook Kidron, where there was a garden, which He and His disciples entered.
43_18_2: And Judas, who betrayed Him, also knew the place, for Jesus often met there with His disciples.
43_18_3: Then Judas, having received a detachment of troops and officers from the chief priests and Pharisees, came there with lanterns, torches, and weapons.
43_18_4: Jesus therefore, knowing all things that would come upon Him, went forward and said to them, “Whom are you seeking?”
43_18_5: They answered Him, “Jesus of Nazareth.” Jesus said to them, “I am He.” And Judas, who betrayed Him, also stood with them.
43_18_6: Now when He said to them, “I am He,” they drew back and fell to the ground.
43_18_7: Then He asked them again, “Whom are you seeking?” And they said, “Jesus of Nazareth.”
43_18_8: Jesus answered, “I told you that I am He. Therefore, if you seek Me, let these go their way,”
43_18_9: that the saying might be fulfilled which He spoke, “Of those whom You gave Me I have lost none.”
43_18_10: Then Simon Peter, having a sword, drew it and struck the high priest’s servant, and cut off his right ear. The servant’s name was Malchus.
43_18_11: So Jesus said to Peter, “Put your sword into the sheath. Shall I not drink the cup which My Father has given Me?”
43_18_12: Then the detachment of troops and the captain and the officers of the Jews arrested Jesus and bound Him.
43_18_13: And they led Him away to Annas first, for he was the father-in-law of Caiaphas who was high priest that year.
43_18_14: Now it was Caiaphas who advised the Jews that it was expedient that one man should die for the people.
43_18_15: And Simon Peter followed Jesus, and so did another disciple. Now that disciple was known to the high priest and went with Jesus into the courtyard of the high priest.
43_18_16: But Peter stood at the door outside. Then the other disciple, who was known to the high priest, went out and spoke to her who kept the door, and brought Peter in.
43_18_17: Then the servant girl who kept the door said to Peter, “You are not also one of this Man’s disciples, are you?” He said, “I am not.”
43_18_18: Now the servants and officers who had made a fire of coals stood there, for it was cold, and they warmed themselves. And Peter stood with them and warmed himself.
43_18_19: The high priest then asked Jesus about His disciples and His teaching.
43_18_20: Jesus answered him, “I spoke openly to the world. I always taught in synagogues and in the temple, where all the Jews come together, and in secret I have said nothing.
43_18_21: Why do you ask me? Ask those who have heard me what I said to them; they know what I said.”
43_18_22: And when He had said these things, one of the officers standing by struck Jesus with his hand, saying, “Is that how you answer the high priest?”
43_18_23: Jesus answered him, “If what I said is wrong, bear witness about the wrong; but if what I said is right, why do you strike me?”
43_18_24: Then Annas sent Him bound to Caiaphas the high priest.
43_18_25: Now Simon Peter was standing and warming himself. Therefore they said to him, “You also are not one of his disciples, are you?” He denied it and said, “I am not!”
43_18_26: One of the high priest’s servants, a relative of the man whose ear Peter had cut off, said, “Didn’t I see you with him in the garden?”
43_18_27: Peter denied it again. Immediately a rooster crowed.
43_18_28: Then they led Jesus from Caiaphas to the governor’s headquarters. It was early morning. They did not enter the headquarters themselves; otherwise they would be defiled and unable to eat the Passover.
43_18_29: Pilate then went out to them and said, “What accusation do you bring against this man?”
43_18_30: They answered him, “If this man were not doing evil, we would not have delivered him over to you.”
43_18_31: Then Pilate said to them, “You take him and judge him according to your law.” Therefore the Jews said to him, “It is not lawful for us to put anyone to death,”
43_18_32: that the saying of Jesus might be fulfilled which He spoke, signifying by what death He would die.
43_18_33: Then Pilate entered the Praetorium again, called Jesus, and said to Him, “Are You the King of the Jews?”
43_18_34: Jesus answered him, “Are you speaking for yourself about this, or did others tell you this concerning Me?”
43_18_35: Pilate answered, “Am I a Jew? Your own nation and the chief priests have delivered You to me. What have You done?”
43_18_36: Jesus answered, “My kingdom is not of this world. If My kingdom were of this world, My servants would fight, so that I should not be delivered to the Jews; but now My kingdom is not from here.”
43_18_37: Pilate therefore said to Him, “Are You a king then?” Jesus answered, “You say rightly that I am a king. For this cause I was born, and for this cause I have come into the world, that I should bear witness to the truth. Everyone who is of the truth hears My voice.”
43_18_38: Pilate said to Him, “What is truth?” And when he had said this, he went out again to the Jews, and said to them, “I find no fault in Him at all.
43_18_39: But you have a custom that I should release someone to you at the Passover. Do you therefore want me to release to you the King of the Jews?”
43_18_40: Then they all cried again, saying, “Not this Man, but Barabbas!” Now Barabbas was a robber.
43_19_1: So then Pilate took Jesus and scourged Him.
43_19_2: And the soldiers twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on His head, and they put on Him a purple robe.
43_19_3: Then they said, “Hail, King of the Jews!” And they struck Him with their hands.
43_19_4: Pilate then went out again, and said to them, “Behold, I am bringing Him out to you, that you may know that I find no fault in Him.”
43_19_5: Then Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe. And Pilate said to them, “Behold the Man!”
43_19_6: Therefore, when the chief priests and officers saw Him, they cried out, saying, “Crucify Him, crucify Him!” Pilate said to them, “You take Him and crucify Him, for I find no fault in Him.”
43_19_7: The Jews answered him, “We have a law, and according to our law He ought to die, because He made Himself the Son of God.”
43_19_8: Therefore, when Pilate heard that saying, he was the more afraid,
43_19_9: and went again into the Praetorium, and said to Jesus, “Where are You from?” But Jesus gave him no answer.
43_19_10: Then Pilate said to Him, “Are You not speaking to me? Do You not know that I have power to crucify You, and power to release You?”
43_19_11: Jesus answered, “You could have no power at all against Me unless it had been given you from above. Therefore the one who delivered Me to you has the greater sin.”
43_19_12: From then on Pilate sought to release Him, but the Jews cried out, saying, “If you let this Man go, you are not Caesar’s friend. Whoever makes himself a king speaks against Caesar.”
43_19_13: When Pilate therefore heard that saying, he brought Jesus out and sat down in the judgment seat in a place that is called The Pavement, but in Hebrew, Gabbatha.
43_19_14: Now it was the Preparation Day of the Passover, and about the sixth hour. And he said to the Jews, “Behold your King!”
43_19_15: But they cried out, “Away with Him, away with Him! Crucify Him!” Pilate said to them, “Shall I crucify your King?” The chief priests answered, “We have no king but Caesar!”
43_19_16: Then he delivered Him to them to be crucified. So they took Jesus and led Him away.
43_19_17: And He, bearing His cross, went out to a place called the Place of a Skull, which is called in Hebrew, Golgotha,
43_19_18: where they crucified Him, and two others with Him, one on either side, and Jesus in the center.
43_19_19: Now Pilate wrote a title and put it on the cross. And the writing was: JESUS OF NAZARETH, THE KING OF THE JEWS.
43_19_20: Then many of the Jews read this inscription, for the place where Jesus was crucified was near the city, and it was written in Hebrew, Latin, and Greek.
43_19_21: So the chief priests of the Jews said to Pilate, “Do not write, ‘The King of the Jews,’ but rather, ‘This man said, I am King of the Jews.’”
43_19_22: Pilate answered, “What I have written, I have written.”
43_19_23: When the soldiers had crucified Jesus, they took his garments and divided them into four parts, one part for each soldier; also his tunic, which was seamless, woven in one piece from top to bottom.
43_19_24: So they said to one another, “Let us not tear it, but cast lots for it, to see whose it shall be.” This was to fulfill the Scripture which says, “They divided my garments among them, and for my clothing they cast lots.” So the soldiers did these things.
43_19_25: Standing by the cross of Jesus were his mother, his mother’s sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary Magdalene.
43_19_26: When Jesus saw his mother and the disciple whom he loved standing nearby, he said to his mother, “Woman, behold, your son!”
43_19_27: Then he said to the disciple, “Behold your mother!” And from that hour the disciple took her into his own home.
43_19_28: After this, Jesus, knowing that all was now finished, said, “I thirst.”
43_19_29: A jar full of sour wine was sitting there; so they fixed a sponge full of sour wine on a hyssop branch and held it up to his mouth.
43_19_30: When Jesus had received the sour wine, he said, “It is finished!” And bowing his head, he gave up his spirit.
43_19_31: Since it was the day of Preparation, the Jews asked Pilate that their legs might be broken and that they might be taken away.
43_19_32: So the soldiers came and broke the legs of the first man and of the other one who had been crucified with him.
43_19_33: But when they came to Jesus and saw that he was already dead, they did not break his legs.
43_19_34: But one of the soldiers pierced his side with a spear, and at once blood and water came out.
43_19_35: He who saw this has testified so that you also may believe. His testimony is true, and he knows that he is telling the truth.
43_19_36: For these things happened so that the Scripture would be fulfilled: Not one of his bones will be broken.
43_19_37: And again another Scripture says, “They will look at the one they pierced.”
43_19_38: After this, Joseph of Arimathea, who was a disciple of Jesus but secretly because of his fear of the Jews, asked Pilate that he might remove Jesus’s body, and Pilate gave him permission; so he came and took his body away.
43_19_39: Nicodemus, who had previously come to him at night, also came, bringing a mixture of about seventy-five pounds of myrrh and aloes.
43_19_40: They took Jesus’s body and wrapped it in linen cloths with the fragrant spices, according to the burial custom of the Jews.
43_19_41: Now in the place where he was crucified there was a garden, and in the garden a new tomb in which no one had yet been laid.
43_19_42: They placed Jesus there because of the Jewish day of preparation and since the tomb was nearby.
43_20_1: Now on the first day of the week Mary Magdalene came to the tomb early, while it was still dark, and saw that the stone had been taken away from the tomb.
43_20_2: So she ran and went to Simon Peter and the other disciple, the one whom Jesus loved, and said to them, “They have taken the Lord out of the tomb, and we do not know where they have laid him.”
43_20_3: At that, Peter and the other disciple went out, heading for the tomb.
43_20_4: The two were running together, but the other disciple outran Peter and got to the tomb first.
43_20_5: And stooping down, he saw the linen cloths lying there, but he did not go in.
43_20_6: Then, following him, Simon Peter also came. He entered the tomb and saw the linen cloths lying there.
43_20_7: And the face cloth, which had been on Jesus' head, was not lying with the linen cloths but was folded up in a separate place by itself.
43_20_8: The other disciple, who had reached the tomb first, then also went in, saw, and believed.
43_20_9: For they did not yet understand the Scripture that he must rise from the dead.
43_20_10: Then the disciples went back to their homes.
43_20_11: But Mary stood outside the tomb, crying. As she was crying, she stooped to look into the tomb.
43_20_12: She saw two angels in white sitting where Jesus’s body had been lying, one at the head and the other at the feet.
43_20_13: They said to her, “Woman, why are you crying?” “Because they’ve taken away my Lord,” she told them, “and I don’t know where they’ve put him.”
43_20_14: Having said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing there, but she did not know it was Jesus.
43_20_15: Jesus said to her, “Woman, why are you crying? Who is it that you’re seeking?” Supposing he was the gardener, she replied, “Sir, if you’ve carried him away, tell me where you’ve put him, and I will take him away.”
43_20_16: Jesus said to her, “Mary.” Turning around, she said to him in Aramaic, “Rabboni!” — which means “Teacher.”
43_20_17: Jesus said to her, “Do not cling to me, for I have not yet ascended to the Father; but go to my brothers and say to them, ‘I am ascending to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.’”
43_20_18: Mary Magdalene came and announced to the disciples, “I have seen the Lord!” and that He had spoken these things to her.
43_20_19: Then, on the evening of that first day of the week, when the doors were shut where the disciples were assembled for fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood among them and said, “Peace be with you.”
43_20_20: When He had said this, He showed them His hands and His side; then the disciples rejoiced when they saw the Lord.
43_20_21: So Jesus said to them again, “Peace to you! As the Father has sent Me, I also send you.”
43_20_22: And when He had said this, He breathed on them and said to them, “Receive the Holy Spirit.”
43_20_23: If you forgive the sins of any, they are forgiven them; if you do not forgive them, they are not forgiven.”
43_20_24: Now Thomas, called the Twin, one of the twelve, was not with them when Jesus came.
43_20_25: The other disciples therefore said to him, “We have seen the Lord!” But he said to them, “Unless I see in His hands the mark of the nails and put my finger into the mark of the nails and place my hand into His side, I will never believe.”
43_20_26: And after eight days His disciples were again inside, and Thomas with them; Jesus came, the doors being shut, and stood in their midst and said, “Peace to you!”
43_20_27: Then He said to Thomas, “Reach your finger here, and look at My hands; and reach your hand here, and put it into My side. Do not disbelieve, but believe.”
43_20_28: And Thomas answered and said to Him, “My Lord and my God!”
43_20_29: Jesus said to him, “Thomas, because you have seen Me, you have believed; blessed are those who have not seen and yet have believed.”
43_20_30: And truly Jesus did many other signs in the presence of His disciples, which are not written in this book;
43_20_31: but these are written so that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and that by believing you may have life in His name.
43_21_1: After these things Jesus revealed Himself again to the disciples by the Sea of Tiberias, and in this way He showed Himself:
43_21_2: Simon Peter, Thomas (called the Twin), Nathanael of Cana in Galilee, the sons of Zebedee, and two others of His disciples were together.
43_21_3: Simon Peter said to them, “I am going fishing.” They said to him, “We will go with you.” They went out and immediately got into the boat, but that night they caught nothing.
43_21_4: But when daybreak came, Jesus stood on the shore; yet the disciples did not know that it was Jesus.
43_21_5: Then Jesus said to them, “Children, do you have any fish?” They answered Him, “No.”
43_21_6: And He said to them, “Cast the net on the right side of the boat, and you will find some.” So they cast it, and now they were not able to haul it in because of the large number of fish.
43_21_7: Therefore that disciple whom Jesus loved said to Peter, “It is the Lord!” Now when Simon Peter heard that it was the Lord, he put on his outer garment (for he had removed it) and plunged into the sea.
43_21_8: But the other disciples came in the little boat, dragging the net full of fish, for they were not far from land, but about a hundred yards off.
43_21_9: Then, as soon as they had come to land, they saw a charcoal fire there, with fish laid out on it, and bread.
43_21_10: Jesus said to them, “Bring some of the fish that you have just caught.”
43_21_11: So Simon Peter went up and dragged the net to land, full of large fish, one hundred and fifty-three; and although there were so many, the net was not torn.
43_21_12: Jesus said to them, “Come and have breakfast.” Yet none of the disciples dared ask Him, “Who are You?” knowing that it was the Lord.
43_21_13: Jesus then came and took the bread and gave it to them, and likewise the fish.
43_21_14: This was now the third time Jesus appeared to the disciples after He was raised from the dead.
43_21_15: So when they had eaten breakfast, Jesus said to Simon Peter, “Simon, son of John, do you love Me more than these?” He said to Him, “Yes, Lord; You know that I love You.” He said to him, “Feed My lambs.”
43_21_16: He said to him again a second time, “Simon, son of John, do you love Me?” He said to Him, “Yes, Lord; You know that I love You.” He said to him, “Tend My sheep.”
43_21_17: He said to him the third time, “Simon, son of John, do you love Me?” Peter was grieved because He said to him the third time, “Do you love Me?” And he said to Him, “Lord, You know all things; You know that I love You.” Jesus said to him, “Feed My sheep.”
43_21_18: Most assuredly, I say to you, when you were younger, you girded yourself and walked where you wished; but when you are old, you will stretch out your hands, and another will gird you and carry you where you do not wish.”
43_21_19: This He spoke, signifying by what kind of death he would glorify God. And when He had spoken this, He said to him, “Follow Me.”
43_21_20: Then Peter, turning around, saw the disciple whom Jesus loved following, who also had leaned on His breast at the supper, and said, “Lord, who is the one who betrays You?”
43_21_21: Peter, seeing him, said to Jesus, “But Lord, what about this man?”
43_21_22: Jesus said to him, “If I will that he remain until I come, what is that to you? You follow Me.”
43_21_23: Then this saying went out among the brethren that this disciple would not die; yet Jesus did not say to him that he would not die, but, “If I will that he remain until I come, what is that to you?”
43_21_24: This is the disciple who testifies of these things and who wrote these things; and we know that his testimony is true.
43_21_25: And there are also many other things that Jesus did, which if every one of them were written down, I suppose not even the world itself could contain the books that would be written.
